gmw_00001120.name: 3C 66A
gmw_00001120.overview: 3C 66A is an intermediate-peaked BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1705.00124] with an uncertain spectroscopic redshift [arXiv:1711.00325]. The active galaxy displays strong multiwavelength variability [arXiv:1109.6000], featuring day-scale gamma-ray fluctuations [arXiv:0912.5355] and high optical polarization [arXiv:astro-ph/0107017].

sources.summary: The paper observes BL Lac object 3C 66A with STACEE, reports no very-high-energy detection, and gives upper limits while discussing its classification and EGRET association.

quotes.quote: At this point a signal, manifest as a difference between ON and OFF count rates, is sought. 3C 66A The Third Cambridge (3C) radio survey at 159 MHz resulted in a catalog of 471 sources in the northern hemisphere. It was later found that the source numbered 66 was composed of two unrelated objects, a BL Lac object now identified as 3C 66A and a radio galaxy, 3C 66B. The BL Lac classification has been supported by optical and X-ray observations. 3C 66A is coincident with the EGRET source 3EG 0222+4253 , but there are other objects in the EGRET error box, including the pulsar J0218+4232. It has been suggested by Kuiper et al that the pulsar contributes to the observed gamma-ray flux at energies less than 500 MeV while the BL Lac object dominates at higher energies. No confirmed detections of this source have been made at very high energies.
STACEE observed 3C 66A from September to December, 2003 as part of a multi-wavelength campaign summarized in Boettcher et al . We acquired a data set of 87 ON/OFF pairs.

Stated differently, we can say that the 3C 66A integral gamma-ray flux above 185 GeV is less than 15 of that of the Crab Nebula, using results from STACEE measurements of the Crab over the same energy range ( ).

sources.summary: The paper constrains 3C 66A as a BL Lac with an approximately 25-day Fermi/LAT characteristic time scale and PSD slopes consistent with earlier analyses.

quotes.quote: For the first time we constrain characteristic ray time scale of variability in two BL Lac sources, 3C 66A and PKS 2155-304 ( _ 0 25 day and _ 0 43 day, respectively, in the observer's frame), which are longer than the soft X-ray time scales detected in blazars and Seyfert galaxies. The comparison of DIC computed for the two models (Table tab:sample ) indicates that the sup-OU model is preferred over the OU model in all sources except 3C 454.3, 3C 66A, and PKS 2155-304 in which DIC = DIC _ supOU DIC _ OU < 10 . The mixed OU process was prefered in 10 blazars from our sample, while in the 3 remaining blazars (two BL Lacs, 3C 66A and PKS 2155-304; and one FSRQ 3C 454.3) the single OU process and the mixed OU process resulted in fits of equally good quality. The posterior probability distributions of the characteristic time scales from the OU process allowed us to constrain the Fermi/LAT characteristic time scales in two BL Lac sources ( _ 0 / day = 1.39^ +0.43 _ 0.29 in 3C 66A, and _ 0 / day = 1.63^ +2.79 _ 0.35 in PKS 2155-304).

Characteristic time scales found through the OU modeling in 3C 66A and PKS 2155-304 fall outside of the frequency range studied by NM13. We found that in PKS 0454-234, 3C 279, 3C 66A, and PKS 2155-304 the two methods are consistent with each other in terms of the derived PSD slopes within the provided error bars. However, the OU ray characteristic time scales detected in PKS 2155-304 and 3C 66A are much longer than those of the BL Lac sources (and also FSRQs) in the soft X-rays. We constrained _ 0 in two BL Lac type blazars, 3C 66A ( _ 0 25 day, or 17 day in the rest frame) and PKS 2155-304 ( _ 0 43 day, or 38 day in the rest frame).

sources.summary: The paper reviews uncertain redshift claims for 3C 66A, reports a high-SNR featureless GTC spectrum, and cannot confirm prior Mg II-based redshift evidence.

quotes.quote: 3C 66A : wills1974 identified this strong radio source as a BLL because of its featureless optical spectrum. miller1978 proposed a redshift of z = 0.444, on the basis on one emission line attributed to Mg II 2800 . A value considered by the authors as tentative and highly uncertain. More recently finke2008 showed an optical spectrum in the range from 4200 to 8500 with no detectable optical features. Our high SNR ( 200) GTC spectrum is also featureless.

We are not able to confirm the Mg II emission proposed by miller1978 because it is out of our spectral range. However at z = 0.444 we would expect to observe H _ emission line at 7020 , where we do not detect any line with EW > 0.2 . We conclude that the redshift of this source is still unknown.

sources.summary: The paper monitors blazar 3C 66A in optical bands, classifying it as an intermediate BL Lac and measuring short- and long-term variability timescales and amplitudes.

quotes.quote: We present results based on the observations of the blazar 3C 66A from 2005 November 06 to 2016 February 14 in the BVR and I broadbands using 1.2m telescope of the Mt. Abu InfraRed Observatory (MIRO). The source was observed on 160 nights out of which on 89 nights it was monitored for more than 1 hr to check for the presence of any intra-day variability (IDV). The blazar 3C 66A exhibited significant variations in the optical flux on short and long term time scales.

Our statistical studies suggest the IDV time scales ranging from 37 min to about 3.12 hours and, in one case, a possibility of the quasi-periodic variations with characteristic timescale of 1.4 hrs. The IDV amplitudes in R band were found to vary from 0.03 mag to as much as 0.6 mag, with larger amplitude of variation when the source was relatively fainter. The typical rate of the flux variation was estimated to be 0.07 mag hr ^ 1 in both, the rising and the falling phases.

3C 66A was first identified by Wills1974 as a blue stellar object having a featureless optical spectrum with (U-B) and (B-V) colors similar to those found for the quasi-stellar objects. It is classified as an intermediate BL Lac (IBL) as its synchrotron emission component usually peaks between 10^ 14 Hz and 10^ 15 Hz Perri2003, Abdo2010b .

sources.summary: The paper monitors 3C 66A as a luminous TeV BL Lac object, finding a high optical state, weak color correlation, and intrinsic non-thermal variability.

quotes.quote: 3C 66A 3C 66A is one of the most luminous blazars at TeV rays (Very High Energy (VHE) , E > 100 GeV). It was detected at VHE rays by VERITAS (Acciari et al. \ 2009). It is classified as a BL Lac object (Maccagni et al. \ 1987). Recently, it has been proposed that this blazar is hosted in a galaxy belonging to a cluster at z=0.340 (Torres-Zafra et al. \ 2016). The synchrotron peak of 3C 66A is located between 10^ 15 and 10^ 16 Hz and hence it is best classified as an intermediate frequency peaked BL Lac object (Perri et al. \ 2003; Fan et al. \ 2016).

During our observing campaign, the average R band magnitude is 14.4 with a maximum of 14.07 magnitude on JD=7200 and a minimum of 14.72 magnitude on JD=7340. Hence, we can say that the source is mostly in a relatively high state during our observing campaign. LCs in other bands also shows similar behaviour.

sources.summary: The paper models 3C 66A as an intermediate-frequency BL Lac in a galaxy cluster, preferring MAGIC low-energy data because VERITAS caught it flaring, and adopting SSC+EC parameters.

quotes.quote: The intermediate-frequency peaked BL Lac object 3C 66A has been observed by several telescopes, e.g. VERITAS and MAGIC . The blazar is located within a galaxy cluster at z = 0.34 . Using this redshift, observations by MAGIC extend up to an optical depth of 1.5 ( E 0.4 TeV) and the observations by VERITAS up to 1.72 ( E 0.45 TeV) . The HAWC observation at 7 TeV is at an optical depth of 9.3 . The observations by VERITAS were performed when the blazar was in a flaring state.

Fermi-LAT observations in the GeV range detect this source at a significance of 170 with a high variability index of 1170. The maximum annual flux of this source (reached in early 2009) was 1.7 brighter than the average source flux. The SED of the source was modelled by Ref. .

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 66A as a QSO/BL Lac object observed by VERITAS, reporting a strong excess, an October 2008 flare, and multiwavelength variability.

quotes.quote: 3C 66A 3C 66A was first identified as a quasi-stellar object (QSO) using optical observations . It was subsequently classified as a BL Lac object based on its significant optical and X-ray variability .

VERITAS observed 3C 66A for 14 hours from September 2007 through January 2008 (hereafter, the 2007-2008 season). Later, from September through November 2008 (hereafter, the 2008-2009 season), a further 46 hours of data were taken. After removing data with poor weather conditions or hardware-related problems, 4.7 hours live time in 2007-2008 and 28.1 hours in 2008-2009 were used for final analysis. Analysis of the data resulted in an excess of 1791 events from the direction of 3C 66A. The excess corresponds to a statistical significance of 21.1 .

Figure 3c66a_flare_mwl shows the multiwavelength light curve of 3C 66A centred around the flare (October 1 to October 10). The light curves obtained show strong variability at every observed wavelength and in particular the flux increase seen by VERITAS and Fermi LAT coincides with an optical outburst.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 66A as a VHE BL Lac blazar with uncertain redshift and reports MAGIC detection during an optical high state, compatible with VERITAS.

quotes.quote: 3C 66A is a very high energy (VHE) gamma-ray blazar classified as a BL Lac object , or an intermediate-frequency peaked BL Lac object (IBL, ). It has an uncertain redshift reported to be 0.444 or 0.321 , estimated from a single spectral line, or a marginally resolved host galaxy respectively. Besides that, the existing upper and lower limits to the redshift are >0.096 , <0.44 and <0.58 .

MAGIC detected a VHE gamma-ray signal from 3C 66A in the period between December 2009 and January 2010, and during an optical active state of 3C 66A . We rule out the emission to come from 3C 66B at a confidence level of 3.6 .

The energy spectrum we unfolded is softer than in the previous MAGIC detection ( = 3.10 0.31_ stat 0.2_ sys ), and at the same time compatible with the VERITAS spectrum of 3C 66A. Compared to VERITAS, MAGIC has a lower threshold and the spectrum is extending to well below 100 GeV .

sources.summary: The paper studies 3C 66A as an intermediate-synchrotron-peaked BL Lac blazar, reporting possible optical quasi-periodicity near three years but no gamma-ray periodicity.

quotes.quote: We find evidences of possible quasi-periodic variability in the optical photometric data of both sources with periods of 3 years for 3C 66A and 1.9 years for B2 1633+38, with significances between 3 and 5 . Only B2 1633+38 shows evidence of this behaviour in the optical polarized data set at a confidence level of 2 4 . This is the first reported evidence of quasi-periodic behaviour in the optical light curve of B2 1633+38. Also a hint of quasi-periodic behaviour is found in the ray light curve of B2 1633+38 with a confidence level 2 , while no periodicity is observed for 3C 66A in this energy range.

3C 66A is a BL Lac type object classified as an intermediate synchrotron peaked blazar, according to its SED ackermann2015 . The redshift of this object is commonly referenced as z=0.444 miller1978 . Nevertheless, this value has been strongly questioned in several works finke2008, furniss2013, Paiano17, torres-zafra2018 , since its spectrum does not show any emission line. furniss2013 and torres-zafra2018 estimated a lower limit of the redshift of z 0.33. 3C 66A has been the target of multiple monitoring campaigns and surveys, showing variability throughout the electromagnetic spectrum on multiple timescales.

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C66A as a 1.7 Gpc blazar whose VHE observations and possible cosmic-ray flare may be affected by focusing from nearby 3C66B.

quotes.quote: The pair 3C66A and 3C66B provides an interesting case. 3C66A is believed to be a blazar at distance 1.7 Gpc. The radio galaxy 3C66B appears to be only 6^ away from the line of sight, at distance of 88 Mpc. VERITAS  2009ApJ...693L.104A has reported observations of VHE photons from 3C66A but not 3C66B, while MAGIC has apparently observed photons from 3C66B  2009ApJ...692L..29A .

It is possible that the magnetic fields of the radio galaxy close to the line of sight could bend (and focus) the trajectories of the high-energy protons produced by blazar 3C66A. Therefore, it is possible that MAGIC has observed a flare that originated in 3C66A as a burst of cosmic rays, which were focused by the radio galaxy, and which produced secondary photons pointing back to 3C66B, rather than 3C66A.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 66A as a z=0.444 BL Lac with reported GeV-to-TeV detections whose high-energy photons challenge conventional propagation models.

quotes.quote: On the one hand, the VERITAS Collaboration recently claimed a detection of gamma-rays above 0.1 TeV (the highest detected energies are not yet reported) coming from 3C 66A , an intermediate-frequency-peaked BL Lac object located at redshift 0.444. This claim coincides with the detection, at GeV energies, of this source in active state by the Fermi-LAT instrument during the same time window . In addition, the MAGIC Collaboration reported gamma-rays above 1 TeV coming from a location consistent with the position of 3C 66A . Those observations would confirm the earlier claims from the Crimean Astrophysical Observatory's GT-48 IACT of several detections from this source above 0.9 TeV . Those detections were not confirmed by the HEGRA and Whipple telescopes, which are more sensitive instruments, but which observed the source at different time windows .

As mentioned above, a detection of TeV photons from a source located at z=0.444 would pose serious problems to conventional models of photon propagation over cosmological distances, where the high energy gammas are expected to disappear due to pair electron-positron production in the EBL.

sources.summary: VERITAS detected 3C 66A strongly in 2008, finding day-scale variability, a steep spectrum, uncertain redshift, and evidence excluding nearby 3C 66B.

quotes.quote: The second IBL to be discovered by VERITAS was 3C 66A . This is a rather famous source that has long been considered a likely candidate for VHE emission. 33 h of observation in 2008 by VERITAS resulted in a strong detection ( 21 ) of 3C 66A with variability seen on day time scales.

The measured energy spectrum is very steep, = 4.11 0.4_ stat 0.6_ sys , which may be entirely due to the absorption by the EBL; 3C 66A has an uncertain redshift of z = 0.44 . 3C 66A lies 0.12^ away from the radio galaxy 3C 66B. However, as shown in Figure 4, the VERITAS data exclude the position of 3C 66B at the 4.3 level.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 66A as one of four TeV blazars giving strong EBL constraints, noting its assumed uncertain redshift and its deviation from the redshift-correlation plot.

quotes.quote: Through calculation, we find that four blazars, 3C 66A, 0716+714, 3C 279, and PG 1553+113, give stronger constraint on the EBL density. Since other TeV blazars are consistent with all listed EBL models within the error range, we do not give their constraint. The EBL upper limits given by the spectra of four TeV blazars are shown in Fig. fig.1 .
Also, we should note that the redshift of 3C 66A, assumed to be z = 0.444, has large uncertain (Miller et al. 1978). If its redshift is less than 0.444, the upper limits obtained will be relaxed.
3C 66A and 0716+714 obviously deviates the correlation shown in Fig. fig.7 , it implies that the assumed redshift might be uncorrect.

sources.summary: The paper models 3C 66A as a TeV blazar whose GeV spectrum requires SSC plus external Compton components, constraining its redshift to 0.15-0.31 and locating the gamma-ray emission beyond the inner BLR.

quotes.quote: Received --; in original form -- firstpage - lastpage 2011 The emission mechanism of TeV blazar 3C 66A is studied employing multi-wavelength observational data. The GeV spectrum observed by Fermi LAT cannot be explained by pure synchrotron self-Compton (SSC) radiation. A multi-component leptonic jet model including the absorption of extragalactic background light reproduces the quasi-simultaneous spectral energy distribution. The external Compton component of the broad line region (BLR) is important for emission above 10 GeV, while emission from 0.1 GeV to 10 GeV is dominated by the SSC component.

Our model implies that the redshift of 3C 66A should be between 0.15 and 0.31, the most likely value being 0.21. Our results also indicate that the ray emission region is beyond the inner zone of BLR.

sources.summary: The paper describes J0222 + 4302 as an intermediate-peaked BL Lac with frequent INOV, including a sub-hour glitch and confirmed variability in new monitoring.

quotes.quote: J0222 + 4302 (3C 66A; z =0.444 ) This blazar (Bramel et al. \ 2005) has been classified as an intermediate-peaked BL Lac object (IBL) whose nonthermal emission peaks in the range 10^ 15 10^ 16 Hz (Perri et al. 2003). During 1998--2000 it was monitored in our programme on 7 nights, for durations ranging between 5 and 10 hours per night. INOV was detected on 6 of the nights (Sagar et al. \ 2004). The only sub-hour feature seen in those DLCs is a 1.5 per cent `glitch' detected on the night of 13 Nov. \ 1999 at 19.6 U.T.
In Table 3 we have combined those published data with the two DLCs obtained in the present work. Of these, the DLC on 28 Sept. \ 2009 showed confirmed INOV using both C_ eff and F statistics.

sources.summary: 3C 66A is described as a z=0.444 low-frequency-peaked BL Lac with multi-band variability, one-sided superluminal core-jet structure, measured core shift, and radio-flare-related core activity.

quotes.quote: Shen -- High precision position measurements of the cores in 3C 66A and 3C 66B (invited) 3C 66A is a low frequency peaked BL Lac object at z=0.444 . It is characterized by prominent variability at radio, IR, and optical frequencies. It shows a one-sided core-jet structure with detected superluminal motion.

The core shift with frequency has been estimated in 2001 and 2006. A large difference has been found between the two measurements possibly due to a strong flux density increase at 15 GHz in 2006. This radio flare is possibly due to the core activity as shown by a new component that emerged from the central core region.

sources.summary: The paper monitors 3C 66A across GeV, optical, polarization, and near-IR bands, finding changing correlations, two emission components, and radio-jet alignment with optical polarization.

quotes.quote: 3C 66A We studied the long-term variations of 3C 66A over 2 years in the GeV band with Fermi and in the optical (flux and polarization) and near infrared band with the Kanata telescope. In 2008, we find a correlation between the gamma-ray flux and the optical properties. This is in contrast to the later behaviours during 2009--2010, a weak correlation along with a gradual increase of the optical flux. We conclude that the different behaviors observed between the first year and the later years might be explained by postulating two different emission components. DA shown in Table tab:PA indicates that the position angle of radio jet is close to the average PA.
It should be noted that a correlation between PD and total flux is significant in 2009.

sources.summary: The paper models 3C66A as an intermediate-peaked BL Lac with debated redshift, using simultaneous SEDs to estimate a 68% redshift range of 0.16-0.32.

quotes.quote: 3C66A 3C66A is an IBL ( 10^ 14 Hz < _ pk <10^ 15 Hz ) object with a debated redshift. firstly proposed a tentative redshift of z = 0.44, but the Mg II emission was still not confirmed. only gave a modest lower limit of z> 0.10 . Some authors fin2008b, yan2010, fur2013 also tried to constrain the redshift and only gave the range from 0.10 to 1.67. In the paper, we use the simultaneous SEDs given by and , the timescale of emitting blob with 12 hours and _ min =200 given by .
In summary, the redshift of 3C66A is 0.16 and 0.24 by the PBL and PL models respectively, and the redshift of 68 C.L. is 0.16 - 0.32, which is in the range predicted by .

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 66A as a z=0.444 IBL blazar with TeV emission, core-jet structure, long optical monitoring, variability, and bluer-when-brighter behavior.

quotes.quote: The blazar 3C 66A is classified as an IBL, because its synchrotron peak is located between 10^ 15 and 10^ 16 Hz 2003A&A...407..453P,2010ApJ...716...30A . The redshift was determined to be z = 0.444 by independent authors 1978bllo.conf..176M,1993ApJS...84..109L . Ever since its optical counterpart was identified by 1974ApJ...190L..97W , 3C 66A was studied over a wide frequency range, from radio to ray. Very Long Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) images from 2.3 to 43 GHz revealed a typical core-jet structure on parsec scales e.g. 2001ApJS..134..181J,2002ApJ...577...85M . It is one of the sources detected in TeV e.g. 2009ApJ...692L..29A,2009ApJ...693L.104A .
Our optical observations of 3C 66A in the , i , and o bands were carried out for 237, 248, and 235 nights, respectively, between 2005 January and 2011 February (Table sources ).

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 66A as an IBL with uncertain redshift, fits its SED well with two EED models, and says further hard-X-ray observations are needed.

quotes.quote: 2. 3C 66A AS an IBL, its redshift is still under debated. In this paper, we set z=0.44 to fit the SEDs. We take t_ v,min =12hr and '_ min =200 zhang2012 .

As shown in Fig.2, we can see that the fitting SEDs are very well in two types of EEDs. In the left panel of Fig.8, the EBL density in BPL EEDs is consistent with that of , and in PLLP EEDs, it is higher than that of in each band. Anyway, we can't rule out both results, which need to distinguish the SEDs via further work using the 20 - 50 KeV observation.

sources.summary: The paper uses 3C 66A optical polarimetry at z=0.444, finding a positive fitted coefficient and a wavelength dependence inconsistent with LIV, implying intrinsic astrophysical origin.

quotes.quote: The linear polarization observations of these five blazars are presented in Table tab:observation , including the following information for each object: the energy bands in which polarization is observed; the observed linear polarization angles; and the corresponding uncertainties of the polarization angles. These polarization observations have been obtained simultaneously in five optical bands ( UBVRI ) for all blazars. The redshifts of the five blazars 3C 66A, S5 0716+714, OJ 287, MK 421, and PKS 2155-304 are 0.444, 0.310, 0.306, 0.031, and 0.116, respectively.

As shown in Figure fig:fit , the polarization angles of blazars S5 0716+714 and PKS 2155-304 are proportional to E^2 with a negative coefficient, and the best-fitting values are =(-9.63 4.640) 10^ 8 ( _ 0 =0.320 0.058 rad) and =(-5.32 3.587) 10^ 7 ( _ 0 =1.645 0.018 rad), respectively. While the rest of the blazars show a positive coefficient with the fitting parameters =(3.386 1.249) 10^ 7 ( _ 0 =0.623 0.024 rad), =(6.546 22.769) 10^ 6 ( _ 0 =0.046 0.482 rad), and =(6.226 7.715) 10^ 7 ( _ 0 =1.544 0.094 rad) for 3C 66A, MK 421, and OJ 287, respectively. Our constraints show that except for 3C 66A at the 5.4 confidence level and PKS 2155-304 at the 3.0 confidence level, the data set of the other three blazars are consistent with the possibility of no LIV at all (i.e., = 0 ) within the 1.8 confidence level.

sources.summary: The paper reports strong optical polarization in 3C 66A, significant day-to-day and intranight variability in polarization and position angle, and only marginal circular polarization.

quotes.quote: 3C 66A Our results on this source, showing an average polarization of 27.3 in U band, confirm that it is one of the most strongly polarized BL Lacs (Takalo Sillanp a a ). Some day-to-day variability of both P and PA is present (Fig.  ter ). A simple ^2 test shows significant variability of P at a 95 confidence level or more in U , B and R filters and of PA in U , V and R .

Only in 3C 66A, the object with the smallest errors due to its relatively high brightness, was circular polarization marginally detected at the 2 level in V and R bands. A detection of non-null circular polarization in 3C 66A was claimed by Takalo Sillanp a a ( ), with larger values than found in our data. We conservatively put an upper limit of 0.4 with a 3 confidence level on the circular polarization of this BL Lac.

sources.summary: The paper models 3C 66A's high-energy spectrum, explaining its Fermi-LAT and VERITAS components with SSC and EC scenarios whose viability depends strongly on the uncertain redshift.

quotes.quote: Our goal is to understand the nature of blazars and the mechanisms for the generation of high-energy rays, through the investigation of the blazar 3C 66A. We model the high energy spectrum of 3C 66A, which has been observed recently with the Fermi-LAT and VERITAS telescope. The spectrum has a hard change from the energy range of 0.2-100 GeV to 200-500 GeV in recent almost contemporaneous observations of two telescopes.

If z=0.444 is adopted, we are able to use the SSC model to produce the Fermi-LAT component and the EC model to the VERITAS component. However, if z=0.1, the intrinsic VERITAS spectrum will be softer, there will be a smooth link between the Fermi-LAT and VERITAS spectra which can be explained using a SSC model.

sources.summary: The review uses 3C 66A as an intermediate BL Lac example, noting gamma-ray dominance, proton-synchrotron model requirements, uncertain redshift, and a preferred z about 0.2-0.3.

quotes.quote: Figure SEDs shows examples of blazar SEDs along the blazar sequence, from the FSRQ 3C279 (a), via the LBL BL Lacertae (b) and the IBL 3C 66A (), to the HBL RGB J0710+591 (d). The sequence of increasing synchrotron peak frequency is clearly visible. However, the \ spectrum of the LBL BL Lacertae indicates a ray flux clearly below the synchrotron level, while the SED of the IBL 3C 66A is clearly dominated by the \ ray flux, in contradiction with the traditional blazar sequence. The emission from blazars is known to be variable at all wavelengths.

The redshift of 3C 66A is usually quoted as z = 0.444 , but is actually highly uncertain as well ( ). A scan through redshifts yielded a preferred redshift of z 0.2 - 0.3 for this object, in good agreement with the estimate found by . The leptonic model fit to 3C66A shown in Fig. SEDs is based on a redshift of z = 0.3 .

sources.summary: The paper finds 3C 66A has a nearly flat GeV spectrum inconsistent with a maximal cascade model, so direct source gamma rays dominate and cascades only moderately modify the spectrum.

quotes.quote: The observed 0.1-100 GeV band spectra of Mrk 501, PKS 2155-304 and 3C 66A have photon indices close to =2 with almost no curvature of the spectrum over the entire energy range. Such a ``flat'' spectral energy distribution is difficult to obtain in the ``maximal cascade'' model. Although the ``universal'' cascade spectrum has average photon index close to =2 in the 0.1-100 GeV band, its deviation from the pure powerlaw behavior is significant, so that this model is in contradiction with the observed spectrum for these blazars. This suggests that the dominant contribution to the observed source spectrum in the cases of Mrk 501, PKS 2155-304 and 3C 66A comes from direct gamma-rays from the source as shown in Fig. mrk-pks-3C66 . Addition of the cascade contribution to the intrinsic emission component in Mrk 501, PKS 2155-304 and 3C 66A leads only to a moderate modification of the source spectrum in the GeV energy range.

sources.summary: The paper uses MAGIC observations of 3C 66A, noting its uncertain emission-line redshift and modeling the source with SSC parameters to expose the pile-up problem.

quotes.quote: 3C 66A This source at z=0.444 was observed in a flaring state by VERITAS and more recently by MAGIC . Both spectra are roughly compatible within uncertainties. Note that the redshift considered for this object is calculated using just one emission line and is thus not very secure, as discussed in ref. . In this study, only the MAGIC spectrum is used due to its larger energy coverage, which better exposes the pile-up problem. We model the source using the parameters given in Table tab1 , which were extracted from ref. and were computed by the authors using an SSC model.

sources.summary: The paper applies the same internal-absorption blazar model to 3C 66A, emphasizing its debated redshift, variable VHE flare, GeV excess, and difficulty matching optical and X-ray data simultaneously.

quotes.quote: We study the applicability of the idea of internal absorption of rays produced through synchrotron radiation of ultrarelativistic protons in highly magnetized blobs to 1ES 0229+200 and 3C 66A, the two TeV blazars which show unusually hard intrinsic ray spectra after being corrected for the intergalactic absorption. We show that for certain combinations of reasonable model parameters, even with quite modest energy requirements, the scenario allows a self-consistent explanation of the non-thermal emission of these objects in the keV, GeV, and TeV energy bands.

The blazar 3C 66A shows a variable VHE signal, as seen with VERITAS acciari09*,lat66a , with a 6 Crab flux flaring episode. Fermi LAT collaboration reported a significant GeV ray excess from the source. Moreover, an increase of the GeV flux simultaneously with the VHE flare was observed lat66a .

sources.summary: VERITAS detected 3C 66A as a variable, soft-spectrum VHE source, excluded nearby 3C 66B as the excess location, and used simultaneous multiwavelength data to model its flaring SED.

quotes.quote: 3C 66A is an EGRET-detected IBL with an uncertain redshift. VERITAS observed this iconic blazar for 33 h of quality-selected live time in 2007-08 , resulting in the discovery of an excess (VER J0222+430) of 1791 VHE rays (21.2 ). The observed integral flux above 200 GeV is 6 Crab and shows evidence for variability on a timescale of days. The VHE spectrum is soft ( = 4.1 0.4 ), and does not change (within limited statistics) during the nights of the observed flare . The nearby (separation 0.12 ^ ) radio galaxy 3C 66B, suggested as the possible origin of the MAGIC excess observed from the same region in 2007, is excluded (at a level of 4.3 ) as the location of the VERITAS excess. During the flaring episode in October 2008, a simultaneous MWL SED was built with VERITAS, Fermi-LAT, F-GAMMA, GASP-WEBT, PAIRITEL, MDM, ATOM, Swift, and Chandra observations.

sources.summary: The paper models 3C 66A as a VERITAS-detected IBL, assumes a fiducial redshift, and finds its upward-curving GeV spectrum difficult to fit.

quotes.quote: We found that the following objects fulfilled our criteria: The FSRQs 3C 273, 3C 279, 3C 454.3, PKS 1510-089, PKS 0420-01, and PKS 0528+134; the LBLs BL Lacertae, AO 0235+164, S5 0716+714, and OJ 287; and the IBLs W Comae and 3C 66A. In the case of 3C 66A, the catalog value of the redshift of z = 0.444 is highly questionable. In our modeling, we assume a fiducial redshift of z = 0.3 for 3C 66A.

While BL Lac objects detected at > 100 GeV rays by ground-based Atmospheric Cherenkov Telescopes have often been found to be well represented by pure SSC models, the careful analysis of the simultaneous SEDs of the VERITAS-detected IBLs W Comae and 3C 66A WComb,3C66A revealed that also in these two cases, a pure SSC fit would require rather extreme parameters with magnetic fields far below equipartition. However, the unusual, apparent upward curvature of the Fermi LAT spectrum of 3C 66A and W Comae can not be satisfactorily represented with our model, irrespective of the dominant target photon field for external Compton scattering.

sources.summary: Swift X-ray monitoring finds 3C 66A dominated by a 2008 outburst, with strong fractional variability, softer-band variability, and interval spectra showing changing synchrotron and inverse-Compton contributions.

quotes.quote: 3C 66A: The light curve (see upper panel of Fig. lc_3c66a ) shows variability manifested mainly in one outburst in 2008. The flux changes are characterized by a fractional variability amplitude Edelson88 F_ var =(67 2) with the uncertainty calculated following . Except for this outburst (with a count rate of 0.7 count/s) the source flux level observed in different epochs is about 0.1-0.2 counts/s. The soft and the hard X-ray light curves in the energy bands of 0.3-3.4 keV and 3.4-10 keV, respectively are shown in Fig lc_3c66a . The fractional variability amplitudes for the soft and hard bands are following: F_ var =(69 2) and F_ var =(56 1) , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C66A as an intermediate-peaked BL Lac and TeV source, noting its core-halo radio structure, one-sided VLBI jet, and marginal radio but rapid optical variability.

quotes.quote: 3C66A, 2FGL J 0222.6+4302 (ISP BL) This is an intermediate synchrotron peaked BL Lac object ISP BL, abd10,fan16 and a TeV ray source. Optical variability at short time scales has been reported for this source xie94 . In the radio band, it appears as a core halo on the < 100 kpc scale and has a one-sided VLBI core jet to the south, in which fast apparent superluminal motions of up to 29 were reported by . No evident correlation between long-term ray by the Fermi Large-Area Telescope and multiband radio emission was found in 3C66A fuhr14 . The present results show a marginal inter-day variability at 5 GHz (with reduced Chi-squared of 1.2) and rapid optical variations with time scales of 0.6 0.1 and 1.4 0.2 days (see Fig. fig1 and Table tab1 ), and no correlation between the radio and optical variations is found.

sources.summary: The paper studies the BL Lac object 3C 66A, constraining its redshift from its environment and spectrum and proposing that its host galaxy belongs to a z=0.340 cluster.

quotes.quote: The BL Lac object 3C 66A is one of the most luminous extragalactic sources at TeV rays (VHE, i.e. E >100 GeV). Since TeV ray radiation is absorbed by the extragalactic background light (EBL), it is crucial to know the redshift of the source in order to reconstruct its original spectral energy distribution, as well as to constrain EBL models. However, the optical spectrum of this BL Lac is almost featureless, so a direct measurement of z is very difficult; in fact, the published redshift value for this source ( z=0.444 ) has been strongly questioned.

The blazar's spectrum shows broad absorption features that we identify as arising in the intergalactic medium, thus allowing us to tentatively set a redshift lower limit at z_ 3C66A 0.33 . As a consequence, we propose that 3C 66A is hosted in a galaxy that belongs to a cluster at z=0.340 .

sources.summary: The paper describes 0219+428/3C 66A as a BL Lac with optical periodicity claims, very fast superluminal motion, modest radio variability, calibration complications from 3C 66B, and an optical decline in 2005.

quotes.quote: 0219+428 (3C 66A) This BL Lac object has been observed intensively at optical wavelengths (e.g. \ 2005ApJ...631..169B ) and found a possible periodicity of 65 days in its optical bright state. Very fast superluminal motion of up to 29 was reported by . Despite some small flux changes, the radio light curves reveal not much variability (Fig. fig:lc ). A major problem for radio measurements of 3C 66A is its close companion 3C 66B, which at some position angles appears in the OFF measurements or the cross scans and makes the calibration more difficult. During the first 50 days of the optical light curve a brightness decrease is visible, which reached its minimum at about JD=2453650 (October 2005), with some shorter variability on top of the long-term trend (Fig. fig:olc ).

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 66A as an IBL TeV source observed by VERITAS, Fermi/LAT, and VERITAS together, with soft spectrum, day-scale variability, and SSC+EC comparison.

quotes.quote: 3C 66A. A IBL object, the observation of VHE was performed from September 2007 through January 2008 by VERITAS and it was confirmed to be a TeV source with an integral flux above 200 GeV 6 of Crab flux (Acciari et al. 2009b). The observed spectrum can be characterized by a soft power-law with photon index =4.1 and a variability on the timescale of days was found. The simultaneous GeV-TeV observations by Fermi /LAT and VERITAS were performed in October 2008 (Reyes et al. 2009). The simultaneously observed broadband SED is from Abdo et al. (2011).

sources.summary: The paper treats 0219+428 as a BL Lac with uncertain redshift, notes absorption-based redshift limits, and finds its innermost TeV-jet components moving inward, suggesting an unstable core reference point.

quotes.quote: 0219+428 (3C 66A): No reliable spectroscopic redshift exists for this BL Lac object. have set limits of 0.3347 z 0.41 based on intergalactic absorption features.

In particular, the innermost two jet components of two TeV-emitting BL Lacs in our sample: 0219+428 (3C 66A) and 1219+285 (W Comae) are both inward-moving. The small velocities and core separations of these moving components may indicate that the core is not a stable reference point in these two jets.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes VERITAS low-flux-state observations of 3C 66A, finding a 12 sigma detection and a curved very-high-energy spectrum without significant state-dependent shape change.

quotes.quote: Through long-term, low-flux-state analyses of 3C 66A and W Comae, we contribute the VHE portion of a future SED for each of these IBL sources in their low-flux states. VERITAS observations of 3C 66A totaling 61 hours of live time in the low-flux state led to a 12 detection. A log-parabola model was found to better fit the spectrum than a power law model, showing significant curvature in the VHE spectrum.
For both sources, there is no significant difference in the spectral shape between the low- and high-flux states in VHE.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 66A as a VHE BL Lac source in its monitoring sample and reports its optical/radio flux level, variability trends, and correlation behavior.

quotes.quote: 3C 66A : One of the brightest sources in both bands. The optical light curve is characterized by fast variability, while in the radio band the variability is clearly slower. In both light curves there is a highly significant decreasing trend. As discussed in Sect. 4.3, the time lag of 860 days (radio leading) is caused by the large radio flare in the beginning of the light curve (see Fig. Fig:lc3 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 66A as a well-known BL Lac and HE/VHE ray blazar with strong historical optical polarization, typically 10-20 percent and sometimes higher.

quotes.quote: 3C 66A 3C 66A is a well-known BL Lac at redshift z >= 0.3347 furniss2013 . The blazar is a bright source of HE (Acciari et al. 2009) and very high energy (VHE) rays (E 100 GeV) 2009ApJ...692L..29A, acciari2009 . There are many polarimetric monitoring observations of this source (e.g. and references therein). In these data the polarisation degree is always high, typically between 10 - 20 with the maximum value measured 33 .

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 66A as a well-known ISP whose XMM-Newton spectrum is power-law-like, with an index matching earlier work and no significant curvature.

quotes.quote: 3C 66A: This source is a well known ISP ( e.g. Aliu et al. 2009). In previous studies, significant negative curvature is found in the SED using Swift /XRT observations (i.e. Wierzcholska Wagner 2016) with break energy of 3.4 keV. We examined the spectra of this blazar using XMM Newton pointing and found that it is well described with power law model. The spectral index ( =2.45) is well matched with the previous studies. However, we did not find significant spectral curvature for this source.

sources.summary: The paper gives 3C 66A an individual blazar variability note, summarizing TeV-band detections, GeV or hard-X-ray behavior, and variability classification.

quotes.quote: 3C 66A : This well-known Very Energetic Radiation Imaging Telescope Array System (VERITAS) detected blazar was in a high GeV activity state during the first year of the LAT operation 2011ApJ...726...43A . The high activity was also reflected in the TeV band light curve Figure fig:1 ; 2011ApJ...726...58A . The analysis suggested the contemporaneous flux enhancements in both TeV and GeV bands (Table tab:dcf ). Later, the object went into a low activity state but was frequently detected in the TeV band at a flux level comparable to its 15 years averaged value.

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C66A as an uncertain TeV-detected AGN, citing the Crimean detection, EGRET association, large redshift, LBL classification, and time-variable plausibility.

quotes.quote: 3C66A: This is, perhaps, the least certain of the TeV detections; it was reported by the Crimean Astrophysical Observatory at the 5.1 level of significance . Although a well-studied AGN and detected by EGRET, 3C66A seems an unlikely candidate for TeV emission because of its large redshift (z = 0.444) and its classification as an LBL. Upper limits have come from other observations ; but these were at other epochs and the source could be time variable on long time-scales. It was bright at longer wavelengths during the observations by the Crimean group.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 66A as the hard GeV counterpart of the TeV region, with a z=0.444 HBL interpretation giving better agreement between extrapolated GeV and TeV spectra.

quotes.quote: 3C 66A/B: TeV emission from this region detected by VERITAS (3C 66A, HBL, z=0.444 ) and MAGIC (3C 66B, RG, z=0.0211 ). Fermi detects hard GeV emission, coincident with 3C 66A (Figures FIG::3C66A_TS and FIG::3C66A_SPEC ). An extrapolation of the GeV spectrum to TeV energies is in better agreement with the TeV data measurements assuming z=0.444 than z=0.0221 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies 0219+428 as an early post-merger BL Lac with transitory infrared colors, mostly straight radio jets, optical quasi-periodicity, and radio/X-ray variability.

quotes.quote: * 0219+428 (3C 66A) (BL Lac, z=0.444 ): This object is positioned in the transitory region of the color-color diagram, thus is classified as an early post-merger system. Both the VLBI and VLA jet appear straight (with the VLBA jet exhibiting one sharp bend). It manifests almost periodic variability in the optical, and variability at radio and X-ray wavelengths.

sources.summary: The paper uses an upper limit for 3C 66A's accretion luminosity, adopts B13's redshift despite uncertainty, and rescales the luminosity limit for its jet-power estimates.

quotes.quote: For two objects (S5 0716+714 and 3C 66A), B13 give no estimates of L_ acc , and we use the upper limits given by G10. In the case of 3C 66A, there is also an uncertainty of its redshift, and B13 used an estimated redshift of z=0.3 based on EBL-corrected SED modelling Abdo11 , which is also marginally consistent with the spectroscopically determined lower limit on the redshift by Furniss13 . Since we use the estimates of the jet power from B13, we adopt their redshift value and rescale the upper limit of L_ acc of G10, who used z=0.444 , by the ratio of D_L^2 , where D_L is the luminosity distance.

sources.summary: The paper describes 0219+428 as a BL Lac whose two innermost jet features move inward, with no reliable spectroscopic redshift and a host-magnitude lower limit.

quotes.quote: 0219+428 (3C 66A): The two innermost jet features of this BL Lac object have robust inward motions. No reliable spectroscopic redshift has been published to date. reported z > 0.42 based on the host galaxy magnitude.

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C 66A as a TeV BL Lac with a featureless high-S/N spectrum, rejects a previously proposed redshift, and leaves its redshift unknown.

quotes.quote: As example, Fig. 4 shows the featureless spectra of two TeV BLLs RGB J0136+391 and 3C 66A. For the latter, a previous redshift of 0.444 was proposed in literature miller1978 and reported in the NED. On the basis of our high S/N ratio and featureless spectrum, we cannot support this value and thus the redshift of 3C 66A is still unknown and the emission models of the source assuming the wrong redshift should be revised.

gmw_00001121.name: PKS 0735+178
gmw_00001121.overview: PKS 0735+178 is a highly variable BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1002.3531, arXiv:1702.02504] located at a redshift of approximately 0.45 [arXiv:2206.03296, arXiv:2301.06565]. It features a superluminal parsec-scale radio jet [arXiv:1702.02504] that exhibits sharp bends [arXiv:astro-ph/0412296, arXiv:astro-ph/0604543] and has been spatially associated with several high-energy neutrino events [arXiv:2301.06565, arXiv:2401.06666].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes PKS 0735+178 as a BL Lac object whose parsec-scale jet alternates between bent and straight modes with associated changes in knot kinematics and optical variability.

quotes.quote: The strong radio source PKS 0735+178 is an optically bright classical BL Lac object, highly variable at radio/optical wavelengths (e.g., Goyal et al. 2009 and references therein). Based on an absorption feature in the optical spectrum, a lower redshift limit of 0.424 was established (Abraham, et al. 1991; Stickel et al. 1993; Scarpa et al. 2000). This is consistent with the non detection of the optical host in the HST snapshot imaging survey by Sbarufatti et al. (2005). A companion (possible absorber) has been detected at a projected separation of 22 kpc (Pursimo et al. 1999).

We study 0735+178 here to augment and improve the understanding of the peculiar motions in the jets of BL Lac objects as a class. We analyzed 15 GHz VLBA (Very Long Baseline Array) observations (/MOJAVE survey) performed at 23 epochs between 1995.27 and 2008.91. We then compared the jet kinematics with the optical and radio light curves available for this BL Lac object and point out some striking correlations between the properties of the radio knots and the features in the light curves. We found a drastic structural mode change in the VLBI jet of 0735+178, between 2000.4 and 2001.8 when its twice sharply bent trajectory turned into a linear shape.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 0735+178 as a BL Lac object at z = 0.45, then reports intraday variability, a possible 22-day R-to-ray lag, and activity-dependent harder/bluer-when-brighter behavior.

quotes.quote: PKS 0735+178 (also known as OI 158, S3 0735+17, 4FGL J0738.1+1742) was identified as a BL Lac object Carswell1974 . Its J2000 coordinates are RA= 07h38m07.3937s, DEC = +17d42m18.998s, and the redshift is z = 0.45 Rector2001 .

In this work, we collected the long-term multi-wavelength data of PKS 0735+178, and also performed a multi-color optical monitoring on intraday timescale. The intraday variability was detected on one night. On the long timescale, a possible 22-day time lag was found between the R and ray bands.

PKS 0735+178 showed a significant harder-when-brighter in the ray band, which is consistent with the observed optical bluer-when-brighter trend on both long-term and intraday timescales. We found that the HWB and BWB trends will be enhanced during the active states, especially for the historical low state. Such phenomenon indicates a special activity-dependent color behavior of PKS 0735+178, and it could be well interpreted by the jet emission model.

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS 0735+178 as an IHBL/blazar at z=0.45, flaring across wavebands near IceCube/Baikal/KM3NeT neutrino events and modeled with leptonic and lepto-hadronic SED scenarios.

quotes.quote: The ray blazar PKS 0735+178, located outside the 90 localization region at 2.2 deg from the best-fit IC-211208A event, was found to be flaring across all wavebands. In addition to leptonic synchrotron (SYN) and synchrotron self-Compton (SSC) emission, we invoke photohadronic ( p ) interactions inside the jet to model the spectral energy distribution (SED) and neutrino emission. We analyze the 100 days ray and X-ray data and 10 days around the neutrino event is chosen to generate the broadband SED.

PKS 0735+178 is an IHBL located at a redshift of z=0.45 0.06 as determined from the detection of its host galaxy 2012A&A...547A...1N . The redshift value of 0.65 has also been suggested in a more recent study by 2021ATel15132....1F , but we used 0.45 in this work. During its largest flaring state in December 2021, it was found to be associated with neutrino events detected by IceCube 2021GCN.31191....1I , Baikal 2021ATel15112....1D , Baksan 2021ATel15143....1P , and KM3NeT 2022ATel15290....1F . We have analyzed the observational data from Swift-XRT , UVOT , NuSTAR and Fermi-LAT to build the multi-wavelength spectral energy distribution (SED) during the flaring state. We initially modeled the SED with a leptonic model, which can explain the SED satisfactorily, and subsequently with a lepto-hadronic model to determine whether PKS 0735+178 could be the source of astrophysical neutrinos.

sources.summary: The review describes PKS 0735+17 as a BL Lac in a historic multiwavelength outburst, possibly associated with several neutrino detections and modeled with lepto-hadronic scenarios.

quotes.quote:
IceCube-211208 and PKS 0735+17 IceCube-211208 was a track-like event with an estimated energy of E 172  TeV with a relatively large statistical 90  localization error region of 13 square degrees. The BL Lac type ISP blazar PKS 0735+17 ( z = 0.45 ) is actually located slightly outside this error region, but was in a historical outburst in HE rays ( Fermi LAT), X-rays ( Swift XRT), and optical -- UV at the time of the neutrino alert (see Fig. Fig:PKS0735_lc , from ). In addition, the source is positionally consistent with the (rather large) localization error regions of a Baikal-GVD detected neutrino event ( E 43  TeV) 3.95 hours after the IceCube-211208 event , of a GeV neutrino detected 4 days earlier by the Baksan Underground Scintillation Telescope , as well as an 18  TeV neutrino a week after IceCube-211208, found in an archival search of data from the Km3NeT undersea neutrino detectors, currently under construction in the Meditarranean Sea . It is this multitude of potential neutrino associations as well as the exceptional flaring state of PKS 0736+17 which drew the attention of the community to this source as a possible, prolific VHE neutrino source.

investigated several lepto-hadronic single-zone emission scenarios for the multi-wavelength and neutrino emission from PKS 0735+178.

Their most optimistic scenario requires a jet with a kinetic luminosity close to the Eddington limit of the source, where relativistic protons interact with an external radiation field from the BLR (see Fig. Fig:PKS0735_BLR ), resulting in a 6.7  probability of detecting one muon / antimuon neutrino during a 3-week flaring period.

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 0735+178 as a potential AGN counterpart to IC-211208A, notes multiwavelength flaring, reports H.E.S.S. observations, and gives non-detection upper limits.

quotes.quote: Three potential AGN gamma-ray counterparts were identified: 4FGL J0738.4+1539, 4FGL J0743.1+1713, and PKS 0735+178 (redshift z=0.45 ). Multiwavelength observations revealed that the gamma-ray blazar PKS 0735+178 was in flaring state in the radio, optical, X-ray, and GeV gamma-ray band  pks0735_icrc2023 . As depicted in fig:ic211208Amap , PKS 0735+178 was located just outside the 90 error region of the neutrino event, 2.0^ away from the best-fit position. observed the direction of PKS 0735+178 for a total of 16 hours from December 8 to 15, 2021, at an average zenith angle of 42.2^ above 0.1 TeV energy .

A circular ROI of 0.1 ^ centered on the position of PKS 0735+178 was defined, and two regions of 0.25 ^ radius around the nearby sources 4FGL J0738.4+1539 and 4FGL J0743.1+1713 were excluded from the background estimation. No significant gamma-ray excess above the expected background was detected from the direction of PKS 0735+178. Therefore, integral flux upper limits at 95 C.L. in the energy range between 0.4 TeV and 100 TeV in the full field of view (FoV) were calculated (see fig:ic211208Amap ).

sources.summary: The paper presents decade-long BVRI monitoring of the blazar PKS 0735+178, finding achromatic optical variability, shot-noise-like behavior, mid-term timescales, and a history of radio/X-ray/gamma-ray emission.

quotes.quote: New data and results on the optical behaviour of the prominent blazar PKS 0735+178 (also know as OI 158, S3 0735+17, DA 237, 1ES 0735+178, 3EG J0737+1721) are presented, through the most continuous BVRI data available in the period 1994-2004 (about 500 nights of observations). In addition, the whole historical light curve, and a new photometric calibration of comparison stars in the field of this source are reported. Several methods for timeseries analysis of sparse data sets are developed, adapted and applied to the reconstructed historical light curve and to each observing season of our unpublished optical database on PKS 0735+178.

Optical spectral indexes are calculated from the multi-band observations and studied on long-term (years) durations as well. For the first time in this source, variability modes, characteristic timescales and the signal power spectrum are explored and identified over 3 decades in time with a sufficient statistics. The novel investigation of mid-term optical scales (days, weeks), could be also applied and compared to blazar gamma-ray light curves that will be provided, on the same timescales, by the forthcoming GLAST observatory. In the last 10 years the optical emission of PKS 0735+178 exhibited a rather achromatic behaviour and a variability mode resembling the shot-noise.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes PKS 0735+178 as a low-frequency-peaked BL Lac whose optical variability follows red noise, with flatter gamma-ray variability and no detected PSD cutoffs.

quotes.quote: We present the results of our power spectral analysis for the BL Lac object PKS 0735+178 utilizing the Fermi LAT survey at high-energy rays, several ground-based optical telescopes, and single-dish radio telescopes operating at GHz frequencies. The novelty of our approach is that, by combining long-term and densely sampled intra-night light curves in the optical regime, we were able to construct for the first time the optical power spectrum of the blazar for a time domain extending from 23 years down to minutes. Our analysis reveals that: (i) the optical variability is consistent with a pure red noise, for which the power spectral density can well be approximated by a single power-law throughout the entire time domain probed; (ii) the slope of power spectral density at high-energy rays ( 1), is significantly flatter than that found at radio and optical frequencies ( 2) within the corresponding time variability range; (iii) for the derived power spectra we did not detect any low-frequency flattening, nor do we see any evidence for cut-offs at the highest frequencies down to the noise floor levels due to measurement uncertainties.

PKS 0735 + 178 J2000.0 R.A. =07^ h 38^ m 07 39 , Dec. =+17 42 19 99 ; redshift z = 0.45 0.06 ; 2012A&A...547A...1Nilsson is an otherwise typical example of a low-frequency-peaked BL Lac object detected in the GeV photon energy range 2010ApJ...715..429Abdo . It is highly polarized in the optical band PD_ opt > 3 ; 1996AJ....112.1877G, 2011ApJS..194...19W , and exhibits a flat-spectrum radio core with a superluminal pc-scale radio jet, both characteristic of blazars in general 1992ApJ...398..454Wills, 2016AJ....152...12L . Despite its pronounced optical and radio variability on yearly timescales 1988AJ.....95..374W, 2007A&A...467..465C , the source is relatively quiet in X-rays 1988ApJ...330..776M , and the existing rather sparse X-ray monitoring data preclude a meaningful power spectral analysis.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0735+178 as a BL Lac/LBL with host-based redshift and reports positive color correlations with significant bluer-when-brighter trends.

quotes.quote: PKS 0735+178 was first classified as a BL Lac object by 1974ApJ...190L.101C . The broadband spectrum suggested that it is a LBL 1996ApJ...463..424U . 2012A&A...547A...1N derived a redshift of 0.45 0.06 for this object by using the host galaxy as a standard candle.

As can be seen in Figure fig:all , the spectral index varied slightly between 1.1 and 1.8. One can see from Figure fig:allcolor , PKS 0735+178 exhibits different levels of positive correlations for all its observed colors. Significant BWB trends were found for the i and o colors, while the i-o color had significance less than 95 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes multi-band follow-up of blazar PKS 0735+178 near IceCube-211208A, finding flaring activity, a TeV cutoff, and difficulty for a one-zone SSC model.

quotes.quote: Here we present results of multi-band observations of the blazar PKS 0735+178 contemporaneous with or immediately following the IceCube astrophysical neutrino candidate IceCube-211208A. An earlier study explored the connection between PKS 0735+178 and IceCube-211208A on the basis of Fermi LAT, SWIFT (UVOT and XRT), and optical observations of the blazar .

The gamma-ray blazar PKS 0735+178 (redshift z=0.45 ) is located immediately outside of the 90 error region (2.13 ^ ; statistical error only) for the neutrino event, 2.2 ^ away from the best-fit position. Subsequent observations of PKS 0735+178 revealed flaring states in the radio band 2021ATel15105....1K , optical band 2021ATel15098....1Z , X-ray band 2021ATel15102....1S,2021ATel15109....1D,2021ATel15113....1F , and GeV gamma-ray band 2021ATel15099....1G .

Based on extensive follow-up observations across the electromagnetic spectrum, we analysed the state of the blazar PKS 0735+178 in the context of a potential association with a VHE neutrino event that was seen with IceCube in December 2021. We analytically demonstrated that the observed SED of the blazar, especially the gamma-ray cutoff in the TeV data, constitutes a challenge to a simple one-zone SSC model.

sources.summary: The paper monitored PKS 0735+178 intensively, finding modest polarization-percentage changes, stronger position-angle variability, and no circular polarization detection.

quotes.quote: PKS 0735+178 Nightly averages of P and PA measurements for this source are reported in Fig.  ter . Little variability is recorded in polarization percentage (significance level greater than 95 only in the V filter), and more significant variations in PA are seen. In all bands, an increase in P is seen in the last night, while the PA simultaneously rotates significantly with respect to the previous nights.

In the case of PKS 0735+178, marginal detection of circular polarization P=-0.24 0.12 was reported by Valtaoja et al. ( ). However, no such polarization was recorded during our campaign, even if our uncertainty level is similar. Our data show an upper limit of about 0.4 at the 3 level for PKS 0735+178.

sources.summary: The paper describes 0735+178 as a BL Lac source with no optical emission lines, a lower-limit redshift, compact VLBI core, sharply bent jet, and Doppler-beamed intermediate-scale emission.

quotes.quote: No emission lines have yet been identified in the optical spectrum of 0735+178, though a lower limit to the redshift of 0.424 is provided by a pair of absorption lines carswell74 . VLBI images of this source show a compact core and a jet that extends to the northeast (e.g. gabuzda92 ) while arcsecond scale VLA images reveal a point source ulvestad83,kollgaard92 . 8, 22 and 43 GHz VLBI images reveal a jet out to approximately 10 mas with two sharp apparent bends of 90 ^ within 2 mas of the core. It also appears that there was a dramatic change in the trajectory of the jet within the first few mas between 1992 and 1995 gomez99,gomez01b .

The intermediate scale flux density detected in 0735+178 appears to be associated with a well-collimated jet, and is therefore likely to be significantly Doppler beamed. For this reason, it is probably not significant that the observed intermediate scale luminosity is above the FR I limit.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0735+178 as a BL Lac with strong non-radial jet motion, quasi-stationary inner components, and ridge-line evolution that links two kinematic behaviors.

quotes.quote: The sources S5 0716+714 ( Britzen2009 ) and PKS 0735+178 ( Britzen2010b ), both classified as BL Lac objects, show similar behavior, with strong non-radial motions discovered in their jets. Although not as pronounced as in S5 1803+784, but also lacking the unprecedented number of VLBI epochs of S5 1803+784, both sources show jet components in their inner-jets that appear quasi-stationary with respect to the core. In addition, PKS 0735+178 appears to interchange between the classical blazar-like behavior of superluminally moving components and the behavior seen in S5 1803+784. PKS 0735+178 might be the key to our understanding of these non-radial motion dominated jets and potentially implies a geometrical effect as the origin of the observed properties.
Similar to S5 1803+784, PKS 0735+178 and S5 0716+714 also show the most prominent evolution of their jet ridge line out to 6 mas (39.6 pc and 33 pc, for their respective redshifts).

sources.summary: The platform paper uses PKS 0735+178 as a multi-messenger case: a gamma-ray flaring blazar spatially near neutrino candidates, followed across radio, optical, X-ray, GeV, and TeV bands.

quotes.quote: Only a few hours earlier, another notification (via the automatic analysis of data recorded by Fermi-LAT provided by the FLaapLUC Lenain:2018 pipeline) had announced the beginning of a gamma-ray flare of the blazar PKS 0735+178, located just outside the 90 error region of the neutrino event.
Furthermore, the KM3NeT neutrino detectors found a candidate up-going muon neutrino (18 TeV) on December 15, spatially coinciding with PKS 0735+178 with a p-value of 0.14.

These observations have revealed activity in the radio, optical, X-ray, and GeV gamma-ray bands, potentially associated with the neutrino event IceCube-211208A. Target-of-opportunity observations with imaging atmospheric Cherenkov telescopes, namely VERITAS and H.E.S.S., yielded stringent upper limits above 100 GeV. Detailed results of these observations that contribute to the study of the blazar PKS 0735+178 and its potential connection to the neutrino event IceCube-211208A are given in 2023arXiv230617819A .

sources.summary: The review says PKS 0735+178 drew attention after IC211208 coincided with a multiwavelength blazar flare and neutrinos from this blazar were discussed.

quotes.quote: PKS 0735+178. This source has attracted attention after the IC211208 event ( 171 TeV). Although the event did not meet the selection criteria established in (the energy was below 200 TeV) and therefore has not been used in any statistical analysis, it coincided with an historical blazar flare, recorded in all bands, from radio to gamma rays. During the days of the December 2021 flare, neutrino events were recorded by all high-energy neutrino telescopes operating on Earth -- in addition to IceCube, these are Baikal-GVD , Baksan Underground Scintillation Telescope (BUST) , and KM3NeT . Neutrinos from this blazar have been discussed, in particular, in .

sources.summary: The paper presents multi-frequency VLBI polarimetry of PKS 0735+178, describing a BL Lac jet whose quiescent-state structure has sharp bends, slow apparent motions, and constrained viewing and Lorentz factors.

quotes.quote: We present polarimetric 5 GHz to 43 GHz VLBI observations of the BL Lacertae object PKS 0735+178, spanning March 1996 to May 2000. Comparison with previous and later observations suggests that the overall kinematic and structural properties of the jet are greatly influenced by its activity. Time intervals of enhanced activity, as reported before 1993 and after 2000 by other studies, are followed by highly superluminal motion along a rectilinear jet. In contrast the less active state in which we performed our observations, shows subluminal or slow superluminal jet features propagating through a twisted jet with two sharp bends of about 90 ^ within the innermost three--milliarcsecond jet structure. Proper motion estimates from the data presented here allow us to constrain the jet viewing angle to values 9^ , and the bulk Lorentz factor to be between 2 and 4.

sources.summary: The paper confirms a previously reported 0735+178 jet RM gradient at 5.5 sigma and notes sign changes that strengthen a helical-field interpretation.

quotes.quote: 0735+178. An RM map made from the same visibility data was published by Gabuzda et al. (2008). Our analysis shows that the previously reported monotonic, transverse RM gradient across the jet has a significance of 5.5 .

Sign changes are observed in the transverse RM gradients detected in 0256+075, 0735+178, 0745+241 and 1652+398. This strengthens the case that these gradients are due to helical (or toroidal) magnetic fields associated with these jets, since a sign change cannot be caused by gradients in the electron density, and must be associated with a change in the direction of the line-of-sight magnetic field.

sources.summary: The proceedings identify PKS 0735+178 as a blazar near an IceCube alert, with a nearby Baikal-GVD event, doubled X-ray flux, and high multi-band state.

quotes.quote: Additionally, a group of researchers found that another blazar PKS 0735+178 is just outside the localization error of 13^ at 90 .l. of the golden alert IceCube-211208A on Dec. 21, 2021 of most probable estimated energy of 172 TeV . It was noted the coincidence that a Baikal-GVD event of most probable estimated energy of 43 TeV was detected about 4 hr after IceCube-211208A and inside the 5.5^ (50 .l. statistical error region) from . In , it is remarked that the source doubled its X-ray flux on Dec. 17, 2021, less than 5 10^3 s of the IceCube event, and was in a very high state in rays, UV and optical and other observations are quoted by Baksan and KM3NeT (and references therein). This flaring source is of the class considered in .

sources.summary: The paper includes 0735+178/PKS 0735+178 among BL Lac AGN with precession periods, notes its IceCube positional association and precession evidence, and lists it among known radio neutrino emitters.

quotes.quote: These AGN, which are classified as BL Lac object (0735+178, 2200 + 420, PG1553 + 113, OJ 287, TXS 0506+056), Quasar (3C 273, 3C 279, 3C 345, 1308+326, PKS 1502 + 106), Seyfert 1 galaxy (3C 120), and radio galaxy (3C 84), have estimated precession periods of the order of 1 to 40 years (as deduced from the literature).

PKS 0735+178 is located just outside the 90 point-spread function containment of the IceCube event, within the expected systematic uncertainty of the determination of the arrival direction. For PKS 0735+178, evidence for precession has been presented in Britzen2010b .
The three known radio sources (TXS 0506+056, PKS 1502+106, PKS 0735+178) have been identified by IceCube as neutrino emitters.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 0735+178 as a BL Lac at z=0.45 near IC211208A, detected at six times its 4FGL flux before the neutrino and coincident with additional neutrino events.

quotes.quote: This was the source 4FGL J0738.1+1742, associated with the BL Lac object PKS 0735+178 at z = 0.45 2012A&A...547A...1N . We detected the source with an average flux of F = (3 1) 10^ 7 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 ( 6 times the average 4FGL value), integrated over the 24 hours before the neutrino detection 2021ATel15099....1G .
The event was detected 3.95 hours after the IceCube event and is consistent with the position of the blazar PKS 0735+178 and that of IC211208A.

A third neutrino event coincident with PKS 0735+178 and the other two high-energy neutrinos was detected on 4 December 2021 by the Baksan Underground Scintillation Telescope (BUST), 4 days before the IceCube event.

sources.summary: For PKS 0735+178, the paper found no obvious global color-magnitude correlation, while noting previous hints and one-magnitude optical flux variations.

quotes.quote: PKS 0735+178: The source was observed during 104 nights in 2008--2012. No obvious global CM correlations found; previously, some hints for behavior of the blazar were reported by . The observed flux variations covered the 1 mag range, and the optical slope varied between _ BR 1 and 1.5.

sources.summary: PKS 0735+178 is presented as an HSP blazar/neutrino-source example, with jet photo-meson interactions and recent multi-messenger study alongside TXS 0506+056.

quotes.quote: Looking at the photo-meson production for HSP as state above, such particle interactions within the jets of highly energetic sources like TXS 0506+056 (4FGL J0509.4+0542) and PKS 0735+178 (4FGL J0738.1 + 1742) prince2023 is a testament of the dynamic multi-messenger and multi-physics aspect of sources that feature extremely accelerated ejecta.

The flaring and variability of the blazar spectra listed in the MOJAVE-FERMI catalogue, the Fermi LAT catalogues, and various others that feature high-energy ray emission from blazars residing in their active phases are an important aspect to identifying the neutrino production from such sources (e.g. TXS 0506+056 (4FGL J0509.4+0542) and PKS 0735+178 (4FGL J0738.1 + 1742)).
Blazars, such as TXS 0506+056 (4FGL J0509.4+0542) and PKS 0735+178 (4FGL J0738.1 + 1742), have been extensively studied in recent years icecube.txs0506.2018,padovani2015,prince2023 .

gmw_00001122.name: PKS 0823 223
gmw_00001122.overview: PKS 0823 223 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:astro-ph/0107017, arXiv:2202.07353] classified as an intermediate-synchrotron-peaked blazar [arXiv:2202.07353]. The source has shown variable linear polarization [arXiv:astro-ph/0107017] but exhibited circular polarization below an upper limit of 0.5 percent [arXiv:astro-ph/0107017] and no intranight optical variability during monitoring in March 2019 [arXiv:2202.07353].

sources.summary: The paper says PKS 0823 223 was more polarized than in past observations, showed position-angle rotation between two observations, and had no significant circular polarization.

quotes.quote: Apart from PKS 0823 223 (more polarized than observed in the past), the sources show levels of flux and polarization consistent with results at previous epochs. PKS 0823 223 was observed twice and found to show different levels of polarization in December 10 and 14. While P was basically constant, PA rotated by about 3 ^ -8 ^ in the different filters.
No significant circular polarization was found for the two other observed BL Lacs. Upper limits can be fixed at 2.5 and 0.5 for 1Jy 0138 097 and PKS 0823 223, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper presents first INOV monitoring for this ISP BL Lac and reports that the source was non-variable on all three March 2019 nights.

quotes.quote: 3FGL J0825.9 2230: This source is an ISP BL Lac and has never been studied for INOV before. It was observed for INOV on three nights during March 2019. It was found not to show INOV on all the three nights.

gmw_00001123.name: KISSR 1494
gmw_00001123.overview: KISSR 1494 is a Seyfert 2 galaxy that hosts an active galactic nucleus with double-peaked optical emission lines [arXiv:1708.01185, arXiv:1412.0400]. High-resolution radio observations reveal a single parsec-scale component with a steep spectral index [arXiv:1708.01185, arXiv:1508.07429], which is interpreted as the base of a synchrotron-emitting coronal wind [arXiv:1508.07429, arXiv:1807.00979].

sources.summary: The paper describes KISSR 1494 as a Seyfert 2 double-peaked AGN candidate; VLBA data detect one steep-spectrum radio component, interpreted more likely as a coronal wind.

quotes.quote: KISSR 1494 This galaxy hosts a Seyfert 2 nucleus which shows double peaked emission lines in its Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) spectrum (for details and the spectrum of KISSR 1494 see Kharb et al. 2015). The H and H lines in KISSR 1494 have equal peaks; such DPAGN are often referred to as an EPAGN. The equal peaks could be due to a nuclear disk or two AGN (Smith et al. 2012). We conducted high resolution, Very Long Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) radio observations of KISSR 1494 using the Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) at the dual frequencies of 1.6 GHz and 5 GHz in August, 2013.

We detected a single radio component at 1.6 GHz but nothing at 5 GHz, which suggests that the radio emission has a steep spectral index (S _ ^ ) and < -1.5 . The radio emission detected at 1.6 GHz has a size of 7.5 5 milliarcseconds or 8 6 pc at a galactic distance of 250 Mpc. The emission has an integrated flux of 650 Jy. Although it is possible that there are two SMBHs in the nucleus of KISS 1494 and only one has been detected in our observations, a more likely interpretation of the radio emission is that it represents a coronal wind arising from the magnetized corona above the accretion disk or from the inner edge of the accretion disk or torus of the AGN.

sources.summary: The paper presents VLBI observations of KISSR 1494, finding one steep-spectrum parsec-scale radio component, AGN-like brightness temperature, double-peaked emission lines, and circumstantial support for a coronal wind.

quotes.quote: We present here the results from dual-frequency phase-referenced VLBI observations of the Seyfert galaxy KISSR 1494, which exhibits double peaked emission lines in its SDSS spectrum. We detect a single radio component at 1.6 GHz, but not at 5 GHz implying a spectral index steeper than 1.5 0.5 ( S_ ^ ). The high brightness temperature of the radio component ( 1.4 10^7 K) and the steep radio spectrum support a non-thermal synchrotron origin. A crude estimate of the black hole mass derived from the M_ BH _ relation is 1.4 1.0 10^8 M_ ; it is accreting at an Eddington rate of 0.02 . The radio data are consistent with either the radio emission coming from the parsec-scale base of a synchrotron wind originating in the magnetised corona above the accretion disk, or from the inner ionised edge of the accretion disk or torus.

While with the present data, it is not possible to clearly distinguish between these scenarios, there appears to be greater circumstantial evidence supporting the coronal wind picture in KISSR 1494. From the kiloparsec-scale radio emission, the time-averaged kinetic power of this outflow is estimated to be Q 1.5 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 , which is typical of radio outflows in low-luminosity AGN.

sources.summary: The paper reports a single parsec-scale VLBI radio core in KISSR 1494, interpreting its steep nonthermal spectrum as an outflowing coronal wind rather than a binary black hole.

quotes.quote: KISSR 1494: VLBI observations of the Seyfert galaxy, KISSR 1494 ( z=0.0574 ), revealed a single radio core of size 7.5 5  milliarcsecond (= 8 6 parsec) with a flux density of 650 Jy at 1.6 GHz Kharb15 . There was no core detection at 5 GHz. The core brightness temperature ( T_b ) of 1.4 10^7 K was too high to be from a nuclear starburst which typically have T_b<10^5 K, while the 1.6-5 GHz radio spectral index of 1.5 0.5 was too steep to be from thermal free-free emission.

The steep radio spectrum was also inconsistent with the KISSR 1494 core being the unresolved base of a relativistic jet, which is expected to have a flat or inverted spectrum. We therefore concluded in Kharb15 that the radio emission was non-thermal synchrotron in origin that was likely coming from the base of an extended outflowing coronal wind. The NLR clouds were probably being pushed out by this broad nuclear outflow, giving rise to the double peaks in the optical spectrum.

sources.summary: KISSR 1494 is described as a Seyfert double-peaked AGN with a weak, steep-spectrum parsec-scale radio core, interpreted not as a normal compact jet but as a synchrotron coronal wind.

quotes.quote: Dual frequency VLBA observations have detected a single weak 0.7 mJy radio core of size ( 8 6 pc) in the Seyfert DPAGN KISSR 1494 Kharb15b . The brightness temperature of this core (T _b 1.4 10^7 K) and steep radio spectrum ( 1.5 0.5 ; S_ ^ ), are consistent with optically thin synchrotron emission. However, the core is not ``compact'' and its brightness is not centrally concentrated; it gets resolved out in images with different weighting schemes. And yet it does not resemble a typical parsec-scale jet component. We have suggested this component to be the base of a tenuous synchrotron-emitting coronal wind.

sources.summary: The paper says KISSR 1494 has the sample’s highest jet kinetic power but no detected VLBA jet, with a weak steep-spectrum component interpreted as a coronal-wind base.

quotes.quote: We find instead that the source with the highest Q_ jet value ( =1.3 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 ), viz., KISSR 1494, did not exhibit a jet in its VLBA image and was a point source in its 5 VLA FIRST image as well.

The dusty AGN photoionization model fits the data well also for the Seyfert galaxy, KISSR 1494, while the shock+precursor model does not Kharb15b . This source does not reveal a jet in VLBA images; rather a weak steep-spectrum radio component detected here is suggested to be the base of a coronal wind.

gmw_00001124.name: GHZ2/GL-z13
gmw_00001124.overview: GHZ2/GL-z13 is a compact, star-forming galaxy spectroscopically confirmed at a redshift of approximately 12.3 [arXiv:2403.10491, arXiv:2403.12683]. It exhibits extreme ionization conditions, low metallicity, and a very steep UV slope, which may be powered by young stellar populations or an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2207.09436, arXiv:2403.12683].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GHZ2 as a robust bright z about 12.2 JWST candidate, compact and star-forming, with a steep UV slope suggesting very young, low-metallicity stars.

quotes.quote: We highlight the detection of the two bright sources GHZ1 and GHZ2, at z 10.6 and z 12.2 , respectively. These objects are located in the color-color diagram fairly away from color selection borders, and have no significant low-redshift solution. Their P(z) is single peaked, the 68 confidence level photometric redshift range being z_ zphot = 9.68-11.15 ( z_ EAzY = 10.28-10.80) for GHZ1 and z_ zphot = 12.03-12.58 ( z_ EAzY = 11.85-12.39) for GHZ2. They are among the most robust candidates at z>9 ever detected, as demonstrated by the several recent works that have presented their selection with redshift and properties comparable to those discussed here Donnan2022,Harikane2022b,Naidu2022b . They are remarkably bright (M _ UV 21) and star-forming (SFR 20 M_ /yr).

GHZ2 is compact with R _e =120 10 pc Yang2022b and it has a very steep UV slope =-3.0. This extreme value is partly driven by the F200W band, as the slope value is =-2.4 0.2 when using the F277W and F356W bands only. These UV slope estimates are anyway indicative of very young ages and very low metallicity, possibly pristine, stellar populations which will need to be confirmed through JWST spectroscopy.

sources.summary: The paper describes GLz12 as a bright z = 12.2 GLASS galaxy whose luminosity challenges the fiducial model, yielding only one analogue under the adopted photometry.
