quotes.quote: GLz12 Naidu2022ApJ...940L..14N and Maisie's Galaxy Finkelstein2022ApJ...940L..55F are two bright galaxies at z 12 . However, observing them in these small-volume ERS programs indicates a surprisingly large number density for high-redshift bright galaxies -- GLz12 sets the number density for galaxies of M_ UV 21 at 10^ 5 Mpc ^ 3 mag ^ 1 together with another z 10 candidate reported in GLASS Naidu2022ApJ...940L..14N ; while Maisie's Galaxy suggests 2 10^ 5 Mpc ^ 3 mag ^ 1 at a luminosity 1 magnitude fainter.

This includes GLz12 from GLASS, a z = 12.2^ +0.1 _ 0.2 target that we discuss in this subsection. Its inferred properties include an average SFR of 10 M _ yr ^ 1 , a stellar mass around 10^ 9 M _ , an intrinsic UV magnitude of 21 , a low dust extinction of A_ v 0.3 and an effective radius around 0.5 pkpc (see also Ono2022arXiv220813582O ). This object was also identified by other independent groups Donnan2023MNRAS.518.6011D,Harikane2023ApJS..265....5H,Santini2023ApJ...942L..27S who inferred somewhat different physical properties.

We identify only one analogue, which is found at the nominated photometric redshift of GLz12 using easy Brammer2008ApJ...686.1503B , i.e. z = 12.2, with the same inferred intrinsic UV magnitudes of 21.0 mag. The top panel of Fig. fig:glf_z12_analogues_sed shows the spectrum and SED of this analogue. As with the two spectroscopically confirmed galaxies, it also shows the spectral features of typical star-forming galaxies at high redshift with a UV slope of 2 Bouwens2014ApJ...793..115B and negligible dust attenuation.

sources.summary: The paper confirms GHZ2/GLASS-z12 at z=12.33 with MIRI H and [OIII] detections, finding a bright, compact, young, metal-enriched galaxy with hard ionization and possible AGN contribution.

quotes.quote: These detections place the bright galaxy GHZ2/GLASS-z12 at z=12.33 0.02 , making it the most distant astronomical object with direct spectroscopic detection of these lines and the brightest confirmed object at this epoch. These observations provide key insights into the conditions of this primeval galaxy, which shows hard ionizing conditions rarely seen in the local Universe and likely driven by compact, young ( 30 Myr) star formation. Its oxygen-to-hydrogen abundance is close to a tenth of the solar value, indicating a rapid metal enrichment during the earliest phases of galaxy formation.

We target the galaxy GHZ2/GLASS-z12 ( =3.498985 , =-30.324767 ) with MIRI to blindly search for the brightest nebular emission lines: [ OIII ] 4959,5007 , H , H (with the doublets [ NII ] 6548,6583 \ and [ SII ] 6716,6731 \ also covered but expected to be too faint at high redshifts). The target stands out as the most robust among the extremely distant galaxy candidates found in one of the first sets of deep JWST NIRCam images, part of the GLASS-JWST Early Release Science Program , with a UV magnitude of M_ UV = -20.5  mag , and a photometric redshift of z 12.0-12.4 .

The physical conditions of GHZ2, revealed directly by the spectroscopy of the ionized gas, are extreme and rarely seen in the local Universe, with a low (but not pristine) metallicity, high ionization conditions, and high electron density. This emerging picture of compact galaxies with extreme conditions seems to be fairly common at high redshifts, particularly among the brightest systems, and might be associated with short bursts of young star formation with ages of a few tens of Myr and with the presence of massive, low-metallicity stars. Some of the properties of this early galaxy also resemble the observed features of AGN, particularly the hard ionizing spectra inferred from the observations presented here and from the JWST/NIRSPec data . It is thus possible that this source might be, at least partially, powered by an active black hole.

sources.summary: The paper confirms GHZ2/GLASS-z12 at z=12.34 and argues its extreme UV lines indicate a hard ionizing source, low metallicity, and dense star formation.

quotes.quote: We spectroscopically confirm the M_ UV = -20.5  mag galaxy GHZ2/GLASS-z12 to be at redshift z=12.34 . The source was selected via NIRCam photometry in GLASS-JWST Early Release Science data, providing the first evidence of a surprising abundance of bright galaxies at z 10 . The NIRSpec PRISM spectrum is remarkable and unlike any local analog. It shows significant detections of N IV, C IV, He II, O III, C III, O II, and Ne III lines, and the first detection in a high-redshift object of the O III Bowen fluorescence line at 3133  \ rest-frame.

GHZ2 displays UV lines with EWs that are only found in active galactic nuclei (AGNs) or composite objects at low/intermediate redshifts, and UV line-intensity ratios that are compatible both with AGNs and star formation in a low-metallicity environment. The nondetection of the very high-ionization lines [ Ne IV ] and [ Ne V ] , and the remarkable similarity between GHZ2 and other known C IV emitters, favors a scenario in which the high ionizing output is due to very low metallicity, massive stars forming in a dense environment. We estimate a metallicity 0.1   , a high ionization parameter log U > -2 , a N/O abundance 4--5 times the solar value, and a subsolar C/O ratio similar to the recently discovered class of nitrogen-enhanced objects at high redshift.

sources.summary: The paper presents GHZ2/GLASS-z13 as a robust z>10 candidate near z=12 and reports deep ALMA observations with no dust continuum or central bright emission line detection.

quotes.quote: The galaxy GHZ2/GLASS-z13 -- first reported by Castellano2022 and Naidu2022 and centered at RA = + 00:13:59.76 DEC = 30:19:29.1 -- stands out as one of the best candidates at z>10 ever detected. Its photometric redshift ( z=11.960-12.423 at a confidence limit of 68 ) has been confirmed by multiple independent teams Castellano2022,Donnan2022,Harikane2022,Naidu2022 , with negligible chances of being a lower-redshift contaminant due to its accurately measured colors and sharp break in the NIRCam photometry. The depth of this feature, associated with the Lyman break, means that the redshift identification is still robust after the recent in-flight re-calibration of the JWST instruments Rigby2022 . Here we present deep ALMA spectroscopic and continuum observations towards this galaxy.

Here we present deep spectroscopic and continuum ALMA observations towards GHZ2/GLASS-z13, one of the brightest and most robust candidates at z>10 , identified in the GLASS-JWST Early Release Science Program. While the lack of dust continuum detection supports its high-redshift nature by ruling out lower redshift dusty interlopers, we do not detect any bright emission line at the position of the target despite covering a total of 30 GHz and 98 of the source's redshift probability distribution ( z=11.9-13.5 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses GHZ2/GL-z13 as a z≈12 blue galaxy case where ALMA non-detection of dust continuum favors dust ejection over spatial segregation.

quotes.quote: GHZ2 ( z=12.35 ) shows a very blue slope = -3.00 0.12 Castellano22, Santini22 based on the updated photometry based on the latest NIRCam calibration files; this value is in contrast with the one initially inferred by for the same source (there referred to as GL-z13 as in Naidu22a ) = -2.4 0.13 . In the rest of the work we show the predictions obtained for both values of inferred for this source. Bakx22 and Popping22 performed deep spectroscopic and continuum ALMA observation of the z 12 galaxy GHZ2/GL-z13 Castellano22,Naidu22a , searching for line emission at rest-frame 88 m (see also Fujimoto22, Kaasinen22, Yoon22 ).

They fail to detect the 88 m dust continuum emission from this source, obtaining a 3 upper limit of <13.8 \ Jy . Assuming a dust temperature of 50 \ K , we can rescale this value to obtain an upper limit on the continuum flux at 158 \ m , F_ IR <2 \ Jy (open orange star in Fig. Fig:04 ). We interpret this result as an indication that this galaxy is dust-cleared by an outflow.

sources.summary: The paper includes GHZ1/GLz11 in joint ALMA-SED analysis, noting its early JWST identification, tentative ALMA signal, lack of robust dust continuum, and favored high-redshift solution.

quotes.quote: The non-detection of the dust continuum from \ is reminiscent of recent ALMA results in other three UV-bright galaxy candidates at z 11 -13: GHZ1/GLz11 , GHZ2/GLz13, and HD1 harikane2022b, bakx2022, popping2022, yoon2022, kaasinen2022, yoon2022 . GHZ1/GLz11 and GHZ2/GLz13 were also identified in the early \ data from the GLASS field treu2022 from different teams e.g., castellano2022, naidu2022, donnan2022, harikane2023 . No robust dust continuum is detected in follow-up deep 1-mm observations with a total of >10 hrs observing time for both candidates bakx2022, popping2022, yoon2022 , while a tentative ( 2.6 ) detection is reported in GHZ1/GLz11 yoon2022 .

To further investigate the high- z ( z 11 ) and the lower- z scenarios for all these candidates, we perform SED fitting to the optical--mm photometry using cigale burgarella2005, noll2009, boquien2019 .
Owing to our and recent deep ALMA observations, the high- z solutions at z 11 -17 are all favored in the UV bright high- z candidates of S5-z17-1, GHZ1/GLz11, GHZ2/GLz13, and HD1 (Section sec:sed ).

sources.summary: The paper treats GL-z12 as a compact high-redshift candidate AGN and argues a visible MBH would need to be overmassive for its low galaxy mass.

quotes.quote: Most of these candidate galaxies are presented as being dominated by star formation, without the presence of an Active Galactic Nucleus (AGN), with the exception of GL-z12, which is a candidate AGN 2022arXiv220813582O based on its compact size. The templates used for determining the physical properties, such as stellar mass, star formation rate (SFR) and age generally assume the absence of an AGN.

An example is GL-z12, where explore the possible presence of an AGN: in the first place, the galaxy mass and SFR have to be re-evaluated if the AGN produces a fraction of the light, and in the second place, they also concord with our suggestion that the MBH should have mass of the order of 10^6 to be visible, for a galaxy with mass 2 10^8 , i.e., the MBH would have to be `overmassive'.

Can these galaxies be progenitors of z=6 quasars? Let's take as an example GL-z10 and GL-z12 2022arXiv220709434N . The stellar masses are reported to be (M_ gal / )=9.6 and (M_ gal / )=9.1 respectively and the corresponding number density (given the survey volume) is 4.5 10^3 Gpc ^ 3 , which is about 3 orders of magnitude larger than the number density of luminous quasars at z 6-7 .

sources.summary: GHZ2/GLASS-z12 is presented as a z=12.34 galaxy with complete NIRSpec/MIRI coverage, strong CIV and many lines, extreme ionization, low metallicity, and possible ionizing-photon leakage.

quotes.quote: GHZ2/GLASS-z12, one of the most distant galaxies found in JWST observations, has been recently observed with both NIRSpec and MIRI spectrographs, establishing a spectroscopic redshift z_ spec =12.34 , and making it the first system at z>10 with complete spectroscopic coverage from rest-frame UV to optical wavelengths. This galaxy is identified as a strong CIV _ 1549 emitter (EW =46 ) with many other detected emission lines, such as NIV ] _ 1488 , HeII _ 1640 , OIII ] _ 1661,1666 , NIII ] _ 1750 , CIII ] _ 1907,1909 , [ OII ] _ 3726,3729 , [ NeIII ] _ 3869 , [ OIII ] _ 4959,5007 , and H , including a remarkable detection of the OIII Bowen fluorescence line at rest-frame =3133 .

Combining six optical strong-line diagnostics (namely R2, R3, R23, O32, Ne3O2, and Ne3O2Hd), we find extreme ionization conditions, with [ O iii ] _ 4959,5007 / [ O ii ] _ 3726,3729 =1.39 0.19 and [ Ne iii ] _ 3869 / [ O ii ] _ 3726, 3729 =0.37 0.18 in stark excess compared to typical values in the ISM at lower redshifts. These line properties are compatible either with an AGN or with a compact, very dense star-forming environment ( 10^2 10^3 /yr/kpc ^2 and 10^4 10^5 /pc ^2 ), with a high ionization parameter ( _ 10 (U) =-1.75 0.16 ), a high ionizing photon production efficiency ( _ ion ) = 25.7_ 0.1 ^ +0.2 , and a low, although not pristine, metal content ranging between 5 and 11 Z _ , indicating a rapid chemical enrichment of the ISM in the last few Myrs. These properties also suggest that a substantial amount of ionizing photons ( 10 ) are leaking outside of GHZ2 and starting to reionize the surrounding intergalactic medium, possibly due to strong radiation driven winds according to some recent models.

sources.summary: The paper identifies GL-z13 as a luminous JWST high-redshift candidate and compares it conservatively to lower-redshift TNG snapshots that still underpredict its stellar mass.

quotes.quote: Recently, Naidu_2022 reported the discovery of two luminous galaxy candidates labeled as GLASS-z11 (hereafter GL-z11) and GLASS-z13 (GL-z13) with _ 10 (M_ * /M_ ) = 9.4_ 0.3 ^ +0.3 and 9.0_ 0.4 ^ +0.3 located at photometric redshifts of z_ phot =10.9_ 0.4 ^ +0.5 and z_ phot = 13.1_ 0.7 ^ +0.8 , respectively (see their table 3).

GL-z11 at 10.9_ 0.4 ^ +0.5 , GL-z13 at 13.1_ 0.7 ^ +0.8 , and CEERS-1749 at 16.0_ 0.6 ^ +0.6 are compared with the simulations at z = 10.98 , 11.98 , and 14.99 , respectively. The comparison with GL-z13 and CEERS-1749 is therewith more conservative because snapshots corresponding to lower redshifts than observed are addressed allowing the galaxies to grow in stars for a longer time span than in the observed cases.

At z = 11.98 and z=10.98 , the maximum stellar masses of subhalos are _ 10 (M_ * /M_ ) = 8.07 and 8.43 , respectively, which is less massive than GL-z13 with _ 10 (M_ * /M_ ) = 9.0_ 0.4 ^ +0.3 and GL-z11 with _ 10 (M_ * /M_ ) = 9.4_ 0.3 ^ +0.3 .

sources.summary: The paper describes GHZ2 as a JWST high-redshift galaxy candidate with reported photometric redshift, SFR and stellar mass constraints, and only an ALMA upper limit on OIII luminosity.

quotes.quote: Among these galaxy candidates, GHZ2 has been independently detected by several groups Castellano22, Naidu22, Donnan22, Harikane22 which have reported a photometric redshift z=11.960-12.423 . Using re-calibrated JWST fluxes Bakx22 have constrained the star formation rate of GZH2 in the range SFR =20^ +15 _ 14 and stellar mass M_ = 1.2^ +7.2 _ 0.4 10^8 . Bakx22, Popping22 used ALMA Band 6 Director's Discretionary Time (DDT) program data to search for 88 m line emission. Their analysis has only provided a 5 upper limit of ( L_ OIII /L_ ) < 1.7 10^ 8 Bakx22 for the integrated line luminosity.

sources.summary: The paper presents GHZ2 as a robust z>10 candidate whose U-shaped SED enables unusually good physical characterization despite its very high redshift.

quotes.quote: We show in Fig. fig:seds a few examples of the SED and best-fit templates of our candidates: two z 7-8 candidates, one with high and a pronounced D4000 (ID1435) and another one with low and a D4000 smaller than 1 (ID4733), indicative of a spectrum dominated by nebular continuum, and the two most robust candidates at z>10 , namely GHZ1 and GHZ2 presented by . We note that two out of these four galaxies exhibit D4000 < 1, which is indicative of extremely young stellar populations. While the optical rest-frame is nicely sampled at z<10 - and also marginally at z 10 - at higher redshift we are limited to the spectral region blueward of the break. For these galaxies longer wavelength observations with MIRI will be a valuable addition.

Remarkably, GHZ2 shows a characteristic U-shape pattern carnall22 that allows us to nicely constrain its SED. Despite its extremely high redshift, its intrinsic luminosity allows for an accurate physical characterization.

gmw_00001125.name: NGC 4494
gmw_00001125.overview: NGC 4494 is an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:0908.3705, arXiv:1310.2607] hosting a kinematically distinct core [arXiv:0908.3705, arXiv:1712.05833], an inner dust ring [arXiv:1310.2607], and a faint central active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1804.10207]. Its outer halo exhibits a declining velocity dispersion [arXiv:1310.2607, arXiv:1212.2768] and a low dark matter fraction [arXiv:1605.06101, arXiv:1602.01105].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4494 as an elliptical galaxy with a central cusp and KDC whose sparse, high-CaT data do not constrain a reliable metallicity gradient.

quotes.quote: NGC 4494 NGC 4494 is classified as an elliptical galaxy located in the Coma I cloud (Forbes et al. 1996; Larsen et al. 2001). Its luminosity profile displays a central cusp (Lauer et al. 2007). It has a KDC (Bender et al. 1988), an intermediate central age of 6.7 Gyrs and a central metallicity of [ Fe/H ] +0.03 (Denic olo et al. 2005).

NGC 4494 Because shorter exposure times were used for NGC 4494, the number and spatial coverage of our measurements are much smaller than for the previous two galaxies. Despite the large scatter in our measured metallicity values, the agreement with the central estimate from Denic olo et al. (2005) is good. Interestingly, the data show no visible gradient in the raw CaT measurements (see Fig. fig:NGC4494 ) and the CaT values seem to scatter about the upper limit of the V03 models. Moreover, the metallicity ``gradient'' seen in Fig. fig:NGC4494 is fictitious and caused by our exclusion of the randomly scattered points above the CaT upper limit. Indeed, the majority of the measured metallicity points in NGC 4494 are very high (i.e., consistent with [ Fe/H ] 0.20 dex) within the galactocentric radius probed and an accurate metallicity gradient could not be measured.

sources.summary: The paper builds live N-body dynamical models of NGC 4494 to constrain its structure and halo mass, finding photometry and kinematics are best matched by a light halo model.

quotes.quote: We present dynamical models of NGC 4494, which we built using our iterative method presented in a previous paper. These models are live N body models consisting of equal mass particles, and they are steady state as confirmed by a fully self-consistent evolution. Our goals were twofold. The first one -- namely to test whether our iterative method could indeed be used to construct galactic models following given observational constraints, both photometric and kinematic -- was fully achieved. Our method allowed us to go beyond a simple spherical model and to make full sets of rotating, axisymmetric models without any limitations to the velocity distribution.

Our second goal was to understand better the structure of NGC 4494, and more specifically to set constraints on its halo mass. For this we tried three families of models: without halo, with a light halo and with a heavy halo, respectively. Our models reproduce well the photometry and the kinematics, the latter except specific regions where some non-equilibrium or non-axisymmetric structure could be present in the galaxy (e.g. the kinematically decoupled core). However, the lower order moments of the velocity distribution (up to and including the second order) do not allow us to discriminate between the three halos. On the other hand, when we extend the comparison to the higher order moments of the velocity distribution obtained from the long-slit data, we find that our light halo model fits the data better than the no halo, or the heavy halo models.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4494 as the strongest outlier in blue-GC and dark-matter trends, reviews conflicting dynamical mass results, and argues its dark-matter content is likely significant.

quotes.quote: NGC 4494 deviates most strongly from the general trend. Given that there appears to be a minimum, or floor, in the blue GC fraction of galaxies of f _ blue > 0.4, changes to the blue GC fraction will not place NGC 4494 on the general trend. So we turn our attention to its low dark matter fraction. Indeed we are not the first to find a low fraction of dark matter.

Several studies using PNe to model the mass of NGC 4494 have derived a remarkably low dark matter content of < 0.5 within 5 R _e (Romanowsky et al. 2003; Napolitano et al. 2009; Deason et al. 2012) However, the more recent NMAGIC modelling of the extended stellar kinematics (Foster et al. 2011) and the PNe data by Morganti et al. (2013) found a higher dark matter fraction of f _ DM = 0.6 0.1 within 5 R _e . They assumed a logarithmic potential for the dark matter rather than an NFW-type profile, which leads to a higher dark matter mass and a relatively low stellar mass-to-light ratio. The wide range of dark matter fractions in the literature suggest that NGC 4494 is difficult to model dynamically, and future mass modelling should attempt to incorporate stellar, PNe and GCs velocity information simultaneously.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4494 is a weak-field outlier with one of the worst model confidences, too-low reconstructed acceleration, overpredicted velocity dispersion, and low GC kurtosis suggesting systemic rotation.

quotes.quote: We can see that the models can describe all the galaxies successfully (the ^2 confidence is always higher than 5 ). The worst cases are NGC 1023 and NGC 4494 with a confidence of approximately 38 . These galaxies require a weaker gravitational field than expected by the priors, as follows from panels (b)-(e) of g1 and g14 .
The objects for which we obtained the reconstructed dynamical acceleration, a , that is too low , NGC 1023 and particularly NGC 4494, might be quickly rotating in the plane of the sky.

The predictions on velocity dispersion are usually too high ( pred ). This is also the case of the galaxies NGC 1023 and NGC 4494 for which we obtained the worst models ( confidence of around 38 for both). Statistical error can easily account for this confidence value. Nevertheless, the shape of the best-fit models for these galaxies seems to be too high for the outermost GCs (Figures fig:g1 and fig:g12 ). The reason for this discrepancy might be the rotation of the GC system in the plane of the sky so that the attractive force is overestimated. This suggestion is supported by the fact that NGC 1023 and NGC 4494 have very low values of the s_4 parameter among the real galaxies in our sample ( tabgcss ) and as we showed in gcss , this suggests a systemic rotation of the GC system.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4494 as a nearby elliptical test case, deriving its smoothed mass distribution and finding it similar to a published radial mass profile inside 3 effective radii.

quotes.quote: NGC 4494 NGC 4494 is a nearby elliptical, the mass distribution of which to 7 effective radii has recently been presented by . The distance to this galaxy is given as 15.8 Mpc by . The V band photometry of this galaxy is presented in Table A1 of . This surface brightness profile indicates an X_e of 3.37 kpc and is deprojected, given the radial variation of the projected eccentricity (assuming an oblate geometry and edge-on viewing).

These are used to characterise the raw M/L , which when smoothed, gives rise to the total mass distribution that is shown in the middle panel of Figure fig:4494 . The total M/L ratio of NGC 4494, in the V band is shown on the right panel of Figure fig:4494 while the enclosed gravitational mass distribution is depicted in the middle panel. This is very similar to the radial mass distribution of NGC 4494, within the inner 3 X_e , as reported by (their Figure 13).

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4494 as an ordinary elliptical/S0 candidate with extended photometric, chemical, kinematic, globular-cluster and UCD measurements, and links many properties to a gas-rich major merger.

quotes.quote: We present a comprehensive analysis of the spatial, kinematic, and chemical properties of stars and globular clusters (GCs) in the `ordinary' elliptical galaxy NGC 4494 using data from the Keck and Subaru telescopes. We derive galaxy surface brightness and colour profiles out to large galactocentric radii. We compare the latter to metallicities derived using the near-infrared Calcium Triplet. We obtain stellar kinematics out to 3.5 effective radii. The latter appear flattened or elongated beyond 1.8 effective radii in contrast to the relatively round photometric isophotes.
In fact, NGC 4494 may be a flattened galaxy, possibly even an S0, seen at an inclination of 45 degrees.

Using model predictions of galaxy formation, and a suite of merger simulations, we find that many of the observational properties of NGC 4494 may be explained by formation in a relatively recent gas-rich major merger.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4494's globular-cluster metallicity distribution as likely trimodal, with well separated GMM peaks and supporting kinematic evidence for three GC subpopulations.

quotes.quote: Since the metallicity distribution of NGC 4494 appears trimodal (Figure fig:ngc4494tri ) we used GMM to test where the metallicity distribution is trimodal. We found peaks at [ Z/H ] = -1.73 0.19 , 0.69 0.14 and 0.26 0.11 , widths of 0.17 0.12 , 0.38 0.12 and 0.19 0.07 and 10.5 5.8 , 29.5 7.7 and 13.0 5.0 GCs in each peak. GMM gives a probability of 0.895 that a trimodal distribution is preferred over a unimodal distribution, a higher probability than for bimodal over unimodal. The peaks are well separated ( D = 3.55 and D = 2.32 ) and the kurtosis is negative k = -1.08 supporting the existence of trimodality. The bimodal and trimodal GMM fits are plotted with the NGC 4494 metallicity histogram in Figure fig:ngc4494tri .

2011MNRAS.415.3393F found evidence for three kinematic subpopulations in that although the bluest GCs and the reddest GCs in NGC 4494 show evidence for rotation, the intermediate colour GCs do not rotate. Taken together these lines of evidence suggest, but do not prove, that NGC 4494 has three GC subpopulations.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 4494 as an intermediate-luminosity elliptical whose comprehensive dynamical analysis permits some CVC uncertainty but still requires dark matter in its halo.

quotes.quote: Mendez+01 first showed that the steeply decreasing outer velocity dispersion profile of the intermediate-luminosity elliptical (ILE) galaxy NGC 4697 could be matched well with an isotropic Hernquist model. They thus concluded that no evidence for dark matter had been found out to 3R_e , but that dark matter could be present if the velocity distribution is anisotropic. Romanowsky+03 drew attention to three further ILEs with steeply decreasing _p(R) , NGC 821, 3379, and 4494, suggesting the presence of little if any dark matter in their halos. However, the well-known mass-anisotropy degeneracy Binney+Mamon82 is much stronger in galaxies such as these ILEs, with de Vaucouleurs type luminosity profiles and steeply decreasing _p profiles, than in galaxies with either more shallow luminosity profiles or radially constant projected velocity dispersions Gerhard93 . With NMAGIC particle models see deLorenzi+07 based on a variety of kinematic data, including several hundred PN line-of-sight velocities, for NGC 4697 deLorenzi+08 , for NGC 3379 deLorenzi+09 and for NGC 4494 Morganti+12 determined the range of quasi-isothermal halo potentials consistent with the data for these galaxies.

Fig. 1c shows the 70 confidence boundaries from the PN likelihood for NGC 3379 and 4697, and from a more comprehensive analysis for NGC 4494. While some spread in the allowed CVCs and anisotropies remains, as expected, dark matter is required in all 3 galaxies. Fig. 1c indicates that the most favoured CVCs may be slightly falling, as in the lower-mass systems in Wu+12 . Relative to the outer CVCs, the baryonic centers of these galaxies are quite centrally concentrated, as could be expected in gas-rich mergers.

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes NGC 4494 with RGGR, testing Sersic-based baryonic modelling, anisotropy, stellar mass, and the running of G within the elliptical galaxy.

quotes.quote: NGC 4494 is an ordinary elliptical recently analysed within RGGR , and which has received considerable attention previously, in particular due to its apparent lack of dark matter . Here we present a variation of the analysis presented in that Ref. , namely we do not directly use the photometric data with the S ersic extension to model the stellar mass, instead only the S ersic profile that best fit the surface brightness of this galaxy is used (an approach here labeled ``Full S ersic''). In part this is relevant to evaluate to what extent our results presented in are robust to small changes on the baryonic mass content. The main results are i) within the 1 error bars, the results are compatible; ii) the best fit value for the anisotropy parameter considerably moves towards radial anisotropy (from about isotropy to 0.44 ) (see further comments in the Conclusions).

The running of G and inside NGC 4494 From the NGC 4494 results above, here the corresponding running of G and inside this galaxy are shown. The galaxy that was in worst agreement with such correlation was NGC 4494, since it displayed a too low value for its mass (considering the solutions presented in Ref. ). Here it is commented that with a small change on the modelling of the baryonic mass (i.e., modelling its mass exclusively from its S ersic profile, which is well inside the errors on the conversion from light to mass), a greater tendency towards higher radial anisotropy, lower mass and higher was found (model with free constant anisotropy).

sources.summary: The paper constructs dynamical models of elliptical galaxy NGC 4494, finding a flat circular-velocity curve, radial anisotropy, high inner dark-matter fraction, and central baryon concentration.

quotes.quote: NGC 4494 is one of several intermediate-luminosity elliptical galaxies inferred to have an unusually diffuse dark matter halo. We use the ^2 made-to-measure particle code NMAGIC to construct axisymmetric models of NGC 4494 from photometric and various kinematic data. The extended kinematics include light spectra in multiple slitlets out to 3.5 R_ e , and hundreds of planetary nebulae velocities out to 7 R_ e , thus allowing us to probe the dark matter content and orbital structure in the halo.

The best-fitting models for NGC 4494 have a dark matter fraction of about 0.6 0.1 at 5R_ e (70 C.L.), and are embedded in a dark matter halo with circular velocity 200 km s ^ 1 . The total circular velocity curve (CVC) is approximately flat at v_ =220 km s ^ 1 outside 0.5R_ e . The orbital anisotropy of the stars is moderately radial.
NGC 4494 shows a particularly high dark matter fraction inside 3R_e , and a strong concentration of baryons in the center.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 4494 as an ordinary elliptical with a curiously low dark matter fraction and abnormally low dark matter concentration compared with simulation predictions.

quotes.quote: Our dark matter fractions are in good agreement with previous studies which find f_ DM (<5R_ eff ) 0.4-0.5 for ordinary ellipticals (e.g. NGC 4494, NGC 3379, NGC 4697 see Figure 12 in napolitano11 ) and f_ DM (<5R_ eff ) 0.8-0.9 for group or cluster central ellipticals (e.g. NGC 1399, NGC 1407, NGC 4486, NGC 4648, NGC 5846 see Figure 8 in das10 ).

The red square highlighted is NGC 4494, which has a curiously low dark matter fraction. studied this system in detail and found an abnormally low dark matter concentration and also derive a similar dark matter fraction within 5 R_ eff . The remaining low mass galaxies have dark matter fractions consistent with the model predictions (at least for a Chabrier/KTG IMF). While the case of NGC 4494 is in contradiction to the predictions from simulations, the general trend suggests that the dark matter properties of lower mass galaxies are not very different from those of higher masses.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC4494 as an ordinary but X-ray-faint elliptical with a dust disk, possible gas loss, low gas metallicity, and slight 250 micron extension.

quotes.quote: NGC4494 is often described as an `ordinary elliptical' (Capaccioli et al. 1992). O'Sullivan Ponman \ (2004) found that its X-ray luminosity was two order of magnitudes lower than expected for its optical luminosity and there are signs of a small dust disk in absorption (Tran et al. 2001). X-ray faint galaxies such as NGC4494 may arise due to losing their hot X-ray gas in outflows. Further evidence of interactions with nearby galaxies or mergers is the low metallicity estimated from the X-ray gas ( <0.1Z_ , O'Sullivan Ponman \ 2004), and this may indicate dilution of interstellar material via an infall of unenriched (cold) material. The galaxy is slightly extended at 250 m.

sources.summary: NGC 4494 is described as an elliptical with an inner dust ring, kinematically distinct core, sustained outer rotation, position-angle twist, and mildly declining dispersion.

quotes.quote: This ``ordinary'' elliptical (E1--2) galaxy contains an inner dust ring Lauer:2005bk . The central regions contain a kinematically distinct core Bender:1994vo and double rotation amplitude maxima Krajnovic:2011jj . Our map shows that rotation is sustained in the outskirts, along with a 10 deg twist in the kinematic position angle around 1.5 \ Proctor:2009iy . In agreement with our velocity dispersion map, Napo:2009a found a radially mild decrease in velocity dispersion using combined PN and long-slit data. The GC kinematics were studied in Foster:2011hh , who found significant rotation for the metal-poor GCs consistent with the stellar motions, but little to no rotation in the metal-rich GCs.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 4494 as difficult to model and dark-matter poor in its halo, with inferred fractions depending on the assumed gravitational potential.

quotes.quote: One of the intermediate mass galaxies studied here, NGC 4494, has been notoriously difficult to model in the literature, with results ranging from a low DM content Romanowsky_2003, Napolitano_2009, Deason_2012 to a high DM content Morganti_2013 . Here, using GC kinematic data that extend far out into the halo, we find that NGC 4494 is DM poor, i.e., f_ DM 0.5 at R_ 9 \ regardless of the adopted stellar M/L_ K and the GC orbital anisotropy when , the slope of the gravitational potential, is assumed to be 0.2. Our R_ for NGC 4494 is close to the scale radius of the NFW DM halo, where the mass distribution is expected to be DM dominated, such that in a typical 10^ 13 halo, f_ DM 0.9 at the scale radius. However, when 0 in equation eq:def_alpha , we obtain f_ DM 0.3-0.6 within 5  and f_ DM 0.5-0.7 within R_ , for varying stellar M/L_ K . This is similar to the result from Morganti_2013 , obtained also by assuming a logarithmic DM halo.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4494 as a core-less early-type comparison galaxy, modeling its edge-on dust torus and finding an inner surface-brightness excess likely caused by a faint LLAGN.

quotes.quote: ID & Reference & [ hh:mm:ss ] & [ dd:mm:ss ] & [ Mpc ] & & [ s ] & & 1 (1) & (2) & (3) & (4) & (5) & (6) & (7) & (8) NGC 4552 & 12:35:39.8 & +12:33:23 & 16.0 & ACS/ F475W & 750 & 9401 & NGC 4494 & 12:31:24.1 & +25:46:31 & 13.7 & WFPC2/ F555W & 1000 & 5454 & The test galaxies for our study are NGC 4552 (M 89, UGC 7760, VCC 1632; Virgo) and NGC 4494 (UGC 7662; Coma). Deep images clearly revealed that these objects host central disk- or ring-like dust structures (see Figure figure:fit_models ). In particular, NGC 4552 hosts a quasi face-on disk surrounded by several dust streams which spread radially beyond the effective radius of the galaxy ferrarese:2006 . The structure in NGC 4494, first observed by , resembles instead a more edge-on ring, or better a torus with a large inner hole and a small opening angle.

For NGC 4494, the best-fit model selected by LOTR (including exclusively stellar and dust components) underestimates the innermost ( R < 0.2 ) surface brightness by 0.3 mag. This discrepancy is most probably due to the presence of a faint central point source, which we interpret as a LLAGN (Section Dust pretending to be a core or an AGN ).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4494 as one of three intermediate-luminosity ellipticals whose PN.S planetary-nebula velocities reach 4--6 effective radii and show declining outer velocity dispersion inconsistent with simple dark-halo models.

quotes.quote: Observations with the PN.S on the 4.2-m William Herschel Telescope have allowed us to extend stellar kinematic studies to the outer parts of three intermediate-luminosity elliptical galaxies, NGC 821, NGC 3379, and NGC 4494 (Table 1). In each of these systems, we have measured 100 PN velocities with uncertainties of 20 km s ^ 1 out to radii of 4--6 R_ eff (Figs. 1--3). The line-of-sight velocities in the outer parts of all these galaxies show a clear decline in dispersion with radius (Fig. 4). A decrease in random velocities with radius has been indicated by small samples of PNe around NGC 3379 , but the more extensive data set presented here provides a more definitive measurement of this decline, and reveals that it also occurs in other similar galaxies. The new data are inconsistent with simple dark halo models (Fig. 4) and thus different from kinematical results for brighter ellipticals [ e.g., ] .

sources.summary: NGC 4494 is one of the elliptical galaxies modeled with a modified potential; its decreasing dispersion profile had previously been fit with an intermediate-mass dark halo and radial anisotropy.

quotes.quote: We will use the inner long slit data and the extended PN kinematics for three galaxies which have published dynamical analyses within DM halo framework: NGC 3379 ( 2007ApJ...664..257D ; 2009MNRAS.395...76D , DL+09 hereafter), NGC 4494 ( nap+09 , N+09), NGC 4374 (N+11). The decreasing velocity dispersion profiles of the first two galaxies have been modeled with an intermediate mass halo, M_ vir 12-12.2 M_ , with concentration c_ vir =6-8 and a fair amount of radial anisotropy in the outer regions.
Before we go on with detailed stellar dynamics, we show in Fig. fig:fig0 the circular velocity of the modified potential as a function of the potential parameters L and for NGC 4494 and NGC 4374.
We have found that the modified potentials allow to nicely model the three galaxies with a distribution of the parameters which turned out to be inconsistent with the results found in spiral systems.

sources.summary: NGC 4494 is one of the Romanowsky elliptical galaxies revisited with updated PNe data; MOND fits are consistent with prior work and dark-halo mass-to-light ratios are low.

quotes.quote: Surprisingly, little dark matter were found contained in the three luminous elliptical galaxies (NGC 821, NGC 3379, NGC 4494) and the data is close to the model by Newtonian dynamics. The ``lack of dark matter'' can be explained by another view of the ``missing mass problem''. The crux of the matter is ``mismatch in acceleration''. This can be explained by unseen mass or modified theory of gravity. Milgrom&Sanders03 showed that those luminous elliptical galaxies reported by Romanowsky03 can be well explained by Modified Newtonian Dynamics (MOND).
We also obtain the velocity dispersion of NGC 4494 and NGC 821 by the same procedure.
In Table tab:masstolight , one notices that the mass-to-light ratios of NGC 3379, NGC 4374, NGC 1344 and NGC 4494 are too small for common dark matter model.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 4494 kinematics, describing its rotation, velocity dispersion, halo behavior, and relevant photometric or interaction features in the early-type-galaxy survey.

quotes.quote: This is an E1 galaxy in the NGC 4565 group. The velocity map from 2011MNRAS.414.2923K shows a kinematically distinct core. The galaxy rotates almost constantly at 60 up to 2 2009MNRAS.393..329N,2016MNRAS.457..147F . In the halo we find that this amplitude decreases with radius.

sources.summary: The paper reports six central-kiloparsec point sources in NGC 4494, with a central hard source that could be a radio-loud AGN dominating hard X-rays.

quotes.quote: NGC 4494 (L2, E1-2) has six point sources in its central kiloparsec and the coordinates of the brightest source agree with the center of the galaxy within 2 . This source has a hard X-ray spectrum, which is best fitted by a two-temperature plasma model or a power-law. The luminosity is L_ 0.5-10 keV =1.6 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 . There is no detection of a source at 5 GHz, with an upper limit of 0.5 mJy WH91 , leading to R_ X < -3.7 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4494 as a declining-velocity case with very low inferred halo density; its fits resemble NGC 3379, with dark matter adding no improvement.

quotes.quote: NGC 4494 This is another of the intriguing cases where romanowsky2003 found a declining velocity profile implying a dark matter halo with a very low density napolitano2009 . From data on both axes of coccato2009 , the fitting characteristics resemble those of NGC 3379: dark matter brings no improvement. A halo is allowed if its outer radius far exceeds the observed region.

sources.summary: The paper jointly fits NGC 4494 Chandra and XMM-Newton data, finding variable normalization and soft/hard flux changes over four months.

quotes.quote: NGC 4494 : The simultaneous fit is jointly performed for Chandra and XMM -Newton data (the contribution of the annular region is 21 in Chandra data) up to 4.5 keV, because Chandra data show a low count-rate at harder energies. We use SMF1, obtaining the best representation of the data set when Norm varies (33 ). Flux variation of 31 (35 ) is obtained for the soft (hard) energy in four months period.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4494 globular clusters have stronger red-color CaT values than predicted, a radically different color-CaT relation from NGC 1407, and bluer high-CaT colors.

quotes.quote: At blue colours the GCs of NGC 1407 and NGC 4278 have weaker CaT values than predicted by Equation eq:CaT_colour . For red colours, NGC 1407, NGC 4278, NGC 4365 and NGC 4649 GCs have weaker CaT strengths than predicted from their colours while NGC 3115, NGC 3377, NGC 4473 and NGC 4494 have stronger than predicted CaT values. The shapes of the colour--CaT relations of NGC 1407 and NGC 4494 are radically different as first noticed by 2011MNRAS.415.3393F . As seen in Figure fig:stacking_CaT1 , at low CaT values NGC 3115, NGC 3377 and NGC 4473 show bluer colours compared to the other galaxies while at high CaT values NGC 3377 and NGC 4494 also show bluer colours than the other galaxies. Additionally, at low CaT values NGC 3115, NGC 3377 and NGC 4473 show higher Na82 strengths than the other galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4494 as a representative early-type galaxy, modeling its globular-cluster system to infer halo mass, concentration, circular velocity, and consistency with observed profiles.

quotes.quote: Here we provide an overview of our method with the guidance of Fig. fig:showcase_ngc4494 , which illustrates the input, fitting, and output of our model for a representative galaxy, NGC 4494.
As an example, in Fig. fig:showcase_ngc4494 a, we show the distribution of the confirmed SLUGGS GCs around NGC 4494.
As an example, in Fig. fig:showcase_ngc4494 , we show the marginalised posterior distributions for the halo mass and concentration for the galaxy NGC 4494.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4494 as a Coma I cloud elliptical with a kinematically distinct core, low specific frequency from HST data, and revised globular-cluster color peaks.

quotes.quote: NGC 4494 Located in the Coma I cloud, this elliptical contains a kinematically distinct core ben88 . From HST data, forb96 suggested a low specific frequency of S _N 2. The same HST data suggested mean color peaks around = 0.95 and 1.15, here we find 0.92 and 1.12.

sources.summary: The paper reports preliminary PNe kinematics for NGC 4494, describing a possible major-axis tilt, a nearly face-on view, and a rotation curve that drops at large radius.

quotes.quote: Preliminary results on PNe radial velocity fields of NGC 3379 and NGC 4494 from the Planetary Nebula Spectrograph, (PN.S, Douglas et al. 2002), have revealed that there is a tilt of the major kinematical axis of the PNe sample with respect to the stellar long-slit data as it is clearly shown in Fig. 1. Incidentally, the latter are viewed quite edge-on, while NGC 3379 and NGC 4494 are possibly nearly face-on. Furthermore, at least for the four systems mentioned above, the rotation velocity curves increase in the inner parts (up to 1 R_ e ) and rapidly drop in the outer parts down to zero at the outermost points.

gmw_00001126.name: NGC 625
gmw_00001126.overview: NGC 625 is a dwarf starburst galaxy in the Sculptor group [arXiv:1409.1239, arXiv:2305.03443] that features a kinematically disturbed disk [arXiv:1404.6252, arXiv:2305.03443] and a 2 kpc northwestern neutral hydrogen tail [arXiv:1409.1239]. The galaxy is undergoing the end of an outflow phase [arXiv:1705.04692] and contains discrete molecular gas clouds [arXiv:2002.09498].

sources.summary: The study classifies NGC 625 as kinematically disturbed, with an inner rotating disk but ambiguous evidence between interaction/merger and outflow interpretations.

quotes.quote: Seven galaxies in our sample have a kinematically disturbed disk: NGC 625, NGC 1569, NGC 4163, NGC 4449, NGC 5253, UGC 6456, and UGC 9128. For these objects, we used channels maps and PV-diagrams to estimate ( _ kin , _ kin ) , V_ sys , PA _ kin , and the rotation velocity in the outer parts. For the inclination, we assumed the optical value.

NGC 625 has been previously studied by Cote2000 and Cannon2004 . Cote2000 suggested that the complex kinematics is due to an interaction/merger, whereas Cannon2004 argued that it is best described by a gaseous outflow superimposed on a rotating disk. We find it difficult to distinguish between these two possibilities. It is clear, however, that the galaxy has a inner, rotating disk with V_ rot 30 km s ^ 1 (see PV-diagram in Appendix app:Atlas ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 625 as a post-burst dwarf with concentrated resolved X-ray emission, disturbed gas kinematics, extraplanar features, and evidence for the end of an outflow phase.

quotes.quote: NGC 625 From Figure fig:ngc625_xraycontour , the strongest X-ray emission is highly concentrated, but resolved, in the center of the starburst. Diffuse soft X-ray emission surrounds this region and extends above and below, reaching close to the edge of the \ disc in projection in the south. Note that while the data for NGC 625 are archival, the X-ray imaging was not previously published. The \ kinematics are highly disturbed in the center of the galaxy and the complex \ velocity structure at larger radii are consistent with a significant outflow of neutral hydrogen Cannon2004 . \ imaging of NGC 625 shows low surface brightness extraplanar features above the central starburst in the same area as the detected X-ray emission including a complete loop with a defined edge Meurer2006 .
We find the end of an outflow phase in NGC 625.
The starburst activity has been declining over the most recent 100 Myr in two galaxies (NGC 625, NGC 5253), allowing us to constrain the longevity of the winds to have an upper limit of 25 Myr.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 625’s molecular gas with ALMA, finding discrete mostly virialized clouds, varied H II associations, and dense Butterfly clouds with little star formation.

quotes.quote: We present the highest resolution (1 ) ^ 12 CO observations of molecular gas in the dwarf starburst galaxy NGC 625, to date, obtained with ALMA. Molecular gas is distributed in discrete clouds within an area of 0.4 kpc ^2 and does not have well-ordered large-scale motions. We measure a total molecular mass in NGC 625 of 5.3 10^6 , assuming a Milky Way CO-to- conversion factor.

Larson scaling relations suggest that molecular clouds in NGC 625 are mostly in virial equilibrium. Comparison of our high-resolution CO observations with a star formation rate map, inferred from ancillary optical observations observations, suggests that about 40 of the molecular clouds coincide with the brightest H ii regions. These bright regions have a range of molecular gas depletion timescales, all within a factor of 3 of the global depletion time in NGC 625 of 106-134 Myr. The highest surface density molecular clouds towards the southwest of the galaxy, in a region we call the Butterfly, do not show strong star formation activity and suggest a depletion time scale longer than 5 Gyr.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 625 as a metal-poor dwarf starburst with a long-lived burst, disrupted gas disk, outflow, and CO emission features compared across ALMA resolutions.

quotes.quote: NGC 625 hosts a massive starburst with several luminous \ regions and Wolf-Rayet (W-R) spectral features skillman03a,skillman03b . The current starburst is relatively long-lived 50 Myr cannon03,mcquinn10 , and the extended episode of star formation, possibly caused by an interaction or merger cote00 , has apparently disrupted the \ disk cannon04a,lelli14 , and caused an outflow detected in UV absorption lines cannon05 .

The green ``spur'' to the east in NGC 625 shows that CO emission is present even without significant dust emission (traced by PACS); the diffuse \ emission is not well traced by this higher-resolution \ map see lelli14 .

NGC 625 was observed in \ with the MOPRA 22-m single dish by cormier14 and with ALMA by imara20 . The total CO velocity-integrated \ \ given by cormier14 is 4.3 0.6 , or a CO(1--0) luminosity of 1.1 10^6 . With ALMA, in a 1 31 1 08 beam, imara20 find = 3.0 0.2 , that over a region of 57 5 20 , gives a luminosity of 1.2 10^6 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 625 as an intermediate starburst dwarf with a coherent northwestern H I tail, a southeastern extension, and membership in the Sculptor group.

quotes.quote: There is not a clear-cut separation between these two types of morphologies, since there are several ``intermediate'' cases that have relatively regular distributions in the inner parts and small tails/filaments in the outer regions (NGC 4163, NGC 625, NGC 6789, and NGC 5253).

NGC 625 has a 2 kpc tail to the North-West, that shows a coherent kinematic structure at V_ los 420 km s ^ 1 . A second tail/extension is present to the South-East, but it does not show a clear kinematic structure. Our total map and velocity field are in close agreement with those from Cote2000 and Cannon2004 . NGC 625 is part of the Sculptor group, but it is quite far from the central, massive galaxy NGC 253, being at a projected distance of 1.3 Mpc Karachentsev2005 .

gmw_00001127.name: NGC 4068
gmw_00001127.overview: NGC 4068 is a nearby, low-metallicity starburst dwarf irregular galaxy located in the Canes Venatici I cloud [arXiv:2209.12522, arXiv:2105.13394]. It features a regularly rotating disk with a central gas depression [arXiv:1404.6252], a lopsided morphology with a broad southeastern H I extension [arXiv:2105.13394, arXiv:1409.1239], and widely distributed starburst activity [arXiv:1910.04167].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4068 as a nearby low-metallicity dwarf irregular galaxy with low oxygen abundance and compact high-dispersion nebulae, including a peculiar object likely tied to a massive WR star.

quotes.quote: In the current paper, we report the identification of the peculiar isolated ionized nebula in the nearby low-metallicity dwarf galaxy NGC 4068 (Fig. Ha_Hubble_map ) and argue that it is associated with a single massive WR star. The nearby dwarf irregular galaxy NGC 4068 is located at the distance D 4.36 Mpc Makarov2013 in the Canis Venatici I cloud. Its gas-phase metallicity estimates has been never published in literature, but our long-slit spectral observations are indicative of the effective (at 0.4 R_ 25 ) oxygen abundance 12+ (O/H) 7.85 ( Z 0.15 Z_ ) reaching 12+ (O/H) 7.4 ( Z 0.05 Z_ ) at the periphery (the analysis will be presented in subsequent paper Moiseev et al., in prep.) This estimate is in agreement with the luminosity of NGC 4068 ( M_B 15.1 Kaisina2012 ) corresponding to 12+ (O/H) 7.8 according to the luminosity--metallicity relation from Berg2012 .

Analyzing the internal kinematics of ionized gas in the galaxy NGC 4068 we found three compact nebulae having high both surface brightness and line of sight velocity dispersion in line (they are denoted as A, B and C in Fig. isigma ). As was previously shown by , combination of such characteristics is typical for objects like WR, LBV, supernova remnants (SNR) and other energetic stellar sources. Subsequent long-slit spectroscopy demonstrated that while the objects B and C have emission lines fluxes ratio typical for SNRs, the Object A (RA = 12h04m06.8s, Dec = 52 36 00 ) exhibits very unusual spectrum with peculiarly faint 6717,6731 lines and highest ratio of in the galaxy (Fig. spectrum_SAO , spectrum_CMO ).

sources.summary: The study classifies NGC 4068 as a regularly rotating starburst dwarf with a central gas depression, shell-like structures, mild lopsidedness, and a rotation curve matching prior work.

quotes.quote: Nine galaxies in our sample (50 ) have a regularly rotating disk: NGC 1705, NGC 2366, NGC 4068, NGC 4214, NGC 6789, UGC 4483, I Zw 18, I Zw 36, and SBS 1415+437. For these objects, we derived rotation curves following a procedure similar to Swaters2009 . As a first step, we obtained initial estimates of the geometrical parameters and of the rotation curve by fitting a tilted-ring model to the VF Begeman1987 .

NGC 4068 has a distribution characterized by a central depression and several shell-like structures. The kinematics is slightly lopsided. Our rotation curve agrees with the one of Swaters2009 within the errors.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4068 as one of four nearby star-forming dwarf galaxies used to compare resolved star formation histories with atomic and ionized gas turbulence indicators.

quotes.quote: We analyzed the resolved star formation histories along with the spatial distribution and kinematics of the atomic and ionized gas of four nearby star-forming dwarf galaxies (NGC 4068, NGC 4163, NGC 6789, UGC 9128) to determine the timescales over which stellar feedback drives turbulence. The four galaxies are within 5 Mpc and have a range of properties including current star formation rates of 0.0005 to 0.01 M _ yr ^ 1 , log(M _* /M _ ) between 7.2 and 8.2, and log(M _ HI /M _ ) between 7.2 and 8.3. Their Color-Magnitude Diagram (CMD) derived star formation histories over the past 500 Myrs were compared to their atomic and ionized gas velocity dispersion and HI energy surface densities as indicators of turbulence.

For our initial study on the impact of stellar feedback on turbulence in the ISM, multi-wavelength observations have been acquired for four nearby low-mass galaxies (NGC 4068, NGC 4163, NGC 6789, and UGC 9128; see Table table:galax ). All four of these galaxies have previously had their global SFHs determined as part of and are part of STARBIRDS McQuinn15a . These systems were selected as a pilot study to test our methodology for connecting star formation timescales with gas kinematics as they are representative of the larger sample.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4068 as a blue starburst dwarf whose SED-derived SFH matches CMD work, with recent inner star formation and extended, twisted H I.

quotes.quote: NGC 4068 NGC 4068 is a starbursting dwarf galaxy, identified by its extremely blue colors gallagher1986 . SED-derived SFHs, shown in Figure fig:ngc4068_sfh , agree with the CMD-derived SFH of mcquinn2010 . Because NGC 4068 has a moderate blue to red gradient, its SED results were expected to have a proportional relationship between t_ ySP and radius. Even with a moderate color gradient, the color profile shows consistently bluer (B-R) values ((B-R) 1 everywhere) than most other galaxies in the sample. The SED-derived results of the three innermost regions suggest that about 50 of the stars in this galaxy formed within the past 1 Gyr, where the burst of star formation has been largely located. Figure fig:ngc4068_4panel shows how the HI extends to the south much farther than the stellar disc in NGC 4068.

sources.summary: NGC4068 has consistent 3.6 micron and optical-band mass models, with stellar dominance inside, dark matter dominance outside, and an extended flat rotation curve.

quotes.quote: NGC4068 : The mass models of this galaxy using 3.6 m and -band match well. The stellar component dominates in the inner radius, and in the outer radius, the dark matter dominates. also studied the mass model of this galaxy, but their rotation curve does not reach the flat part ( 2.5 kpc), while the rotation curve from the present analysis clearly reaches the flat part and extends 4.5 kpc. Besides that, the velocity at which the rotation curve reaches the flat part is lesser than the maximum rising velocity from sparc_mass_model . However, in the inner part, the mass models look similar to sparc_mass_model when pseudo-Isothermal and Burkert profile is used for their halo model.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4068 as having widespread starburst activity, diffuse ionized gas in neutral-gas cavities, and fountain-like low-surface-brightness extensions.

quotes.quote: NGC 4068 In Figure fig:ngc4068 , NGC 4068 has high surface brightness \ emission tracing the location of recent star formation. This emission is spatially coincident with the peaks in the \ density along the plane of the disk and is dominated by bubble-like structures typical of \ regions. Based on the relative concentration of BHeB and RGB stars (see Table tab:star_formation ), the starburst is NGC 4068 is widely spatially distributed, in agreement with the distribution of \ regions. Defined structures in the \ emission are also seen including filaments and arcs of material, particularly in the north. NGC 4068 also has diffuse \ detected throughout the large, lower-density neutral gas cavities of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4068 as a BCD-type galaxy with studied OB-star content; its BPT points include high-dispersion regions but no obvious correlation.

quotes.quote: NGC 4068 is a BCD-type galaxy. Bastian et al. (2011) [ 9 ] found 60 OB stars in NGC 4068 by analyzing stellar population.

There are some points out of the star formation region in Fig. 3 corresponding to NGC 4068 although the correlation between and in this sample is not observed (Fig. 4). NGC 4068: There is not an obvious correlation between and , but some points with high velocity dispersion should be studied.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4068 as a lopsided starburst dwarf with a broad southeastern H I extension in the low-mass Canes Venatici I cloud.

quotes.quote: Other galaxies, instead, show lopsided morphologies, characterized by minor asymmetries and/or extensions in the outer parts (NGC 2366, NGC 4068, NGC 4214, UGC 6456, UGC 9128, UGC 4483, and SBS 1415+437).
NGC 4068 shows a broad extension to the South-East. This object is in the Canes Venatici I (CVn I) cloud, which is an extended, loose group inhabited by low-mass galaxies Karachentsev2003 .

gmw_00001128.name: NGC 4163
gmw_00001128.overview: NGC 4163 is a star-forming dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2201.07339] located in the CVn I cloud [arXiv:1409.1239] with a kinematically disturbed disk [arXiv:1404.6252] and a clear galactic wind [arXiv:1910.04167]. It features a red giant branch [arXiv:0902.3675] and irregular tails that suggest past interactions [arXiv:1409.1239, arXiv:1910.04167].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC4163 as an example CMD target, noting its prominent RGB, lack of IC4662-like active star-forming regions, and AGB-star locus.

quotes.quote: Figure col shows the results for ACS observations of NGC4163 and IC4662. These galaxies were chosen in part for their differences in stellar populations even though both have a prominent RGB, which will be evident in their CMDs shown below. There are regions suggestive of active star formation in the image of IC4662 that are not apparent in NGC4163.

Figure n4163 shows example CMDs from ACS observations of NGC4163 and IC4662 in the F814W and F606W filters. The most prominent feature on the CMDs of these galaxies (and of most other galaxies in the database) is the RGB, seen here as solid wedges due to the high density of points. Other populations visible include the Main Sequence and Blue Loop stars that together form a vertical band seen between about 0.4 < F606W-F814W < +0.4 . Above and redward of the TRGB are the Asymptotic Giant Branch stars, which are centered at about F814W=23 and F606W-F814W=+1.3 for NGC4163 and F814W=22.6 and F606W-F814W=+1.5 for IC4662.

sources.summary: The study classifies NGC 4163 as kinematically disturbed, with a tiny velocity gradient, low rotation-to-dispersion ratio, and mismatched stellar and gas position angles.

quotes.quote: Seven galaxies in our sample have a kinematically disturbed disk: NGC 625, NGC 1569, NGC 4163, NGC 4449, NGC 5253, UGC 6456, and UGC 9128. For these objects, we used channels maps and PV-diagrams to estimate ( _ kin , _ kin ) , V_ sys , PA _ kin , and the rotation velocity in the outer parts. For the inclination, we assumed the optical value.
NGC 4163 shows a very small velocity gradient of 10 km s ^ 1 . The complex kinematics may be due to the low V_ rot / _ ratio. The PA of the stellar body and of the disk significantly differ by 40 ^ .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4163 as one of four nearby star-forming dwarf galaxies used to compare resolved star formation histories with atomic and ionized gas turbulence indicators.

quotes.quote: We analyzed the resolved star formation histories along with the spatial distribution and kinematics of the atomic and ionized gas of four nearby star-forming dwarf galaxies (NGC 4068, NGC 4163, NGC 6789, UGC 9128) to determine the timescales over which stellar feedback drives turbulence. The four galaxies are within 5 Mpc and have a range of properties including current star formation rates of 0.0005 to 0.01 M _ yr ^ 1 , log(M _* /M _ ) between 7.2 and 8.2, and log(M _ HI /M _ ) between 7.2 and 8.3. All four of these galaxies have previously had their global SFHs determined as part of and are part of STARBIRDS McQuinn15a . These systems were selected as a pilot study to test our methodology for connecting star formation timescales with gas kinematics as they are representative of the larger sample.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4163 as an intermediate dwarf with western and possible southern tails, located in the CVn I cloud near several irregular galaxies.

quotes.quote: There is not a clear-cut separation between these two types of morphologies, since there are several ``intermediate'' cases that have relatively regular distributions in the inner parts and small tails/filaments in the outer regions (NGC 4163, NGC 625, NGC 6789, and NGC 5253).

NGC 4163 shows a tail to the West and, possibly, a second tail to the South see also Hunter2012 . NGC 4163 is in the CVn I cloud and lie close to several other Irrs (at D_ p 30 kpc), including the starburst dwarf NGC 4214 and the ``compact'' irregular NGC 4190 (UGC 7232). Intriguingly, NGC 4190 has been classified as a BCD by Karachentsev2013 and shows a disturbed morphology see Swaters2002a .

sources.summary: The paper reports a clear galactic wind in NGC 4163, with ionized gas outside the disk, irregular H I tails suggesting interaction, and post-starburst star formation history.

quotes.quote: NGC 4163 In Figure fig:ngc4163 , there is a clear detection of galactic wind in NGC 4163. We find ionized gas around the galaxy in nearly all directions, reaching the farthest distance in the west. Traced by the \ emission, this gas is well outside the \ disk with a morphology that suggests a free-flowing wind. The projected physical scale of the outflowing gas is much less than a tenth of the virial radius.

gmw_00001129.name: NGC 6789
gmw_00001129.overview: NGC 6789 is a star-forming dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2201.07339] with a compact, regularly rotating disk [arXiv:1404.6252, arXiv:1409.1239]. It is located in the Local Void and exhibits asymmetric features in its outer regions [arXiv:1409.1239].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6789 as one of four nearby star-forming dwarf galaxies used to compare resolved star formation histories with atomic and ionized gas turbulence indicators.

quotes.quote: We analyzed the resolved star formation histories along with the spatial distribution and kinematics of the atomic and ionized gas of four nearby star-forming dwarf galaxies (NGC 4068, NGC 4163, NGC 6789, UGC 9128) to determine the timescales over which stellar feedback drives turbulence. The four galaxies are within 5 Mpc and have a range of properties including current star formation rates of 0.0005 to 0.01 M _ yr ^ 1 , log(M _* /M _ ) between 7.2 and 8.2, and log(M _ HI /M _ ) between 7.2 and 8.3. Their Color-Magnitude Diagram (CMD) derived star formation histories over the past 500 Myrs were compared to their atomic and ionized gas velocity dispersion and HI energy surface densities as indicators of turbulence.

For our initial study on the impact of stellar feedback on turbulence in the ISM, multi-wavelength observations have been acquired for four nearby low-mass galaxies (NGC 4068, NGC 4163, NGC 6789, and UGC 9128; see Table table:galax ). All four of these galaxies have previously had their global SFHs determined as part of and are part of STARBIRDS McQuinn15a . These systems were selected as a pilot study to test our methodology for connecting star formation timescales with gas kinematics as they are representative of the larger sample.

sources.summary: The study classifies NGC 6789 as a regularly rotating compact starburst dwarf disk extending to 3.5 optical scale lengths, with inclination constrained through 3D modeling.

quotes.quote: Nine galaxies in our sample (50 ) have a regularly rotating disk: NGC 1705, NGC 2366, NGC 4068, NGC 4214, NGC 6789, UGC 4483, I Zw 18, I Zw 36, and SBS 1415+437. For these objects, we derived rotation curves following a procedure similar to Swaters2009 . As a first step, we obtained initial estimates of the geometrical parameters and of the rotation curve by fitting a tilted-ring model to the VF Begeman1987 .
NGC 6789 has a compact disk that extends out to only 3.5 optical scale lengths. The inclination is uncertain: we derived i = 43^ 7^ using 3D disk models.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6789 as an intermediate starburst dwarf with a regularly rotating disk, asymmetric outer features, and strong isolation in the Local Void.

quotes.quote: There is not a clear-cut separation between these two types of morphologies, since there are several ``intermediate'' cases that have relatively regular distributions in the inner parts and small tails/filaments in the outer regions (NGC 4163, NGC 625, NGC 6789, and NGC 5253).

NGC 6789 has a regularly-rotating disk with several asymmetric features in the outer parts. This galaxy is in the Local Void and its nearest massive companion (NGC 6946) is at a projected distance of 2.5 Mpc Drozdovsky2001 .

gmw_00001130.name: UGC 4483
gmw_00001130.overview: UGC 4483 is a nearby, metal-poor blue compact dwarf galaxy located in the M81 group [arXiv:1207.2696, arXiv:2102.13119]. It features a regularly rotating, compact gas disk with a steep rotation curve [arXiv:1404.6252], an X-shaped optical morphology [arXiv:1501.06709], and a lopsided structure with a small northwestern extension [arXiv:1409.1239].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 4483 as a nearby blue compact dwarf with a differentially rotating disk, a steep rotation curve, central mass concentration, and substantial baryonic contribution.

quotes.quote: UGC 4483 is a nearby Blue Compact Dwarf (BCD) galaxy. HST observations have resolved the galaxy into single stars and this has led to the derivation of its star formation history and to a direct estimate of its stellar mass. We have analysed archival VLA observations of the 21-cm line and found that UGC 4483 has a steeply-rising rotation curve which flattens in the outer parts at a velocity of 20 km s ^ 1 . Radial motions of 5 km s ^ 1 may also be present. As far as we know, UGC 4483 is the lowest-mass galaxy with a differentially rotating disk.

The steep rise of the rotation curve indicates that there is a strong central concentration of mass. We have built mass models using the HST information on the stellar mass to break the disk-halo degeneracy: old stars contribute 50 of the observed rotation velocity at 2.2 disk scale-lengths. Baryons (gas and stars) constitute an important fraction of the total dynamical mass.

sources.summary: UGC 4483 shows arrow-shaped H I matching its X-shaped optical morphology, with extended gas, a vertical kinematic axis, rotating-disk modeling, and high-resolution peaks near star formation.

quotes.quote: UGC 4483 (also known as Mrk 0090 or SBS 0826+528) From Ott et al. (2012; their Fig. 7), from the VLA ANGST, and Lelli et al. (2012b; their Fig. 1 and 2) and Lelli, Verheijan Fraternali (2014a, b; their Fig. 1, C.11, and Fig. 1, 2), with the VLA. The intermediate resolution (20 x 20 ) H 1 map shows almost vertical (PA _ HI 80 ^ ) "arrow-shaped" emission, where the head of the arrow spatially coincides with the bright star formation knot to the North, heading the cometary optical morphology. There is also extended H 1 emission to the South and Northwest. The central H 1 morphology matches well the observed "X-shaped" and overall optical morphology (PA _ opt 80 ^ ). The kinematical axis is vertical (PA _ kin 90 ^ ), roughly matching the position angle of the brighter arm (PA _ opt 95 ^ ) associated with the "X-shaped" optical morphology.

The velocity field can be modeled by a rotating disk, possibly with a radial component. High resolution (10 x 10 ) H 1 observations mimic the larger-scale arrow-shape emission, but two H 1 peaks are resolved, the brightest of which is elongated to the Southeast and spatially coincides with the bright optical star formation knot. There is extended emission also to the South and East. On these scales, the H 1 major axis (PA _ HI 60 ^ ) appears to follow in the direction of the fainter "arm" associated with the "X-shaped" optical morphology (PA _ opt 60 ^ ).

sources.summary: The study includes UGC 4483 as a regularly rotating dwarf, citing prior work showing a compact gas disk, steep-to-flat rotation curve, and dynamically important baryons.

quotes.quote: Nine galaxies in our sample (50 ) have a regularly rotating disk: NGC 1705, NGC 2366, NGC 4068, NGC 4214, NGC 6789, UGC 4483, I Zw 18, I Zw 36, and SBS 1415+437. For these objects, we derived rotation curves following a procedure similar to Swaters2009 . As a first step, we obtained initial estimates of the geometrical parameters and of the rotation curve by fitting a tilted-ring model to the VF Begeman1987 .

In Lelli2012a, Lelli2012b , we presented our results for two show-case galaxies: I Zw 18 and UGC 4483. For both objects, we showed that i) the gas forms a compact, rotating disk; ii) the rotation curve rises steeply in the inner parts and flattens in the outer regions; and iii) old stars and atomic gas are dynamically important, since they constitute at least 30 of the total dynamical mass within the last measured point of the rotation curve.

sources.summary: The paper studies UGC 4483 as a nearby metal-poor blue compact dwarf, resolving its stellar populations, star-formation history, and old population traced by candidate RR Lyrae stars.

quotes.quote: We present new WFC3/UVIS observations of UGC 4483, the closest example of a metal-poor blue compact dwarf galaxy, with a metallicity of Z 1/15 \ Z_ and located at a distance of D 3.4 Mpc. The extremely high quality of our new data allows us to clearly resolve the multiple stellar evolutionary phases populating the color-magnitude diagram (CMD), to reach more than 4 mag deeper than the tip of the red giant branch, and to detect for the first time core He-burning stars with masses 2 M _ , populating the red clump and possibly the horizontal branch (HB) of the galaxy. By applying the synthetic CMD method to our observations, we determine an average star formation rate over the whole Hubble time of at least (7.01 0.44) 10^ 4 M_ /yr , corresponding to a total astrated stellar mass of (9.60 0.61) 10^6 M_ , 87 of which went into stars at epochs earlier than 1 Gyr ago. With our star formation history recovery method we find the best fit with a distance modulus of DM = 27.45 0.10 , slightly lower than previous estimates. Finally, we find strong evidence of an old ( 10 Gyr) stellar population in UGC 4483 thanks to the detection of an HB phase and the identification of six candidate RR Lyrae variable stars.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 4483 as a lopsided starburst dwarf with a regularly rotating disk, a small northwestern extension, and a location in the M81 group.

quotes.quote: Other galaxies, instead, show lopsided morphologies, characterized by minor asymmetries and/or extensions in the outer parts (NGC 2366, NGC 4068, NGC 4214, UGC 6456, UGC 9128, UGC 4483, and SBS 1415+437).
UGC 4483 has a regularly-rotating disk with a small extension to the North-West. This galaxy is in the M81 group and lies between the group center and the NGC 2403 sub-group Karachentsev2002 .

gmw_00001131.name: UGC 9128
gmw_00001131.overview: UGC 9128 is a nearby, isolated star-forming dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:2201.07339, arXiv:1409.1239] with a lopsided optical morphology [arXiv:1409.1239] and a kinematically disturbed disk [arXiv:1404.6252]. The galaxy is gas-rich [arXiv:1912.05352, arXiv:2303.05104], featuring three holes in its neutral hydrogen disk [arXiv:2006.01735] and an oblate dark matter halo [arXiv:2303.05104].

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC 9128 as one of four nearby star-forming dwarf galaxies used to compare resolved star formation histories with atomic and ionized gas turbulence indicators.

quotes.quote: We analyzed the resolved star formation histories along with the spatial distribution and kinematics of the atomic and ionized gas of four nearby star-forming dwarf galaxies (NGC 4068, NGC 4163, NGC 6789, UGC 9128) to determine the timescales over which stellar feedback drives turbulence. The four galaxies are within 5 Mpc and have a range of properties including current star formation rates of 0.0005 to 0.01 M _ yr ^ 1 , log(M _* /M _ ) between 7.2 and 8.2, and log(M _ HI /M _ ) between 7.2 and 8.3. Their Color-Magnitude Diagram (CMD) derived star formation histories over the past 500 Myrs were compared to their atomic and ionized gas velocity dispersion and HI energy surface densities as indicators of turbulence.

For our initial study on the impact of stellar feedback on turbulence in the ISM, multi-wavelength observations have been acquired for four nearby low-mass galaxies (NGC 4068, NGC 4163, NGC 6789, and UGC 9128; see Table table:galax ). All four of these galaxies have previously had their global SFHs determined as part of and are part of STARBIRDS McQuinn15a . These systems were selected as a pilot study to test our methodology for connecting star formation timescales with gas kinematics as they are representative of the larger sample.

sources.summary: The study classifies UGC 9128 as disturbed, with a 25 km/s disk, irregular velocity field, broad asymmetric profiles, and mismatched optical and kinematic axes.

quotes.quote: Seven galaxies in our sample have a kinematically disturbed disk: NGC 625, NGC 1569, NGC 4163, NGC 4449, NGC 5253, UGC 6456, and UGC 9128. For these objects, we used channels maps and PV-diagrams to estimate ( _ kin , _ kin ) , V_ sys , PA _ kin , and the rotation velocity in the outer parts. For the inclination, we assumed the optical value.

UGC 9128 has a disk that rotates at 25 km s ^ 1 , but the VF is very irregular and the line profiles are broad and asymmetric, possibly due to non-circular motions. The optical and kinematic PA differ by 30 ^ .

sources.summary: The paper describes DDO 187 as one of the smallest sample galaxies by H I disk radius, with three holes including two overlapping holes.

quotes.quote: DDO 187 DDO 187 (PGC 50961, UGC 9128) is one of the smallest galaxy in the sample in terms of \ disk radius. It has three holes and two of them (Nos. 2 and 3) are overlapped. The galaxy is at a distance of 2.2 Mpc from the Milky Way with a V-band magnitude of 12.7 . We estimated the scale height and the size of the \ disk to be about 60 pc and 0.5 kpc respectively.

sources.summary: The paper reports elevated H I velocity dispersion for DDO187 and places it among gas-rich dwarf galaxies with very oblate halos, including the lowest quoted q value.

quotes.quote: We also included two galaxies NGC 4190 and UGC 8833 from the VLA ACS Nearby Galaxy Survey Treasury (VLA ANGST) survey ott.etal.2012 . Table 1 shows the sample of 20 galaxies, of which first 8 are relatively large, massive spirals (NGC 0628, NGC 3184, NGC 4736, NGC 5194, NGC 5236 and NGC 6946), and the remaining 12 are smaller spiral, dwarf or irregular galaxies. The procedure for determining the velocity dispersion across the galaxy disks as a function of radius is described in ianjamasimanana12 and ianjamasimanana.etal.2017 as well as in das.etal.2020 . We basically co-added the individual velocity profiles over radial bins to obtain azimuthally averaged, high signal-to-noise ratio (S/N) velocity dispersion values for each radial bin. The stacked profiles were fitted with single Gaussian functions, where the half width of the fitted Gaussian represents the velocity dispersion of the gas. This method gives a better estimate of the velocity dispersion compared to the moment2 maps. Figure 4 in the appendix shows the radial variation of the velocity dispersion across the galaxy disks. Its value is less than 10 kms ^ 1 at the R _ 25 radius, except for a few cases (NGC 5194, NGC 4190, DDO 187 and IC 10), where _ H i lies between 11 and 14 kms ^ 1 .

Figure 2 and Table 2 show that galaxies with more massive stellar masses (M(*) > 10 ^9 M _ ) have q values close to q 1 at R_ 25 and beyond (e.g. NGC 628 and NGC 6946). The exceptions are NGC 4214 and NGC 5236. Of these two galaxies, NGC 4214 is a relatively small galaxy and close to being a dwarf olling.1996 . In fact, based on its rotation velocity, stellar mass, and absolute B-band magnitude values, NGC 4214 has been classified as a dwarf galaxy by 2008AJ....136.2782L . But NGC 5236 is quite massive ( M(*) 6.9 10^ 10 M_ and so its low q value is quite surprising. For the less massive galaxies, a large fraction of which are LSB dwarfs, q is much smaller (Figure 2 and table 2). Six of them have q=0.2-0.3 (Holmberg1, M81dwfA, IC10, DDO75, DDO187 and UGC8833) and 3 of them have q=0.4-0.5 (Holmberg2, F564-V3, DDO46). The exception is IC1613 which has a relatively large q 1 value at R_ 25 .

It is interesting to note that the gas rich galaxies (9 of them) all have oblate halos, with q<0.6 but the stellar dominated galaxies have a range of q values with q 0.2 (very oblate) to q 1.25 (prolate). Perhaps the most important result from this study is that the gas rich galaxies all have oblate halos at R_ 25 (Fig. 3). There are 9 gas rich galaxies that have M(gas)/M(baryon>0.5 and they are all dwarf galaxies : Holmberg1, Holmberg2, M81dwfA, DDO46, DDO53, DDO75, DDO187, F564-V3 and UGC8833. The q varies between 0.11 in DDO187 to 0.55 in Holmberg2. The stellar dominated galaxies, however, have a variety of q values ranging from q=0.21 in IC10 to q=1.25 for NGC5194 at R_ 25 .
