sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 9128 as a lopsided but relatively regular starburst dwarf whose optical and kinematic position angles differ and which appears very isolated.

quotes.quote: Other galaxies, instead, show lopsided morphologies, characterized by minor asymmetries and/or extensions in the outer parts (NGC 2366, NGC 4068, NGC 4214, UGC 6456, UGC 9128, UGC 4483, and SBS 1415+437).

UGC 9128 has a relatively regular distribution, but the optical and kinematic position angles differ by 30 ^ . This galaxy appears very isolated; the closest massive galaxy is the Milky Way at D 2.2 Mpc Karachentsev2013 .

sources.summary: DDO187 is treated as a gas-rich dwarf whose stellar disk extends unusually far, with dynamical mass several times H I mass beyond R25 and the largest baryonic mass discrepancy.

quotes.quote: The remaining 3 galaxies, DDO46, DDO63 and DDO187 are gas rich dwarfs and are all part of the Local Irregulars That Trace Luminosity Extremes; The H i Nearby Galaxy Survey ( LITTLE THINGS ) survey hunter.etal.2012 which is a survey of nearby, gas rich irregular/dwarf galaxies.
The most striking case is DDO187, where M(dyn) is several times the value of M( H i ) in the region R>R_ 25 for both z_ 1/2 =0.5 and 1 kpc (Table 2).
The M(dyn)/M(baryonic) is the largest for the gas rich dwarf galaxy DDO187.

gmw_00001132.name: UGCA 290
gmw_00001132.overview: UGCA 290 is a starburst dwarf galaxy that may be a member of the NGC 4631 group [arXiv:1409.1239]. It exhibits an unsettled structure where the gas distribution is offset from its stellar component, featuring irregular kinematics dominated by distinct clouds [arXiv:1404.6252, arXiv:1409.1239].

sources.summary: The study reports new VLA observations of UGCA 290 and classifies it as unsettled, with gas offset from the stars and irregular cloud-dominated kinematics.

quotes.quote: SBS 1415+437 and UGCA 290 were observed with the B, C, and D arrays of the VLA between March 2011 and April 2012. The correlator was used in dual-polarization WIDAR mode with a total bandwidth of 2.0 MHz and 256 spectral line channels, providing a velocity resolution of 1.9 km s ^ 1 .
UGCA 290 has a distribution that is offset with respect to the stellar component. The kinematics is irregular and dominated by a few distinct clouds.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGCA 290 as a starburst dwarf with H I offset from its stars, consistent flux despite different map extent, and possible NGC 4631 group membership.

quotes.quote: Moreover, the use of the optical center returns high values of A for galaxies that show a strong offset between the distribution and the stellar body (e.g. NGC 1705 and UGCA 290), that may indicate a recent interaction/accretion event.

UGCA 290 has a peculiar distribution that is off-set with respect to the stellar component. Our total map at 20'' 20'' resolution is less extended than the one obtained by Kovac2009 using WSRT data at 52.2'' 30.9'' resolution, but the fluxes are consistent within the uncertainties, indicating that our total map is not missing diffuse emission. UGCA 290 may be part of the NGC 4631 group; its projected distance from NGC 4631 is 700 kpc.

gmw_00001133.name: I Zw 36
gmw_00001133.overview: I Zw 36 is an isolated starburst blue compact dwarf galaxy located in the Canes Venatici I group [arXiv:0710.0711, arXiv:2006.01735]. It features a heavily disturbed, asymmetric gas distribution with a central rotating disk that is offset from its optical center [arXiv:1409.1239, arXiv:1404.6252].

sources.summary: The paper presents UGCA 281 as an isolated blue compact dwarf with a bright emission knot, starburst character, disputed magnitudes, and new BVR photometry.

quotes.quote: UGCA 281=Mkn 209 . On the western side of this blue compact dwarf galaxy (BCD), there is a very bright emission knot with a short arc. Judging by the distance, 5.43 Mpc, measured by Tully et al. (2007) with HST, this compact starburst galaxy has no close neighbors and is quite appropriate to be called ``intergalactic HII region'' given by Sargent Searle (1970). We note some inconsistency in the integrated blue magnitudes for this galaxy.

So, Papaderos et al. (1996) obtained B_T = 14.84 , while Makarova et al. (1997) and Gil de Paz et al. (2003) give for it 15.14 and 14.15 mag, respectively. We observed UGCA 281 with the 6-m telescope in the B,V,R bands and obtained B_T = 14 87 0 05 . This magnitude measured by M.E.Sharina is finally presented in Table 2.

sources.summary: The paper describes I Zw 36 as a heavily disturbed dwarf with asymmetric gas connected to a rotating disk, southern tail-like emission, northern extension, and no optical counterpart to the extended gas.

quotes.quote: Several galaxies have heavily disturbed distributions, characterized by large-scale asymmetries, long filaments, and/or a large optical- offset (NGC 4449, I Zw 18, NGC 1705, UGC 6541, NGC 1569, and I Zw 36).

I Zw 36 has an extended, asymmetric distribution, that is kinematically-connected to a central rotating disk (see Ashley2013 and LVF14). Data at 10 '' resolution (see Fig. fig:Izw36 ) reveal that the emission forms a tail-like structure to the South at receding velocities ( V_ los 300 to 310 km s ^ 1 ; V_ sys = 277 km s ^ 1 ) and a broad extension to the North near the systemic velocity ( V_ los 270 to 290 km s ^ 1 ), possibly connected to the approaching side of the disk ( V_ los 250 to 260 km s ^ 1 ). There are no optical features associated with the extended gas down to _ R 26 mag arcsec ^ 2 . I Zw 36 is in the CVn I cloud.

sources.summary: The paper treats Haro 29 as one of the low-metallicity objects used for helium abundance work and notes its extended star-formation history limits burst-driven Y/O changes.

quotes.quote: In sections 4 to 7 we redetermine the He ^+ /H ^+ values for I Zw 18, NGC 2363, Haro 29, and SBS 0335--052, based on the observations of izo97 and izo99 and on photoionization models for these objects. These four objects were selected from their sample based on the following criteria: the two objects with the lowest heavy element abundances and the two objects with the smallest observational errors pei01 . We derive He ^+ /H ^+ , N_e (He  ii ), T (He  ii ), and (3889) self-consistently based on all the observed He  i line intensities, with the exception of those strongly affected by underlying absorption. By combining the He ^+ /H ^+ values of these four objects with that of NGC 346 we derive a new Y_p (nHc) in section 8.

From chemical evolution models it is found that changes in Y/ O are small even in the presence of a very strong burst of star formation car99 ; moreover these objects seem to have been forming stars over periods of at least 1 to 2 Gyr implying that most of their oxygen formed before the present burst, as in the case of Haro 29 e.g. sch01 , and consequently that the expected changes in Y/ O due to bursts are very small. The Y_p (nHc) value derived by us is significantly smaller than the value derived by izo98 , from the Y - O/H linear regression for a sample of 45 BCGs, and by izo99 , from the average for the two most metal deficient galaxies known (I Zw 18 and SBS 0335--052), that amount to 0.2443 0.0015 and 0.2452 0.0015 respectively. Our T_e (He  ii ) determinations, that we prefer, produce Y values that are about 0.007 smaller than those derived by izo98 and izo99 , a small but significative difference, as we will see below.

sources.summary: The study classifies I Zw 36 as a regularly rotating starburst dwarf whose asymmetric gas distribution contains a compact central rotating disk offset from the optical center.

quotes.quote: Nine galaxies in our sample (50 ) have a regularly rotating disk: NGC 1705, NGC 2366, NGC 4068, NGC 4214, NGC 6789, UGC 4483, I Zw 18, I Zw 36, and SBS 1415+437. For these objects, we derived rotation curves following a procedure similar to Swaters2009 . As a first step, we obtained initial estimates of the geometrical parameters and of the rotation curve by fitting a tilted-ring model to the VF Begeman1987 .

I Zw 36 has an extended and asymmetric distribution see Ashley2013 , but in the central parts the forms a compact, rotating disk. The optical and kinematic centers are offset by 12 '' ( 340 pc), while PA _ opt and PA _ kin differ by 36 ^ .

sources.summary: The paper identifies Haro 29 as a starburst blue compact dwarf in Canes Venatici I with two intact holes and measured H I dimensions.

quotes.quote: Haro 29 Haro 29 is a starburst blue compact dwarf. It is a member of the Canes Venatici I Group of galaxies and is at a distance of about 5.8 Mpc. It is also known as Mrk 209, I Zw 36, UGCA 281 and PGC 40665. The \ scale height of this galaxy is 220 pc and the \ disk radius is 2.3 kpc respectively. The galaxy has two intact holes.

sources.summary: Mk 209 is described with a high-column-density HI peak, a regular but under-resolved velocity field, and an XV-map velocity gradient consistent with solid-body rotation.

quotes.quote: Mk 209: The peak of the high-column-density region is N_ HI =2.9 10^ 21 2 . The velocity field appears regular but could not be fitted properly because of insufficient resolution. The velocity gradient was fitted manually to the XV map; it is consistent with a solid body rotation curve. HI in Mk 209 (IZw 36) has also been observed with the VLA by Viallefond et al. (1987).

gmw_00001134.name: SBS 1415+437
gmw_00001134.overview: SBS 1415+437 is a metal-poor blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0701138] with a lopsided, regularly rotating disk [arXiv:1404.6252, arXiv:1409.1239]. It exhibits a cometary structure [arXiv:1404.6252] with a southern starburst region [arXiv:1404.6252] and has sustained star formation for over 2 Gyr [arXiv:astro-ph/0701138].

sources.summary: The study classifies SBS 1415+437 as a cometary, lopsided BCD whose kinematic center coincides with the southern starburst rather than the optical center.

quotes.quote: Nine galaxies in our sample (50 ) have a regularly rotating disk: NGC 1705, NGC 2366, NGC 4068, NGC 4214, NGC 6789, UGC 4483, I Zw 18, I Zw 36, and SBS 1415+437. For these objects, we derived rotation curves following a procedure similar to Swaters2009 . As a first step, we obtained initial estimates of the geometrical parameters and of the rotation curve by fitting a tilted-ring model to the VF Begeman1987 .

SBS 1415+437 is a prototype ``cometary'' BCD, as the starburst region is located at the edge of an elongated stellar body. Remarkably, the kinematic center does not coincide with the optical one but with the starburst region to the South (see Appendix app:Atlas ; the object at R.A. 14 ^ h 17 ^ m 00 ^ s and Dec. 43 ^ 29 ' 45 '' is a foreground star). The lopsided distribution and kinematics may be due to a pattern of elliptical orbits centered on the starburst region cf. Baldwin1980 .

sources.summary: SBS 1415+437 has an extended single-peaked H I disk whose peak lies on a southern star-forming knot, with resolved tails, optical alignment, and lopsided outskirts.

quotes.quote: SBS 1415+437 From Lelli, Verheijan Fraternali (2014a, b; their Fig. C.18, and Fig. 1, 2), with the VLA. The intermediate resolution (20 x 20 ) H 1 map shows a single-peaked Northeast-Southwest (PA _ HI 115 ^ ) disk, extended to the North, and with smaller extensions to the South, East and West. The H 1 peak is positionally coincident, not with the center of the optical disk, but with the Southern bright star formation knot that heads the cometary structure, while the cometary tail is mirrored in the Northeastern extension. High resolution (10 x 10 ) H 1 observations show a similar morphology to the intermediate resolution image, but where the extended South, East and West emission is now resolved into a web of small tails and filaments. The H 1 emission at intermediate-high resolution scales is roughly aligned with the optical disk (PA _ opt 120 ^ ) and the kinematical axis (PA _ kin 115 ^ ). The velocity field is ordered (rotation) only in the center, with the outskirts displaying lopsidedness.

sources.summary: The review describes SBS1415+437 as a distant metal-poor BCD with an RGB, a 13.6 Mpc distance, intermediate-age AGB stars, young stars, and sustained star formation.

quotes.quote: For example, Fig. sbs shows the CMDs from HST/ACS photometry of two of the three most metal-poor BCDs ever discovered: IZw18 To06 and SBS1415+437 Al05 , both more distant than 10 Mpc. In the case of SBS1415+437 (central panel in Fig. sbs ) a prominent RGB is clearly measured, demonstrating that the galaxy contains stars older than about 2 Gyr. The luminosity of the RGB tip implies a distance of 13.6 Mpc Al05 . By comparing the CMD with the Padova isochrones of appropriate metallicity (Z=0.001), shifted at that distance, it is apparent that SBS1415+437 also contains intermediate age AGB stars (including the thermally pulsing phase and carbon stars) and young massive stars. This leads to the important conclusion that, although very blue, currently active, metal poor, and gas rich, this system has not started only recently to form stars but has managed to sustain a significant star formation activity over more than 2 Gyr.

sources.summary: The paper describes SBS 1415+437 as a lopsided starburst dwarf with an extended disk and possible unidentified faint companions because of its relatively large distance.

quotes.quote: Other galaxies, instead, show lopsided morphologies, characterized by minor asymmetries and/or extensions in the outer parts (NGC 2366, NGC 4068, NGC 4214, UGC 6456, UGC 9128, UGC 4483, and SBS 1415+437).
SBS 1415+437 has an extended, lopsided disk. The galaxy is at a relatively large distance ( 13.6 Mpc), thus it is possible that faint, nearby companions have not been identified by optical surveys.

gmw_00001135.name: SDSS J0819+5356
gmw_00001135.overview: SDSS J0819+5356 is a quasar host galaxy that is classified as a disk galaxy [arXiv:1506.05147]. Structural modeling reveals that the host galaxy has a small Sersic index and a compact effective radius of 2.2 kpc [arXiv:1506.05147].

sources.summary: The paper models the quasar host galaxy in SDSS J0819+5356, measuring its luminosity and finding a small effective radius of 2.2 kpc with a low Sersic index indicative of a disk host.

quotes.quote: As shown in Table tab:host , we measured the quasar host galaxy luminosity in four systems (SDSS J0819+5356, SDSS J0832+0404, SDSS J0904+1512 and SDSS J1515+1511), and found upper limits found for an additional system (SDSS J1322+1052). When modelling the host galaxies, we considered a singular isothermal profile for the lensing galaxy. Since the real lenses may not be isothermal, this affects the inferred size and luminosity of the hosts. All four hosts with available measurements have small effective radii of 2.2, 3.2, 0.4 and 2.1 kpc for SDSS J0819+5356, SDSS J0832+0404, SDSS J0904+1512 and SDSS J1515+1511, respectively. All four hosts have small Sersic indices indicative of disk galaxies.

gmw_00001136.name: SDSS J0832+0404
gmw_00001136.overview: SDSS J0832+0404 is a quasar host galaxy [arXiv:1506.05147] that is classified as a disk galaxy [arXiv:1506.05147]. Modeling of the system reveals that the host galaxy has a small Sersic index and a small effective radius of 3.2 kpc [arXiv:1506.05147].

sources.summary: The paper models the quasar host galaxy in SDSS J0832+0404, measuring its luminosity and finding a small effective radius of 3.2 kpc with a low Sersic index indicative of a disk host.

quotes.quote: As shown in Table tab:host , we measured the quasar host galaxy luminosity in four systems (SDSS J0819+5356, SDSS J0832+0404, SDSS J0904+1512 and SDSS J1515+1511), and found upper limits found for an additional system (SDSS J1322+1052). When modelling the host galaxies, we considered a singular isothermal profile for the lensing galaxy. Since the real lenses may not be isothermal, this affects the inferred size and luminosity of the hosts. All four hosts with available measurements have small effective radii of 2.2, 3.2, 0.4 and 2.1 kpc for SDSS J0819+5356, SDSS J0832+0404, SDSS J0904+1512 and SDSS J1515+1511, respectively. All four hosts have small Sersic indices indicative of disk galaxies.

gmw_00001137.name: SDSS J0904+1512
gmw_00001137.overview: SDSS J0904+1512 is a strongly lensed quasar system with a source redshift of 1.826 [arXiv:0912.1462, arXiv:1506.05147]. The quasar's host galaxy has a small effective radius of 0.4 kpc and a low Sersic index indicative of a disk galaxy [arXiv:1506.05147].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J0904+1512 as a confirmed two-image lensed quasar whose faint lens galaxy was fit in I band, yielding a Sersic index, image separation, and source redshift.

quotes.quote: SDSS J0904+1512. The I band image shows that the lens galaxy is located near the brighter lens image, like HE1104-1805 wisotzki93 or SDSS J1226-0006 inada08 , and a fit to the galaxy profile yields the S ersic index of 4.1. When analyzing V and R band images, we fixed the galaxy profile to the I band profile because the galaxy is too faint to fit the galaxy profile correctly. The image separation is =1 128 0 007 , and the source redshift is z_s=1.826 0.002 .

sources.summary: The paper models the quasar host galaxy in SDSS J0904+1512, measuring its luminosity and finding a small effective radius of 0.4 kpc with a low Sersic index indicative of a disk host.

quotes.quote: As shown in Table tab:host , we measured the quasar host galaxy luminosity in four systems (SDSS J0819+5356, SDSS J0832+0404, SDSS J0904+1512 and SDSS J1515+1511), and found upper limits found for an additional system (SDSS J1322+1052). When modelling the host galaxies, we considered a singular isothermal profile for the lensing galaxy. Since the real lenses may not be isothermal, this affects the inferred size and luminosity of the hosts. All four hosts with available measurements have small effective radii of 2.2, 3.2, 0.4 and 2.1 kpc for SDSS J0819+5356, SDSS J0832+0404, SDSS J0904+1512 and SDSS J1515+1511, respectively. All four hosts have small Sersic indices indicative of disk galaxies.

gmw_00001138.name: SDSS J1515+1511
gmw_00001138.overview: SDSS J1515+1511 is a doubly imaged lensed quasar [arXiv:1701.04272] located at a redshift of 2.049 [arXiv:1701.04272]. Its host galaxy is a disk galaxy [arXiv:1506.05147] with a small effective radius of 2.1 kpc [arXiv:1506.05147].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes SDSS J1515+1511 as a doubly imaged lensed quasar, measuring its image time delay, source redshift, spectra, and usefulness for reverberation studies.

quotes.quote: We analyse new optical observations of the gravitational lens system SDSS J1515+1511. These include a 2.6--year photometric monitoring with the Liverpool Telescope (LT) in the band, as well as a spectroscopic follow--up with the LT and the Gran Telescopio Canarias (GTC).

The lensed quasar SDSS J1515+1511 consists of two optically bright images (A and B) separated by 2 2014AJ....147..153I . Although Inada et al. reported a source redshift z_ s = 2.054, this relies on the SDSS spectrum of the A image taken in 2007, which has all its emission features at z_ em = 2.049. It is also worth to note that the SDSS/BOSS spectrum of A in 2012, as well as the new spectra of A and B in 2015 (see Section subsubsec:gtcspec ), contain emission lines at a redshift of 2.049. We thus adopt z_ s = z_ em = 2.049 throughout this paper.

sources.summary: The paper models the quasar host galaxy in SDSS J1515+1511, measuring its luminosity and finding a small effective radius of 2.1 kpc with a low Sersic index indicative of a disk host.

quotes.quote: As shown in Table tab:host , we measured the quasar host galaxy luminosity in four systems (SDSS J0819+5356, SDSS J0832+0404, SDSS J0904+1512 and SDSS J1515+1511), and found upper limits found for an additional system (SDSS J1322+1052). When modelling the host galaxies, we considered a singular isothermal profile for the lensing galaxy. Since the real lenses may not be isothermal, this affects the inferred size and luminosity of the hosts. All four hosts with available measurements have small effective radii of 2.2, 3.2, 0.4 and 2.1 kpc for SDSS J0819+5356, SDSS J0832+0404, SDSS J0904+1512 and SDSS J1515+1511, respectively. All four hosts have small Sersic indices indicative of disk galaxies.

gmw_00001139.name: NGC 2997
gmw_00001139.overview: NGC 2997 is a giant, grand-design spiral galaxy [arXiv:1105.5711, arXiv:2303.16849] containing a weak central bar [arXiv:1204.5599] and a pseudobulge with an active nuclear spiral [arXiv:1004.5393]. It is the largest member of the loose galaxy group LGG 180 [arXiv:2303.16849] and features an extended neutral hydrogen disk [arXiv:1709.08733, arXiv:1312.3953].

sources.summary: The paper presents GMRT radio continuum observations of NGC 2997, measuring its integrated spectrum, central flat-spectrum source, circumnuclear ring, hotspots, magnetic field, and spiral-arm radio features.

quotes.quote: We present high-resolution, high-sensitivity radio continuum observations of the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 2997 at 332, 616 and 1272 MHz using the Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope (GMRT). The integrated spectrum of this galaxy has a spectral index of 0.92 ( S_ ^ ) and we place an upper limit to the thermal fraction at 1272 MHz of 10 per cent. Our multi-frequency study shows a relatively flat spectrum source ( 0.6 ) at the centre of the galaxy. This leads to radio detection of a circumnuclear ring in the high resolution map at 1272 MHz . We detect five hotspots in the ring, with average star formation rate of 0.024 M_ yr^ 1 , a median SN rate of 0.001 yr^ 1 and luminosity of 10^ 20 W Hz^ 1 .

NGC 2997 is an interesting grand design spiral galaxy of type SAB(rs) located in the loose galaxy group LGG 180 b14 . The disc inclination is 40^ b26 and the southern part of the disc is closer to us b29 . We assume a distance of 13.8 Mpc b33 to NGC 2997 and note that 1 arc-min corresponds to a linear scale of 4.0 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 2997 as an isolated giant spiral and largest LGG 180 member, surveying 55 surrounding dwarf candidates whose distributions suggest a dynamically young group.

quotes.quote: We present the discovery of 48 new and the analysis of 55, including 7 previously discovered, dwarf galaxy candidates around the giant spiral galaxy NGC 2997 using deep BVI images from the KMTNet Supernova Program. Their V band central surface brightness and total absolute magnitudes range between 20.3--26.7 mag arcsec ^ 2 and --(8.02--17.69) mag, respectively, while the I band effective radii are between 0.14 and 2.97 kpc. We obtain -1.43 0.02 for the faint-end slope of their luminosity function, comparable to previously measured values but shallower than theoretical predictions based on CDM models. The distance-independent distributions of their mass and color from the host galaxy NGC 2997 suggest that the group could be dynamically young, prior to the development of significant mass segregation or radial color gradients. The systematically bluer colors of the brighter candidates than the fainter ones indicate higher star formation activities in brighter members.

NGC 2997 is a giant spiral galaxy of type SAB(rs) with a redshift of z 0.003611 2004Meyer and an inclination angle of 40 1981Milliard . It is the largest member of the loose galaxy group LGG 180 1993Garcia and is relatively isolated from other major galaxies with no known companion within 110 kpc 2009Hess . The galaxy is reported to have a dynamical mass of 2.1 10 ^ 11 , a gas accretion rate of 1.2 \ yr ^ 1 , and a thick H I disk 2009Hess .

sources.summary: Deep observations of spiral NGC 2997 find no large clouds far from the disk but reveal a thick lagging disk, anomalous-velocity clouds, and inferred extragalactic gas accretion.

quotes.quote: Chengular 3 NCRA (TIFR) Pune 411007 India; chengalur@ncra.tifr.res We present deep \ observations of the moderately inclined spiral galaxy, NGC 2997. The goal of these observations was to search for \ clouds in the vicinity of NGC 2997 analogous to the high velocity clouds of the Milky Way and gain insight into their origins. We find evidence for the presence of a galactic fountain as well as the accretion of intragalactic material, however we do not identify any large clouds of \ far from the disk of the galaxy. NGC 2997 has a thick, lagging \ disk that is modeled with a vertical velocity gradient of 18-31 \ kpc ^ 1 .

Anomalous velocity \ clouds with masses of order 10^7 , which cannot be explained by galactic fountain models allow us to estimate a lower limit to the accretion of extragalactic gas of 1.2 \ yr ^ 1 . The number and mass of these clouds have implications for cosmological simulations of large scale structure and the presence of dark matter halos. We have used values from the the literature to estimate a star formation rate of 5 1 \ yr ^ 1 and to derive a new distance to NGC 2997 of 12.2 0.9 Mpc using published Tully-Fisher relations. galaxies: individual (NGC 2297) -- galaxies: ISM -- galaxies: kinematics and dynamics -- galaxies: structure In the Milky Way Galaxy, 21 cm observations of neutral atomic hydrogen ( ) provide a unique probe of the structure and dynamics of the interstellar medium.

sources.summary: The paper uses near-infrared photometry of NGC 2997 to identify young complexes in its southern arm and infer a spiral density-wave pattern speed from their color gradient.

quotes.quote: Deep JHK- photometry of the southern arm of the grand-design spiral galaxy NGC 2997 was obtained by ISAAC/VLT. All sources in the field brighter than K=19 mag were located. Color-color diagrams were used to identify young stellar complexes among the extended sources. Ages can be estimated for the youngest complexes and correlated with azimuthal distances from the spiral arms defined by the K-band intensity variation.

The alignment and color gradient of the bright, diffuse complexes strongly support a density wave scenario for NGC 2997. Only the brightest complexes with \ <-12 mag show a well aligned structure along the arm, suggesting that a strong compression in the gas due to the spiral potential is required to form these most massive aggregates, while smaller starforming regions are formed more randomly in the disk. The sharp transition between the two groups at \ = -12 mag may be associated with expulsion of gas when the first supernovae explode in the complex.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2997 as a hot-spot galaxy with many central compact sources and additional massive clusters beyond the center, though less rich than NGC 5236 in current data.

quotes.quote: NGC 2997 NGC 2997 is an example of a ``hot spot'' galaxy (Meaburn et. al. 1982) with a number of UV luminous knots near the centre. Walsh et. al. (1986) studied the knots and concluded that they are in fact very massive star clusters, and Maoz et. al. (1996) further investigated the central region of NGC 2997 using the HST. On an image taken with the repaired HST through the F606W filter they identified 155 compact sources, all with diameters of a few pc. Of 24 clusters detected in the F606W filter as well as in an earlier F220W image, all have colours implying ages less than 100 Myr and masses 10^4 M_ .

In our study we have found a number of massive star clusters also outside the centre of NGC 2997. Taking the numbers at face value, the cluster system does not appear to be as rich as that of NGC 5236, but with better and more complete data we would expect to see a number of YMCs in NGC 2997 that could rival that in NGC 5236.

sources.summary: The paper uses NIR observations of NGC 2997's southern spiral arm to connect young clusters, spiral-arm offsets, and a density-wave pattern speed estimate.

quotes.quote: Such knots in NGC 2997 have been identified as massive stellar clusters with ages of less than 10 Myr using JHK photometry and K band spectra. Their azimuthal distances from the spiral arms, as measured in the K band, correlates with their ages suggesting that the pattern speed of an underlying density wave can be derived. This method is tested on the grand-design spiral NGC 2997 using VLT data.

The analysis of sources in a field centered on the southern arm of NGC 2997 observed in NIR bands with ISAAC/VLT offers the following tentative conclusions: = the youngest stellar clusters with magnitudes \ <-12 \ are concentrated in the arm regions, the bright, young clusters (0.1 <Q ) show an age gradient where the youngest are further away (in front of) the spiral arm, the pattern speed of the density wave can be estimated to 18 km s ^ 1 kpc ^ 1 using this gradient and assuming circular motions and formation along a front with the same shape as the spiral arms, and fainter young clusters have a more uniform distribution. This is consistent with the standard density wave picture where the main spiral pattern starts just outside the ILR. The most massive clusters seem to be triggered by a front associated to the density wave while smaller clusters are more uniform distributed.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2997 as the sample representative, describing its Sc spiral structure, distance, NIR imaging depth, detected sources, and cluster-complex properties along its arms.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we will only present results for NGC 2997 as a representative for the full sample. Deep maps of the galaxies were obtained in JHK-bands with the HAWK-I/VLT instrument which provides a 7 ^ field with 0.1 ^ pixels. NGC 2997 is classified as Sc(s)I.3 and has an oval distortion in its central parts outside which a grand-design, two-armed spiral structure with a pitch angle of 21 ^ is seen. Its distance was assumed to be 19.2 Mpc derived from its velocity relative to the 3K-CMB and a Hubble constant of 73 km/s/Mpc. The reduced, stacked images had a seeing of 0.4 ^ which translates to a linear resolution of 40 pc at the distance of NGC 2997.
More than 5300 sources were identified on the K-band image of NGC 2997 using SExtractor bertin96 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2997 as a grand-design Sc spiral with measured pattern speed and rotation curve, finding stars alone fail and gas allows stability only for selected parameters.

quotes.quote: NGC 2997 This is a grand-design spiral galaxy of the Hubble type Sc MM81 . The pattern speed of the grand-design spiral structure is measured to be 16 km s ^ 1 kpc ^ 1 by GD09 , by using the measurement of azimuthal age-gradient of newly formed stars. The rotation curve is taken from PET78 .

On comparing the |s|_ obs values that we obtained for both NGC 6946 and NGC 2997, with Fig. fig2 , we find that the inequality given by equation (8) is not satisfied for any value of Q_ s , considered here. Thus, the dispersion relation for a purely stellar disc does not yield a real solution for |k| and hence it does not yield a stable density wave corresponding to the observed pattern speeds of both these galaxies.
For NGC 2997, the inequality given by equation (8) is satisfied only for Q_ s = 1.5 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2997 as having central star-forming regions, a weak bar, a main spiral with a sharp southern-arm turn, and substantial inter-arm complexes.

quotes.quote: NGC 2997: Besides a ring of star forming regions at the center, a bar with a_2 = 10 ends around 45 , where the main spiral emerges with ( a_2 , i ) = (0.4, 21.2 ). The regular logarithmic spiral pattern terminates close to 125 , where the southern arm has an abrupt turn.
The azimuthal distribution of complexes relative to the two-armed spiral is shown in Fig. fig:mq2997 for NGC 2997, considering only the radial range with a strong, symmetric pattern.
The contrast between arm and inter-arm regions varies significantly, where NGC 2997 has a larger amount of inter-arm complexes than NGC 1365.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2997 as a spiral with compressed magnetic fields, a well-developed magnetic arm, high polarization from stronger density waves, and possibly efficient mean-field dynamo action.

quotes.quote: The southern-sky spiral galaxy NGC 2997 hosts compressed magnetic fields at the inner edges of material arms, as well as one well-developed magnetic arm han99 . The stronger density waves in NGC 2997 lead to higher degrees of polarization at 6.2 of typically 25 at the inner edge of the northern arm, similar to those in M 51 fletcher11 .

Further polarization observations with higher resolution of IC 342, NGC 2997, NGC 6946, and other galaxies with magnetic arms in different evolutionary stages are needed for a better understanding of this phenomenon. htbp The total and polarized radio continuum emission from the nearby spiral galaxy IC 342 was observed in four wavelength bands with the Effelsberg and VLA telescopes.

The much longer magnetic arms in NGC 2997 and NGC 6946 are possibly due to more efficient mean-field dynamo action in a more stable density-wave environment, while the dynamo in IC 342 is slow and weak, probably disturbed by the bar, tidal interaction, or a transient spiral pattern.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2997 as an L* disk comparable to NGC 3198, emphasizing its low-inclination geometry and extended H I disk.

quotes.quote: Of particular note, Pisano (2014) used the Green Bank Telescope (GBT) to reach down to \ 1.2 10^ 18 cm ^ 2 (5 ) in two disk galaxies, NGC 6946 and NGC 2997. Although the single-dish GBT has a large beam (a width of 16 and 32 kpc in NGC 6946 and NGC 2997, respectively), it does not suffer from the spatial filtering of interferometer observations that make them insensitive to emission on large angular scales. NGC 6946 was found to have a previously undetected filament connecting it to its companion galaxies, while NGC 2997 exhibited an extended \ disk with an outer radius R 53 kpc.

In NGC 2997, which is at low inclination and hence geometric corrections are small, the disk extends much further out, to approximately 53 kpc at the 1.2 10^ 18 cm ^ 2 level, and any steepening in the slope of \ versus radius occurs at or beyond the limits of the data.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 2997 as a pseudobulge galaxy and reports its central morphology, Sersic index, and mid-infrared star-formation state.

quotes.quote: NGC 2997 .SXT5../Sc(s) Pseudobulge. The bulge is comprised of a bright nuclear spiral that nearly forms a ring at 5 . The decomposition yields n_b=1.0 , and the bulge is actively forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =1.5 mag.

sources.summary: The GBT data show NGC 2997 has an extended H I disk and gas-rich satellites but no filament, with slightly more H I mass and larger extent than interferometric measurements.

quotes.quote: Around NGC 2997, the GBT \ data reveal an extended \ disk and all of its surrounding gas-rich satellite galaxies, but no filamentary features. Furthermore, the \ mass as measured with the GBT is only 7 higher than past interferometric measurements. After correcting for resolution differences, the \ extent of the galaxy is 23 larger at the = 1.2 10 ^ 18 \ level as measured by the GBT.

gmw_00001140.name: VIDEO z7 28
gmw_00001140.overview: VIDEO z7 28 is the brightest candidate in its sample of high-redshift galaxies [arXiv:2304.02494]. It has a photometric redshift of z=7.38 and a red IRAC color, though it may potentially be a brown dwarf [arXiv:2304.02494].

sources.summary: The paper highlights VIDEO z7 28 as the brightest candidate in its z~7 sample, with red IRAC color, z=7.38 photometric redshift, and possible brown-dwarf ambiguity.

quotes.quote: An interesting example is the brightest object in our sample, VIDEO z7 28, which has a red IRAC colour of [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] = 1.3 0.4 and a photometric redshift of z=7.38^ +0.03 _ 0.06 . Bowler_2020 extend the expected IRAC colours derived by smit2014 out to higher redshifts, predicting that galaxies with strong H + [ O iii ] emission ( EW_ 0 = 500 - 2000 ) can have colours as high as [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] 1.0 at z 7.5 , in agreement with our photometry for this object. As we discuss later in Section sec:previous work , we show that several previous z 7 candidates at around z=7.4 are likely to be BDs. VIDEO z7 28 passes our selection criterion with the BD fit showing ^ 2 _ BD = 11.1 . It is slightly extended in the data with a FWHM of 1.5 arcsec in comparison to the PSF of the Y band.

However, we cannot fully rule out a BD solution for this source, and further follow-up will be required to confirm this extremely luminous LBG. We note that our brightest bin contains only one galaxy, VIDEO z7 28. If VIDEO z7 28 is indeed shown to be a BD with later follow-up, this would change this point into an upper limit of 2.07 10^ 8 objects / mag / Mpc ^ 3 , leaving our conclusions unchanged.

gmw_00001141.name: J0155-0857
gmw_00001141.overview: J0155-0857 is a quasar characterized by an asymmetric emission-line profile [arXiv:1106.1180]. Its broad emission lines exhibit a redshift relative to its narrow lines, with a slight asymmetry peaking at longer wavelengths [arXiv:1106.1180].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J0155-0857 as asymmetric line profile, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar quasar.

quotes.quote: Classified: asymmetric line profile. J0155-0857 The line of this source shows a small ( 1 500 ) red-shift with respect to the narrow lines. A slight asymmetry in the line profile is reported. The asymmetry is clearer in the profile, which peaks at longer wavelengths ( v 2 200 ). shen10a reported a blue-shift of 600 for and 2 300 for .

gmw_00001142.name: J0221+0101
gmw_00001142.overview: J0221+0101 is a quasar located at a redshift of 0.354 [arXiv:1106.1180]. Both the broad and narrow components of its emission lines exhibit a velocity shift of 1,300 km/s, displaying boxy profiles with no obvious asymmetry [arXiv:1106.1180].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J0221+0101 as others, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar quasar.

quotes.quote: Classified: others. J0221+0101 Both and broad components of this z_ NL =0.354 quasar show a 1 300 shift with respect to the narrow lines. Line profiles are rather boxy with no obvious asymmetry. shen10a report no significant shift for , and an exceedingly pronounced shift for ( 3 100 ).

gmw_00001143.name: J0918+3156
gmw_00001143.overview: J0918+3156 is a peculiar quasar that exhibits shifted emission-line structures [arXiv:1106.1180]. Its broad emission lines are redshifted relative to its narrow lines, but the differing shift velocities suggest this is not caused by a binary black hole or a recoil event [arXiv:1106.1180].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J0918+3156 as others, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar quasar.

quotes.quote: Classified: others. J0918+3156 The peculiar properties of this object were first reported by bonning07 . The and broad lines are red-shifted with respect to the NLs. However, the shift is 3 000 for and only 1050 for see bonning07,shen10a , suggesting that the shift is due neither to a BHB nor a recoil.

gmw_00001144.name: J0919+1108
gmw_00001144.overview: The quasar J0919+1108 is classified as a double-peaked emitter with broad emission lines that are slightly redshifted [arXiv:1106.1180]. Its complex morphology likely results from a merger or a strong gravitational interaction with a nearby galaxy [arXiv:1106.1180].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J0919+1108 as double peaked emitter, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar quasar.

quotes.quote: Classified: double peaked emitter. J0919+1108 The broad emission lines of this source are slightly red-shifted ( 700-1 000 , depending on the subtraction of the lines). The SDSS image of the quasar reveals a complex morphology, probably resulting by a strong gravitational interaction or a merger with a nearby galaxy. shen10a reported a blue-shift for the broad component of and a red-shift for , which is not confirmed in our analysis.

gmw_00001145.name: J0921+3835
gmw_00001145.overview: The active galactic nucleus J0921+3835 is classified as a peculiar quasar candidate [arXiv:1106.1180]. It exhibits asymmetric Balmer lines with a blue-shifted peak and an unidentified emission line shifted from the rest frame [arXiv:1106.1180].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J0921+3835 as others, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar quasar.

quotes.quote: Classified: others. J0921+3835 This object shows clearly asymmetric Balmer lines, with a peak at 1 200 blue-wards consistent with the values reported by shen10a . The / flux ratio is 3.3 . An unidentified line is observed at 5812 , corresponding to a 2 300 shift with respect to the rest frame.

gmw_00001146.name: J0931+3204
gmw_00001146.overview: The active galaxy J0931+3204 is classified as a supermassive black hole binary candidate [arXiv:1106.1180]. Its quasar spectrum exhibits asymmetric narrow emission lines and a distinct blue-wing bump [arXiv:1106.1180].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J0931+3204 as black hole binary candidate, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar quasar.

quotes.quote: Classified: black hole binary candidate. J0931+3204 The spectrum of this quasar shows relatively broad and asymmetric narrow lines, which makes the determination of z_ NL uncertain. The blue wing of shows a bump at 3 500 with respect to the peak of the line. This feature is not observed in .

gmw_00001147.name: J0932+0318
gmw_00001147.overview: J0932+0318 is a quasar that exhibits a velocity shift of approximately 4,000 km/s in the peaks of its emission lines [arXiv:1106.1180]. It has been studied as a potential massive black-hole binary candidate or a double-peaked emitter with an asymmetric broad-line region [arXiv:1106.1180].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J0932+0318 as others, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar quasar.

quotes.quote: Classified: others. J0932+0318 This source shows a shift of 4 000 in the peak of the and the lines. shen10a reported two different velocity offsets for ( 3 900 ) and ( 1 100 ) respectively, but such a difference is not confirmed by our analysis. The line appears slightly asymmetric, though the line profile is poorly determined. This object has been studied in detail by barrows11 , who suggested it to be a DPE from an asymmetric BL region, or a BHB.

gmw_00001148.name: J0936+5331
gmw_00001148.overview: J0936+5331 is a quasar [arXiv:1106.1180, arXiv:2311.08915] that exhibits Fe II emission [arXiv:0807.1787] and is classified as a double-peaked emitter [arXiv:1106.1180]. The object displays broad-line profile variability [arXiv:1312.6694, arXiv:2311.08915], including a red feature that disappeared over a ten-year timescale [arXiv:2311.08915].

sources.summary: The paper fits SDSS J093653.84+533126.8 with a retrograde warped-disc model, notes Fe II emission, and reports a generally good qualitative line-profile match except in the wings.

quotes.quote: The FeII emission lines are apparent in J0936+5331.

A retrograde disc is needed to match the line profiles with the observations for SDSS J094321.97+042412.0, J093653.84+533126.8. Fig. f454 shows the comparison between the line profiles and the observed profile for SDSS J094321.97+042412.0 with i=17^ , n_1 = 2.1 , n_3=24^ , _0 = 202^ . And the parameters in Fig. f473 for SDSS J093653.84+533126.8 are: i=8^ , n_1=2.1 , n_3=6^ , _0 = 117^ . The disc zone is still taken from R_ in =250r_ g to R_ out = 1000 r_ g and n_2=3.0 . We see that the overall match except that in the two wings between the line profiles and the observations is good qualitatively.

sources.summary: The paper classifies J0936+5331 as double peaked emitter or black hole binary candidate, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar quasar.

quotes.quote: Classified: double peaked emitter or black hole binary candidate. J0936+5331 The Balmer lines of this z_ NL =0.228 quasar show a strongly asymmetric profile, with a bright blue peak at 2 100 2250 in the analysis by shen10a with respect to the narrow lines. The red wing of is 3.1 brighter than ; the blue wing is 4.0 brighter. No peak or bump is observed in the blue wing at 2 100 .

sources.summary: The paper assigns this object to broad-line variability, noting dramatic profile changes, apparent blueshifts of red peaks over 8.3 yr, and similar changes reported by two earlier studies.

quotes.quote: J093653.84 + 533126.8 . This object has among the most dramatic profile changes in our followup sample. The redshifted peaks of broad \ and \ appear to have blueshifted by >2000 km s ^ 1 over 8.3 yr, while no obvious shifts were seen in the bases of the broad lines. eracleous11 reported a profile change in the \ region similar to our findings based on a spectrum taken 6.7 yr after the original SDSS observation; no second-epoch spectrum was available for the \ region. Decarli2013 also reported a similar profile change in the \ and \ regions based on a spectrum taken 7.5 yr after the original SDSS observation.

sources.summary: The paper cites SDSS J0936+5331 as a quasar with unmistakable broad H-line profile changes, including a red feature disappearing over about ten years.

quotes.quote: However, the authors flagged the case of SDSS J0936+5331 (illustrated in Fig. fig4 ) which shows a strong additional red feature which disappeared on a timescale of 10 years. This short timescale suggests that some other process unrelated to the dynamic timescale may be involved, and provides a useful comparison with the PG quasars with a mean redshift z = 0.20 . The possibility that the rapid changes in quasar emission line profiles are associated with the presence of a binary supermassive black hole (BBH) is addressed by , who argue that most BBHs will not exhibit double-peaked broad lines due to limitations in parameter space. By implication, this rules out profile changes similar to those illustrated in Fig. fig4 for SDSS J0936+5331.
In the sample of 50 candidates selected by , only the quasar SDSS J0936+5331 showed unmistakeable evidence for changes in the H line profile, with a marginal additional candidate SDSS J1345+1144.

gmw_00001149.name: J0942+0900
gmw_00001149.overview: J0942+0900 is a double-peaked quasar [arXiv:1203.6071] characterized by an asymmetric emission-line profile [arXiv:1106.1180]. Its broad Balmer lines are exceptionally wide [arXiv:1106.1180, arXiv:1203.6071], suggesting that the emission region is located close to the central black hole [arXiv:1203.6071].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J0942+0900 as asymmetric line profile, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar quasar.

quotes.quote: Classified: asymmetric line profile. J0942+0900 This object has been labeled as a galaxy by the SDSS automatic pipeline, presumably because the broad lines are too flat and extended. The Balmer lines are 38 000 broad (the broadest ever reported) and asymmetric (the blue peak is brighter and peaks around 11 500 , while the red side is fainter, flatter and extends red-wards of +20 000 ). The flux ratio between and is 2.6, and the lines have similar profile.

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSSJ094215.12 + 090015.8 as a double-peaked quasar with an exceptionally broad line, suggesting emission close to the black hole.

quotes.quote: SDSSJ094215.12 + 090015.8 . This is quasar with double-peaked . The FWHM of the broad component is extremely wide and reaches 40,600 , which is the broadest known wan05 . The broad \ suggests that the emission region is close to the black hole 80 - 100 r_ g str06 .

gmw_00001150.name: J0946+0139
gmw_00001150.overview: J0946+0139 is a Population A quasar at a redshift of 0.2203 [arXiv:1106.1180, arXiv:1203.5992]. It is classified as an extreme double-peaked emitter that exhibits a large H-beta peak redshift and a large Fe II redshift [arXiv:1106.1180, arXiv:1203.5992].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J0946+0139 as extreme double peaked emitter, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar quasar.

quotes.quote: Classified: extreme double peaked emitter. J0946+0139 The Balmer lines of this z_ NL =0.2203 quasar peak at 1550 blue-wards of the NL system see also shen10a . boroson10 reported an ``anomalous profile'' for this source. The broad lines show no obvious second peak, but the blue wings are slightly more extended than the red ones. Also, the flux ratio between and is larger in the blue wing.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J0946+0139 as the lone Population A source in its sample with both a large H peak redshift and a large FeII redshift.

quotes.quote: In only one pop. A source in our sample SDSS J0946+0139 do we find a large H peak (90 intensity level) redshift of 1830 . This source is similar to OQ208 ( Marzianietal93 and discussed in H08) which shows > 2000 . SDSS J0946 is the only pop. A source with a large FeII redshift in our Z10 sample (1/260).

gmw_00001151.name: J1000+2233
gmw_00001151.overview: The active galactic nucleus J1000+2233, also known as 4C+22.25, is classified as having an asymmetric emission line profile [arXiv:1106.1180]. It is considered a candidate binary black hole system [arXiv:1010.4303] with faint, extremely blue-shifted Balmer lines [arXiv:1106.1180].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 4C+22.25 as an ideal binary-black-hole candidate for testing its model, with a single active secondary, tidally perturbed BLR, and broad, faint BELs.

quotes.quote: As an example we notice that the binary candidate 4C+22.25, described in the recent work of Decarli et al. (2010), can represent an ideal case to test and improve the predictions of our study. The binary model consistent with the observations of this source requires the presence of a single active BH, which is supposed to be the secondary, a total mass of 10^9 and an orbital period of the order of 30 yr. According to our findings, these orbital parameters imply that the BLR of the active BH is tidally perturbed with respect to the case of an isolated AGN. As already reported in the letter of Decarli et al. (2010), one of the peculiarities of the optical spectrum of 4C+22.25 is that the BELs are very broad and faint. This can be interpreted as a further observational evidence of the truncation of the secondary BLR that is present together with the reduced flux ratios.

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1000+2233 as asymmetric line profile, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar quasar.

quotes.quote: Classified: asymmetric line profile. J1000+2233 This source was serendipitously discovered by our group decarli_4c2225 out of the SDSS database, and share some of the properties of J0942+0900. The Balmer lines appear faint and extremely blue-shifted ( 8 700 ). Also the line shows a blue-shift, but its magnitude is poorly constrained since the line is only partially covered by the SDSS spectrum.

gmw_00001152.name: J1010+3725
gmw_00001152.overview: J1010+3725 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2009.10763] classified as an extreme double-peaked emitter and a supermassive black hole binary candidate [arXiv:1106.1180]. The object exhibits a complex emission-line profile with two peaks separated by a velocity difference of 1400 km/s [arXiv:1106.1180], and it displays broad H-alpha emission [arXiv:2009.10763].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1010+3725 as a detected broad-line DOG source, uses H rather than Mg II for its black-hole mass estimate, and models broad H emission for it.

quotes.quote: Pdetect , we detect one source (J1028+5011) in all three bands and five sources only in the hard and full bands (J1010+3725, J1235+4827, J1248+4242, J1324+4501, and J1525+1234).
Among the M_ BH measurements for our 12 sources, 11 of them are based on Mg ii , and one (J1010+3725; whose Mg ii region is not covered by SDSS) is based on H .

For J1010+3725 and J1028+5011, we adopt the model in Shen11 and use one narrow Gaussian line plus three broad Gaussian lines to fit their H lines. The FWHM values are displayed in Table MBHTable . We compare our FWHM values with those in Shen11 and Paris17 and present the best-fit Mg ii and H [ O iii ] spectra in Appendix append: optspec . Again, we note that only two of our sources (J1010+3725 and J1028+5011) have broad H lines (i.e. FWHM>1000 km s^ 1 ), while all the sources show broad Mg ii lines (except J1010+3725 whose spectrum does not cover the Mg ii line).

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1010+3725 as extreme double peaked emitter or black hole binary candidate, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar

quotes.quote: Classified: extreme double peaked emitter or black hole binary candidate. J1010+3725 This object shows a complex profile (both for the 4959 and the 5 008 emission lines). Two peaks are observed, with a velocity difference of 1 400 . The blue peaks are fainter.

gmw_00001153.name: J1012+2613
gmw_00001153.overview: J1012+2613 is a quasar located at a redshift of 0.3783 [arXiv:1106.1180]. Its broad Balmer lines peak approximately 6,000 kilometers per second blueward of its narrow lines [arXiv:1106.1180].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1012+2613 as others, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar quasar.

quotes.quote: Classified: others. J1012+2613 The Balmer lines of this z_ NL =0.3783 quasar show similar properties to those of J1000+2233: The BLs peak 6 000 blue-wards of the NLs. The red wing of and the blue wing of are not covered.

gmw_00001154.name: J1027+6050
gmw_00001154.overview: J1027+6050 is classified as an extreme double-peaked emitter [arXiv:1106.1180]. The galaxy is also considered a candidate for hosting a supermassive black hole binary [arXiv:1106.1180].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1027+6050 as extreme double peaked emitter or black hole binary candidate, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar

quotes.quote: Classified: extreme double peaked emitter or black hole binary candidate. J1027+6050 This is the only EDPE already known strateva03 . The blue peaks of and are 7 000 blue-shifted with respect to NLs. The shift is missed in the analysis by shen10a , probably because of the faintness of the lines.

gmw_00001155.name: J1050+3456
gmw_00001155.overview: The active galaxy J1050+3456 is a quasar located at a redshift of approximately 0.272 [arXiv:0907.3470]. It is classified as an extreme double-peaked emitter [arXiv:0907.3470, arXiv:1106.1180] with broad emission lines blueshifted by about 3,500 km/s relative to its host galaxy [arXiv:0907.3470, arXiv:1106.1180].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J105041.35+345631.3 as a quasar with broad lines blueshifted from the host, weighing recoil, binary, superposition, and double-peaked-emitter interpretations.

quotes.quote: The quasar SDSS J105041.35+345631.3 ( z = 0.272 ) has broad emission lines blueshifted by 3500 relative to the narrow lines and the host galaxy. Such an object may be a candidate for a recoiling supermassive black hole, a binary black hole, a superposition of two objects, or an unusual geometry for the broad emission-line region. The absence of narrow lines at the broad line redshift argues against superposition. New Keck spectra of 1050 \ place tight constraints on the binary model.

The combination of large velocity shift and symmetrical \ profile, as well as aspects of the narrow line spectrum, make 1050 \ an interesting candidate for black hole recoil. Other aspects of the spectrum, however, suggest that the object is most likely an extreme case of a ``double-peaked emitter.''

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1050 as an unusual SDSS quasar where SBHB, recoiling-black-hole, and double-peaked-emitter interpretations remain viable pending further observations.

quotes.quote: Most recently, shields09 reported the discovery of another unusual SDSS quasar, J1050, for which an SBHB is a viable explanation, along with a recoiling SBH, and a double-peaked emitter. As with the previous cases, further observations are needed in order to discriminate among proposed hypotheses.

J0927, J1536, and J1050 do not make up a uniform class of objects because their emission-line spectra differ from each other. A common property shared by all three objects is that their emission line systems exhibit shifts in the range 2000-4000 km s^ 1 . If such velocity shifts are interpreted in the context of a binary model, and if the emission-line systems are associated with one or both of the black holes, the expectation is that the orbital motion of the binary should give rise to a measurable velocity change of the emission lines on a time-scale of several years. For all three objects this basic test was carried out by taking spectra of the object at a few different epochs and measuring the change in the position of the emission line peaks.

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1050+3456 as extreme double peaked emitter, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar quasar.

quotes.quote: Classified: extreme double peaked emitter. J1050+3456 This source was discovered by shields09 out of the SDSS database. The broad component of Balmer lines is clearly shifted 3 400 blue-wards; a similar value was found by shen10a . No NL is observed at the redshift of the BLs.

gmw_00001156.name: J1105+0414
gmw_00001156.overview: J1105+0414 is a quasar that has been classified as a candidate supermassive black hole binary [arXiv:1106.1180]. The object exhibits a broad emission-line peak that is shifted 6,000 km/s blue-ward of its narrow component [arXiv:1106.1180].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1105+0414 as black hole binary candidate, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar quasar.

quotes.quote: Classified: black hole binary candidate. J1105+0414 This quasar was found in the galaxy sample. The broad line peaks 6 000 blue-wards of the narrow component. Due to its faintness, the line profile is poorly constrained.

gmw_00001157.name: J1117+6741
gmw_00001157.overview: J1117+6741 is an active galactic nucleus classified as an extreme double-peaked emitter [arXiv:1106.1180]. The source is intrinsically faint, and its broad emission-line component appears slightly redshifted relative to its narrow lines [arXiv:1106.1180].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1117+6741 as extreme double peaked emitter, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar quasar.

quotes.quote: Classified: extreme double peaked emitter. J1117+6741 The broad component of this source appears slightly redshifted with respect to the NLs. The properties of are difficult to characterize, due to its intrinsic faintness.

gmw_00001158.name: J1154+0134
gmw_00001158.overview: J1154+0134 is a quasar located at a redshift of 0.469 [arXiv:1106.1180]. It exhibits identical emission-line profiles for its hydrogen-beta and oxygen-III lines, which are shifted 3,500 kilometers per second blue-ward of their expected wavelengths and may support the binary black hole hypothesis [arXiv:1106.1180].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1154+0134 as others, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar quasar.

quotes.quote: Classified: others. J1154+0134 The and lines of this z_ NL =0.469 quasar have identical profiles, with a peak 3 500 blue-wards of the expected wavelengths and a rather broad red wing. The line profiles resemble the one of other sources with asymmetric lines (e.g., J0012-1022), but the magnitude of the shift and the similarity between and support the BHB hypothesis. The low S/N of the spectrum of the SDSS spectrum hinder any conclusion on the nature of this source.

gmw_00001159.name: J1207+0604
gmw_00001159.overview: J1207+0604 is a quasar characterized by an asymmetric emission line profile [arXiv:1106.1180]. It is considered a candidate for hosting a supermassive black hole binary system [arXiv:1106.1180].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1207+0604 as asymmetric line profile or black hole binary candidate, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar quasar

quotes.quote: Classified: asymmetric line profile or black hole binary candidate. J1207+0604 The broad component of the Balmer lines of this quasar is rather symmetric but shifted 2 500 blue-wards with respect to NLs. The flux ratio between and is 4 , roughly constant with respect to the line-of-sight velocity. This source was not included in the compilation by shen10a .

gmw_00001160.name: J1211+4647
gmw_00001160.overview: J1211+4647 is a quasar characterized by a shifted emission-line structure [arXiv:1106.1180]. The bulk of its Balmer line broad components is shifted 1,700 km/s blue-wards of the narrow-line system, and the line profile shows a bump in the red wing that may indicate a double-peaked emitter nature [arXiv:1106.1180].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1211+4647 as others, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar quasar.

quotes.quote: Classified: others. J1211+4647 The bulk of the Balmer line broad components of this source is shifted 1 700 blue-wards of the NL system consistent values are reported in shen10a . The / flux ratio is 4 , constant along the velocity profile. The line shows a bump at 6 000 in the red wing, possibly revealing the DPE-like nature of this source.

gmw_00001161.name: J1215+4146
gmw_00001161.overview: J1215+4146 is a quasar that exhibits a peculiar Balmer line profile [arXiv:1106.1180]. The bulk of this emission originates from a bright bump in the red wing, while the blue side of the line may feature a faint wing [arXiv:1106.1180].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1215+4146 as others, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar quasar.

quotes.quote: Classified: others. J1215+4146 This quasar shows a peculiar Balmer line profile. The bulk of emission arises from a bright bump in the red wing. The blue side of the line may also present a faint wing, the actual presence of which depends on the continuum modeling.

gmw_00001162.name: J1216+4159
gmw_00001162.overview: J1216+4159 is a quasar that exhibits a broad emission line profile with a clear blue-shift of approximately 2300 km/s [arXiv:1106.1180]. The broad component of this emission line is barely detected, showing a flux seven times fainter than the narrow component [arXiv:1106.1180].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1216+4159 as others, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar quasar.

quotes.quote: Classified: others. J1216+4159 The broad emission of this quasar is clearly blue-shifted ( 2 300 ). The line profile shows no relevant asymmetry. The broad component is barely detected, its flux being 7 times fainter than .

gmw_00001163.name: J1328-0129
gmw_00001163.overview: J1328-0129 is a quasar located at a redshift of 0.1514 [arXiv:1106.1180]. The broad component of its Balmer lines is blue-shifted relative to the narrow lines, displaying a boxy profile with no asymmetry [arXiv:1106.1180].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1328-0129 as others, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar quasar.

quotes.quote: Classified: others. J1328-0129 The broad component of the Balmer lines in this z_ NL =0.1514 quasar are blue-shifted ( 3 100 ) with respect to NLs. The line profile is boxy, with no significant asymmetry. The / flux ratio is 5 , constant over the velocity range.

gmw_00001164.name: J1414+1658
gmw_00001164.overview: J1414+1658 is a quasar characterized by a shifted emission-line structure [arXiv:1106.1180]. The bulk of its broad emission lines is redshifted by approximately 1200 km/s relative to its narrow lines, with a brighter red wing [arXiv:1106.1180].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1414+1658 as others, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar quasar.

quotes.quote: Classified: others. J1414+1658 The bulk of the BLs of this quasars is redshifted ( 1 200 ) with respect to NLs. The red wing is brighter. The / flux ratio is 4 in the blue wing and around 3 in the red wing.

gmw_00001165.name: J1440+3319
gmw_00001165.overview: J1440+3319 is a quasar characterized by a shifted emission-line structure [arXiv:1106.1180]. Its emission line peaks 3,700 km/s blueward of the narrow lines and displays an extended red wing [arXiv:1106.1180].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1440+3319 as others, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar quasar.

quotes.quote: Classified: others. J1440+3319 The line of this source peaks at 3 700 blue-wards of the NLs, and shows an extended red wing. The line profile is similar. The properties of this quasar are half the way between the objects with asymmetric line profiles (e.g., J1154+0134) and the typical DPEs, though this source has not been included in any previous compilation of DPEs.

gmw_00001166.name: J1539+3333
gmw_00001166.overview: The active galaxy J1539+3333 is classified as a double-peaked emitter and a supermassive black hole binary candidate [arXiv:1106.1180]. Its spectrum displays three sets of emission lines at two distinct redshifts [arXiv:1106.1180].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1539+3333 as double peaked emitter or black hole binary candidate, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar quasar.

quotes.quote: Classified: double peaked emitter or black hole binary candidate. J1539+3333 From the spectroscopic point of view, the properties of this source are similar to those of J0927+2943. The spectrum presents three sets of lines at two different redshifts: Broad balmer lines (driving the redshift estimate by the SDSS pipeline) and faint narrow lines are detected at z_1=0.1993 . Another set of (brighter) narrow lines is observed at z_2=0.2263 .

gmw_00001167.name: J1652+3123
gmw_00001167.overview: J1652+3123 is a quasar located at a redshift of 0.5929 [arXiv:1106.1180]. It is classified as a candidate massive black hole binary or another type of peculiar active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1106.1180].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1652+3123 as black hole binary candidate or others, describing shifted or unusual emission-line structure used to evaluate whether it is a massive black-hole binary candidate or another peculiar quasar.

quotes.quote: Classified: black hole binary candidate or others . J1652+3123 The and broad lines of this z_ NL =0.5929 quasar show a small blue-shift ( 500 ) with respect to NLs. The line profiles are similar and do not show any significant asymmetry. Our shift estimates are consistent with those reported by shen10a .

gmw_00001168.name: J0347+0105
gmw_00001168.overview: J0347+0105 is a radio-quiet narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1107.5155, arXiv:1801.03519] that hosts a water maser [arXiv:1107.5155, arXiv:2109.07841]. The galaxy features a compact, clumpy toroidal dust structure [arXiv:1107.5155] and exhibits diffuse emission extending south and east of its core [arXiv:1801.03519].

sources.summary: The paper reports IRAS 03450+0055 as a new maser-detected NLS1 galaxy whose nuclear radio continuum and obscured toroidal structure leave the maser origin unresolved.

quotes.quote: We discovered maser emission in two NLS1 galaxies: IGR J16385-2057, and IRAS 03450+0055.
The spectra of Mrk 766 and IRAS 03450 contain instead a single, relatively broad feature blue-shifted w..t. the systemic velocity.

IRAS 0345 This galaxy has not been well studied. The nuclear radio continuum has been imaged by thean00 at 8.4 GHz with the VLA A-array, showing a single, slightly resolved source with an integrated flux of 6.8 mJy (Thean et al. 2000; their Fig. 1). The galaxy was also part of the sample studied by thompson09 using Spitzer mid-infrared (MIR) spectroscopic data and their conclusion was that its observational characteristics are consistent with a nucleus obscured by a compact and clumpy toroidal structure of dust. Owing to the lack of radio interferometric measurements, however, the association of the maser emission with the AGN or the nuclear starburst present in this galaxy cannot yet be determined.

sources.summary: The source is a radio-quiet NLS1 with a water maser, weak diffuse emission south and east of the core, elevated map noise, and possible variability.

quotes.quote: J0347+0105 IRAS 03450+0055 is a radio-quiet NLS1 associated with a water maser, often observed in NLS1s Tarchi11 . The source was already studied at 8.4 GHz with the VLA-A by Thean00 , who found a flux of 6.8 mJy and an unresolved core, while it was not detected neither through VLBI observations at 22 GHz nor at 37 GHz. In our map (Fig. fig:J0347p0105 ) the source seems to show some diffuse emission, particularly extended 2 ^ (1.2 kpc) both South (PA 188 ^ ) and East (PA 81 ^ ) of the core. The map noise is higher with respect to the rest of the sample, possibly because of the lack of four antennas during the observations. The core size deconvolved from the beam is 204 115 pc.
It is worth noting that the broad- and in-band spectral indexes are not in agreement, possibly indicating some variability in this object.

sources.summary: The paper discusses J0347+0105 as an NLS1 source, summarizing its radio and spectral-index properties, flux density, luminosity, and whether extended emission is linked to AGN activity or the host galaxy.

quotes.quote: J0347+0105 J0347+0105 is a radio-quiet NLS1 at z = 0.031, previously studied with the JVLA in A configuration at 8.4 GHz by 2000thean1 . They found only a marginally resolved core with a flux density of 6.8 mJy. It was not detected at 22 GHz with VLBI 2016doi1 , but the sensitivity of those observations was poor with a detection limit around 7 mJy. J0347+0105 has also been found to exhibit water maser emission 2011tarchi1 , though its origin remains unclear.

gmw_00001169.name: J0629-0545
gmw_00001169.overview: J0629-0545 is a radio-quiet narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:2109.07841] classified as an ultra-luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:2109.07841]. It exhibits starburst-level star formation [arXiv:2109.07841] and contains two distinct radio components separated by 2.4 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1801.03519].

sources.summary: The paper describes this steep-spectrum radio-loud NLS1 as a two-component source whose components differ in flux and spectral index, leaving the core identification uncertain.

quotes.quote: J0629 0545 IRAS 06269-0543 is classified as a steep-spectrum radio-loud NLS1, and was observed with the VLA-A at 8.4 GHz by Moran00 . This source was detected in the NVSS, but it is not present in the FIRST survey. The source is not centered on the NVSS coordinates, but it is found within the error bars. The map (Fig. fig:J0629m0545 ), indeed, shows two evident components, separated by 2.4 kpc, in perfect agreement with Moran00 results.

The south-western component is the brightest and the closest to the NVSS coordinates, with a peak flux of 12.47 mJy and a spectral index of 0.14 0.01. The north-eastern component (PA 73 ^ with respect to the other component) has instead a flux of 0.42 mJy, and an inverted spectral index (-0.03 0.04).
It is not clear which one of these two components is the source core.

sources.summary: The paper discusses J0629-0545 as an NLS1 source, summarizing its radio emission and star-formation diagnostics, flux density, luminosity, and whether extended emission is linked to AGN activity or the host galaxy.

quotes.quote: J0629-0545 J0629-0545 (IRAS 06269-0543) is a radio-quiet NLS1 at z = 0.117 that has been identified as an ultra-luminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG, 2002zheng1 ). Its integrated flux density is 14.58 mJy, with the flux density of the tapered maps approximately similar. It exhibits starburst level star formation at a rate of 183 M_ yr ^ 1 derived from the CO(1-0) luminosity 2019tan1 . This is evident also in our star formation diagnostics, as the W3-W4 colour is 2.88, q22 = 1.23, S_ W3 is 153.7 mJy higher than S_ 1.4GHz, JVLA (45.3 mJy).

gmw_00001170.name: J0632+6340
gmw_00001170.overview: J0632+6340 is a nearby, radio-quiet narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy hosted by a spiral galaxy [arXiv:1801.03519, arXiv:2109.07841]. It exhibits a two-sided extended radio morphology that spans 1 kiloparsec end-to-end and is confined within the host galaxy's bulge [arXiv:2109.07841].

sources.summary: The source is a radio-quiet NLS1 previously unresolved at small scale, while the JVLA map reveals larger-scale north-south diffuse emission and steep spectra.

quotes.quote: J0632+6340 UGC 3478 is a radio-quiet NLS1. It was previously studied by Kinney00 and Schmitt01 at 8.4 GHz, finding an unresolved radio structure with a scale of 25 pc and a flux of 1.4 mJy. Our map (Fig. fig:J0632p6340 ) reveal an extended structure on larger scales, with emission diffuse for 0.6 kpc both north and south of the unresolved core, roughly aligned along PA 30 ^ . The core has a deconvolved size of 66 58 pc. Both the latter and the integrated emission have a steep spectrum, and the broad-band index is even steeper.

sources.summary: The paper discusses J0632+6340 as an NLS1 source, summarizing its radio and spectral-index properties, flux density, luminosity, and whether extended emission is linked to AGN activity or the host galaxy.

quotes.quote: J0632+6340 J0632+6340 (UGC 3478) is a radio-quiet, nearby ( z = 0.013) NLS1 with a modest integrated flux density of 2.83 mJy, and a low luminosity of log L_ , int = 37.76 erg s ^ 1 . The peak flux density is only 1.69 mJy beam ^ 1 , so a considerable fraction of the radio emission comes from the regions of extended emission. The radio map (Fig fig:J0632spind ) shows a two-sided extended morphology, approximately at a PA of 40 . The extent of the whole emitting region is 1 kpc end-to-end, it is well confined within the bulge of its spiral host galaxy (Figs. fig:J0632-host and fig:J0632-host-zoom ), and seems to be aligned with its major axis.

gmw_00001171.name: J0706+3901
gmw_00001171.overview: J0706+3901 is a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.086 [arXiv:1801.03519, arXiv:2109.07841]. It features a compact, unresolved core with diffuse emission [arXiv:1801.03519] and a steep radio spectrum that suggests it is a low-luminosity compact steep-spectrum candidate [arXiv:1801.03519].

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies this object as steep-spectrum radio-loud, finds an unresolved core with diffuse emission, and identifies it as a low-luminosity CSS candidate.

quotes.quote: J0706+3901 FBQS J0706+3901 is a bona-fide F-NLS1s by Foschini15 . Our measurements instead indicate that this object is a steep-spectrum radio-loud NLS1, since all of its spectral indexes are larger than 0.5. The map (Fig. fig:J0706p3901 ) shows an unresolved core, with an approximate size of 800 400 pc, and an intermediate morphology, with a significant diffuse emission around the core. Since it is compact and it has a steep radio spectrum, but a relatively low luminosity, it is classifiable as a low-luminosity CSS.

sources.summary: The paper discusses J0706+3901 as an NLS1 source, summarizing its radio emission and star-formation diagnostics, flux density, luminosity, and whether extended emission is linked to AGN activity or the host galaxy.

quotes.quote: J0706+3901 J0706+3901 (FBQS J0706+3901) is a radio-quiet NLS1 at z = 0.086 with a modest S_ int of 2.14 mJy. The flux densities of the tapered maps are approximately the same, and the luminosity is moderate with log L_ , int = 39.33 erg s ^ 1 . It does not show any resolved structure in any of the maps (Figures fig:J0706spind , fig:J0706-90k , and fig:J0706-60k ). Its total and core spectral indices are around -0.8, consistent with either optically thin synchrotron emission or star formation.

gmw_00001172.name: J0713+3820
gmw_00001172.overview: J0713+3820 is a radio-quiet narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.123 [arXiv:2109.07841]. It exhibits moderate radio luminosity, with a considerable portion of its radio emission originating from extended regions [arXiv:2109.07841].

sources.summary: The paper discusses J0713+3820 as an NLS1 source, summarizing its radio and spectral-index properties, flux density, luminosity, and whether extended emission is linked to AGN activity or the host galaxy.

quotes.quote: J0713+3820 J0713+3820 (FBQS J0713+3820) is a radio-quiet NLS1 at z = 0.123 with a peak flux density of 2.32 mJy, integrated flux density of 3.39 mJy, and 60k tapered map flux density of 3.80 mJy, thus a considerable amount of the emission originates from extended regions (Fig. fig:J0713 ). The radio luminosity is moderate with log L_ , int = 39.82 erg s ^ 1 . The map (Fig. fig:J0713spind ) shows a steep spectral index around 1.0 throughout the emitting region. This source has been detected in WENSS (25 mJy) and FIRST (10.78 mJy), yielding spectral indices of -0.57 between 325 MHz and 1.4 GHz, and -0.88 between 1.4 and 5.2 GHz.

gmw_00001173.name: J0804+3853
gmw_00001173.overview: J0804+3853 is a radio-quiet narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.212 [arXiv:2109.07841]. It features a steep-spectrum radio emission [arXiv:1801.03519, arXiv:2109.07841] that is dominated by a strong starburst [arXiv:1801.03519] and extends asymmetric contours toward the east [arXiv:1801.03519, arXiv:2109.07841].

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies this source as steep-spectrum, finds eastward contour extension and a kiloparsec-scale core, and argues its radio emission is starburst dominated.
